Title: Caramelized Black Pepper Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup dark brown sugar
2. About 1/4 cup nam pla (fish sauce)
3. 1/4 cup water
4. 3 tablespoons rice vinegar
5. 1 teaspoon minced garlic
6. 1 teaspoon finely grated fresh ginger
7. 1 teaspoon coarsely ground pepper
8. 2 fresh Thai chiles, halved, or dried red chiles
9. 1 tablespoon canola oil
10. 1 shallot, thinly sliced
11. 1 pound skinless, boneless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
12. 4 cilantro sprigs
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the sugar, fish sauce, water, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger, pepper and chiles.
2. Heat the oil in a large deep skillet. Add the shallot and cook over moderate heat until softened, about 4 minutes.
3. Add the fish sauce mixture and the chicken and simmer over high heat until the chicken is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
4. Transfer to a serving bowl, garnish with the cilantro and serve.
Time:
Prep: 25 mins
Total: 35 mins
Notes: Because fish sauces vary in their saltiness, Phan advises adding the fish sauce to taste.
Author: Charles Phan

Title: Chocolate Babka
Ingredients:
1. 1 1/2 cups warm milk, 110 degrees
2. 2 (1/4 ounce each) packages active dry yeast
3. 1 3/4 cups plus a pinch of sugar
4. 3 whole large eggs, room temperature
5. 2 large egg yolks, room temperature
6. 6 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for work surface
7. 1 teaspoon salt
8. 1 3/4 cups (3 1/2 sticks) unsalted butter, cut into 1-inch pieces, room temperature, plus more for bowl and loaf pans
9. 2 1/4 pounds semisweet chocolate, very finely chopped
10. 2 1/2 tablespoons ground cinnamon
11. 1 tablespoon heavy cream
12. Streusel topping (below)
Instructions:
1. Pour warm milk into a small bowl. Sprinkle yeast and pinch of sugar over milk; let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. In a bowl, whisk together 3/4 cup sugar, 2 eggs, and egg yolks. Add egg mixture to yeast mixture, and whisk to combine.
3. In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, combine flour and salt. Add egg mixture, and beat on low speed.
Yield: Makes 3 loaves (but I made two full-sized and three miniature ones)
Notes: When shaping the babka, twist dough evenly throughout the length of the roll a full 5 to 6 turns. The babka can be prepared up to step 8 and frozen for up to a month before baking. When ready to bake, remove from freezer; let stand at room temperature for about 5 hours, and bake.
Author: deb

Title: Feta Salsa
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 pound feta (cow’s milk)
2. 2/3 cup sundried tomatoes in oil
3. 1/2 cup pitted Kalamata olives
4. 2 tablespoons fresh dill
5. 3 tablespoons flat-leaf parsley
6. 2 scallions
7. 1/4 cup olive oil
Instructions:
1. Crumble the feta into a bowl.
2. Chop the tomatoes and olives, dill and parsley and thinly slice the scallions.
3. Gently mix the ingredients together and drizzle with a few tablespoons of olive oil, or to taste.
Notes: Consider this recipe as more of a guideline. It is flexible, allowing for different herbs or additional ingredients like capers.
Author: Deb

Title: Spiced Beef Pho with Sesame Chile Oil
Notes: The page content is unavailable, so details about the recipe could not be extracted.

Title: Chicken Empanadas with Chorizo
Ingredients:
1. 4 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
2. 1 tablespoon salt
3. 2 teaspoons baking powder
4. 1 cup cold unsalted butter, cut into pieces
5. 1 large egg
6. 3/4 cup ice water
7. 1 tablespoon distilled white vinegar
8. 2 pounds chicken thighs and legs
9. Salt and pepper to taste
10. 2 tablespoons olive oil
11. 1/2 pound chorizo, casing removed and crumbled
12. 1 medium onion, finely chopped
13. 3 garlic cloves, minced
14. 1/2 cup dry white wine
15. 1 cup chicken broth
16. 1/2 cup pitted green olives, chopped
17. 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro
Instructions:
1. 1. In a large bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and baking powder.
2. 2. Add butter and blend with fingertips or a pastry cutter until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. 3. In a small bowl, whisk together egg, ice water, and vinegar.
4. 4. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture, stirring until the dough just comes together.
5. 5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently with the heel of your hand until smooth.
6. 6. Divide dough into 2 pieces, wrap in plastic wrap, and chill for at least 1 hour.
7. 7. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
8. 8. In a large heavy skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
9. 9. Add chicken and brown on all sides, about 8 minutes.
10. 10. Transfer chicken to a plate.
11. 11. Add chorizo to the skillet and cook, stirring, until browned, about 3 minutes.
12. 12. Add onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
13. 13. Return chicken to the skillet and add wine, broth, and olives.
14. 14. Bring to a simmer, cover, and braise for 30 minutes.
15. 15. Remove chicken from the skillet and let cool.
16. 16. Remove skin and bones from chicken and shred meat.
17. 17. Return shredded chicken to the skillet and stir in cilantro.
18. 18. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
19. 19. On a lightly floured surface, roll out dough to 1/8-inch thickness.
20. 20. Cut out 6-inch rounds.
21. 21. Place 2 tablespoons of filling in the center of each round.
22. 22. Fold dough over filling to form a half-moon shape.
23. 23. Crimp edges with a fork to seal.
24. 24. Arrange empanadas on a baking sheet and bake until golden brown, about 25 minutes.
Time:
Prep: 1 hour
Cook: 1 hour 30 minutes
Total: 2 hours 30 minutes
Notes: These empanadas can be frozen, either baked or unbaked, for a convenient freezer meal.
Author: Deb

Title: Chicken Tikka Masala
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup plain low-fat yogurt
2. 2 garlic cloves, minced
3. 1 tablespoon finely grated fresh ginger
4. 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cumin
5. 1 1/2 teaspoons ground coriander
6. 1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom
7. 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
8. 1/4 teaspoon ground turmeric
9. Salt and freshly ground pepper
10. 2 1/2 pounds skinless, boneless chicken thighs, fat trimmed
11. 2 tablespoons plus 1 teaspoon vegetable oil
12. 1/4 cup blanched whole almonds
13. 1 large onion, finely chopped
14. 2 garlic cloves, minced
15. 1 teaspoon minced fresh ginger
16. 1 1/2 tablespoons garam masala
17. 1 1/2 teaspoons pure chile powder
18. 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper
19. 1 (35-ounce) can peeled tomatoes, finely chopped, juices reserved
20. 1 pinch of sugar
21. 1 cup heavy cream
Instructions:
1. In a large glass or stainless steel bowl, combine the yogurt, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cardamom, cayenne and turmeric. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Using a sharp knife, make a few shallow slashes in each piece of chicken. Add the chicken to the marinade, turn to coat and refrigerate overnight.
3. Preheat the broiler and position a rack about 8 inches from the heat. Remove the chicken from the marinade; scrape off as much of the marinade as possible. Season the chicken with salt and pepper and spread the pieces on a baking sheet. Broil the chicken, turning once or twice, until just cooked through and browned in spots, about 12 minutes. Transfer to a cutting board and cut it into 2-inch pieces.
4. Meanwhile, in a small skillet, heat 1 teaspoon of the oil. Add the almonds and cook over moderate heat, stirring constantly, until golden, about 5 minutes. Transfer the almonds to a plate and let cool completely. In a food processor, pulse the almonds until finely ground.
5. In a large enameled cast-iron casserole, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil until shimmering. Add the onion, garlic and ginger and cook over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until tender and golden, about 8 minutes. Add the garam masala, chile powder and cayenne and cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes with their juices and the sugar and season with salt and pepper. Cover partially and cook over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is slightly thickened, about 20 minutes. Add the cream and ground almonds and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until thickened, about 10 minutes longer. Stir in the chicken; simmer gently for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, and serve.
Time:
Prep: 45 mins
Cook: 12 mins
Total: 9 hrs 30 mins
Notes: ['The Chicken Tikka Masala can be refrigerated for up to 3 days. Reheat gently before serving.', 'Variation: the marinade and sauce here are also delicious with shrimp, lamb, and vegetables.', 'Serve with steamed basmati rice, rice pilaf, or warm naan.']
Author: Grace Parisi

Title: Arroz Con Pollo (Cuban Chicken with Rice)
Ingredients:
1. 3 large garlic cloves
2. 2 tablespoons distilled white vinegar
3. 2 teaspoons dried oregano, crumbled
4. 4 chicken breast halves with bone, halved crosswise
5. 4 chicken drumsticks
6. 4 chicken thighs
7. 3 ounces Spanish chorizo (cured sausage), skin discarded and sausage cut into 1/4-inch-thick slices
8. 1 tablespoon olive oil
9. 2 medium onions, chopped
10. 1 green bell pepper, chopped
11. 3 large garlic cloves, chopped
12. 2 teaspoons ground cumin
13. 2 teaspoons dried oregano
14. 1 1/2 teaspoons paprika, preferably the hot stuff, plus more to taste
15. 2 Turkish bay leaves or 1 California
16. 1 lb. tomatoes, seeded and chopped
17. 1 12-ounce bottle beer (not dark)
18. 1 1/2 cups reduced-sodium chicken broth
19. 2 cups long-grain white rice (14 ounces)
20. 1/4 cup drained rinsed bottled pimiento or roasted red pepper strips
Instructions:
1. Mince and mash garlic to a paste with 2 teaspoons salt, then transfer to a large bowl. Stir in vinegar and oregano.
2. Remove skin and excess fat from chicken, then toss chicken with marinade until coated and marinate, covered and chilled, at least 1 hour.
3. Cook chorizo in olive oil in a 6- to 7-quart heavy pot (12 inches wide) over medium-high heat, stirring, until some fat is rendered, 2 to 3 minutes. Add onions, bell pepper, and garlic and cook, stirring until softened, about 5 minutes.
4. Add cumin, oregano, paprika, 1 1/4 teaspoons salt, and bay leaves and cook, stirring, 1 minute.
5. Add chicken with marinade to chorizo mixture and cook, uncovered, over medium heat, stirring frequently, 10 minutes.
6. Stir in tomatoes, beer, broth, and rice and bring to a boil, making sure rice is submerged.
Time:
Prep: 1 hour (including marinating time)
Cook: Approximately 30 minutes
Total: 1 hour 30 minutes
Notes: The only thing I’d change next time is to add much more kick to this recipe. I’d swap the regular paprika with the spicy stuff, and add quite a bit more. In addition, I might finely dice some green pepper on top, instead of the pimentos or red pepper strips. I think it might be a prettier, more texture-contrasted garnish.
Author: Deb

Title: Alain Ducasse's Gougères
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup water
2. 1/2 cup milk
3. 1 stick (4 ounces) unsalted butter, cut into tablespoons
4. Large pinch of coarse salt
5. 1 cup all-purpose flour
6. 4 large eggs
7. 3 1/2 ounces shredded Gruyère cheese (1 cup), plus more for sprinkling
8. Freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F and line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine the water, milk, butter, and salt and bring to a boil.
3. Add the flour all at once and stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until a smooth dough forms.
4. Reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring, until the dough pulls away from the sides of the pan and a film forms on the bottom, about 2 minutes.
5. Transfer the dough to a bowl and let cool slightly.
6. Beat in the eggs one at a time, beating thoroughly between each one, until the dough is smooth and shiny.
7. Stir in the Gruyère cheese and season with pepper.
8. Transfer the dough to a pastry bag fitted with a 1/2-inch round tip and pipe tablespoon-size mounds onto the prepared baking sheets, 2 inches apart.
9. Sprinkle with more Gruyère cheese.
10. Bake for about 22 minutes, until puffed and golden brown.
11. Let cool slightly before serving.
Time:
Prep: 15 mins
Cook: 30 mins
Total: 45 mins
Notes: These gougères can be made ahead and frozen. Reheat in the oven before serving.
Author: Alain Ducasse

Title: Kale and White Bean Stew
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons cooking oil, divided
2. 1/4 pound mild or hot sausages, casings removed
3. 2 onions, chopped
4. 3 garlic cloves, minced
5. 1 pound (about 6 cups) kale, tough stems removed, leaves washed well, and shredded
6. 3 1/3 cups canned diced tomatoes (two 15-ounce cans) with their juice
7. 1 1/4 teaspoons kosher salt
8. 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
9. 4 cups canned cannellini beans (2 15.5-ounce cans), drained and rinsed
Instructions:
1. In a Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over moderate heat. Add the sausage and cook.
2. Break the meat up with a fork, until it loses its pink color, about 2 minutes.
3. Add the remaining tablespoon of oil to the pan and then stir in the onions. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions start to soften, about 3 minutes.
4. Add the garlic and kale to the pan.
5. Cook, stirring, until the kale wilts, about 2 minutes.
6. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, and pepper; bring to a simmer. Reduce the heat and simmer, covered, until the kale is tender, about 15 minutes.
7. Stir the beans into the stew and cook until warmed through, about 5 minutes. If you like, mash some of the beans with a fork to thicken the sauce.
Time: Total: 50 mins
Notes: ['Shred kale leaves as fine as possible when making quick soups and stews, so that each bite will include tender greens instead of a chewy mouthful.']
Author: Food & Wine Editors

Title: Creamed Chard and Spring Onions
Ingredients:
1. 1 1-pound bunch Swiss chard, thick stems removed and leaves sliced into ribbons
2. 3 spring onions, ends trimmed, white and some green parts sliced into thin coins
3. 3 tablespoons butter
4. 3 tablespoons all-purpose flour
5. 1 1/4 cups milk
6. Salt and pepper
Instructions:
1. Wash your chard, but no need to dry it, just place it in a large pot over high heat. Cook, covered, with just the water clinging to leaves, stirring occasionally, until wilted, about 6 minutes.
2. Press or squeeze out the excess liquid any number of ways, either by wringing it out in cheesecloth (my favorite method), putting it in a mesh strainer and pressing the moisture out with a spatula or large spoon or letting it cool long enough to grab small handfuls and squeezing them to remove as much water as possible.
3. Wipe out the large pot so you can use it again. Heat milk or cream in a small saucepan over moderate heat, stirring, until warm. Keep warm.
4. Meanwhile, cook onion and garlic, if using, in butter in your wiped-out large pot over moderately low heat, stirring occasionally, until softened, about six minutes.
5. Whisk in flour and cook roux, whisking, about three minutes.
6. Add warm milk or cream in a slow stream, whisking constantly to prevent lumps, and simmer, whisking, until thickened, three to four minutes.
7. Stir in chard, then salt and pepper to taste and cook, stirring, until heated through.
Time: Cook: 20 minutes
Notes: You can swap cream or half and half for all or a portion of the milk, if you want this to be extra lush. You could also stir in a few tablespoons of grated Parmesan.
Author: Deb

Title: Summer-Vegetable Casserole
Ingredients:
1. Extra-virgin olive oil, for drizzling
2. 1/2 pound Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and sliced 1/4 inch thick
3. Salt and freshly ground pepper
4. 1 yellow bell pepper, thinly sliced
5. 1 small onion, thinly sliced
6. 1 large garlic clove, minced
7. 1 teaspoon thyme leaves
8. 1/2 pound plum tomatoes, sliced 1/4 inch thick
9. 2 small zucchini (1/2 pound), sliced on the diagonal 1/4 inch thick
10. 3 tablespoons freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°. Coat a 9-inch baking dish with olive oil. Spread the potatoes in the dish in an even layer; drizzle with oil and season with salt and pepper.
2. In a bowl, combine the bell pepper, onion, garlic and thyme and season with salt and pepper. Arrange two-thirds of the bell pepper mixture over the potatoes and drizzle with oil.
3. Top with the tomatoes and the zucchini; drizzle with oil and season with salt and pepper. Cover with the remaining bell pepper mixture and sprinkle with the cheese.
4. Cover the casserole with foil and bake for 40 minutes. Increase the oven temperature to 425°. Uncover the casserole and bake for about 20 minutes longer, until the vegetables are tender and glazed on top.
5. Let stand for 10 minutes. Serve warm.
Time:
Prep: 15 mins
Cook: 1 hr 10 mins
Total: 1 hr 25 mins
Author: Sextantio hotel

Title: Braised Leeks With Lemon and Parsley
Ingredients:
1. 4 large leeks, trimmed and halved lengthwise
2. 2 tablespoons olive oil
3. 1/2 cup dry white wine
4. 1 cup chicken or turkey broth
5. 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
6. Salt and freshly ground black pepper
7. 1 lemon, juiced
8. 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 325°F (163°C).
2. Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Place leeks cut side down in the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
4. Add white wine and broth to the skillet, then add butter.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Bring the liquid to a simmer, then transfer the skillet to the oven.
7. Braise the leeks in the oven until tender, about 30 minutes.
8. Remove from the oven and drizzle with lemon juice.
9. Sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 35 minutes
Total: 45 minutes
Notes: For enhanced sweetness, brown the leeks cut side down before adding the braising liquid.
Author: J. Kenji López-Alt

Title: Cold Rice Noodles with Peanut-Lime Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken thighs
2. 1 tablespoon fresh lime juice
3. 1 tablespoon fish sauce
4. 1 tablespoon soy sauce
5. 1 tablespoon brown sugar
6. 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
7. 2 cloves garlic, minced
8. 8 ounces rice noodles
9. 1/4 cup peanut butter
10. 2 tablespoons soy sauce
11. 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice
12. 2 tablespoons rice vinegar
13. 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
14. 1 to 2 teaspoons chili paste or 1 to 2 serrano chiles, minced
15. 1/4 cup chopped roasted peanuts
16. 1/2 cup chopped fresh mint, basil, and/or cilantro
17. 1 cucumber, julienned
18. 2 carrots, julienned
19. 4 scallions, thinly sliced
Instructions:
1. Marinate the chicken: In a bowl, combine lime juice, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, ginger, and garlic. Add chicken and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
2. Cook the chicken: Grill or broil the chicken until cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Let cool and chop into bite-sized pieces.
3. Cook the noodles: Prepare rice noodles according to package instructions. Drain and rinse under cold water.
4. Make the peanut sauce: In a bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, rice vinegar, ginger, and chili paste or minced chiles.
5. Assemble the dish: In a large bowl, combine noodles, chicken, cucumber, carrots, scallions, and herbs. Toss with peanut sauce until well coated.
6. Serve: Garnish with chopped peanuts and additional herbs, if desired.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 15 minutes
Total: 45 minutes
Notes: The original recipe suggested using lemongrass and mung bean sprouts, but this adaptation omits them for simplicity. Adjust the amount of chiles based on your heat preference.
Author: Deb Perelman

Title: Slow-Cooker Beef Ragu
Ingredients:
1. 1 medium yellow onion, diced small
2. 3 garlic cloves, minced
3. 6 tablespoons tomato paste
4. 3 tablespoons chopped fresh oregano leaves (or 3 teaspoons dried)
5. 1 beef chuck roast (4 pounds), halved
6. Coarse salt and ground pepper
7. 1 to 2 tablespoons red-wine vinegar
Instructions:
1. In a 5-to-6-quart slow cooker, combine onion, garlic, tomato paste, and oregano. Season roast with salt and pepper and place on top of onion mixture.
2. Add 2 cups water, cover, and cook on high until meat is tender and can easily be pulled apart with a fork, 4 1/2 hours (or 9 hours on low). Let cool 10 minutes, then shred meat in slow cooker with 2 forks and stir in vinegar to taste.
Time:
Prep: 10 mins
Cook: 4 1/2 hours on high or 9 hours on low
Total: 5 hrs
Notes: Served over pasta or polenta, or in sandwich rolls, this simple meat dish becomes a star.
Author: Martha Stewart

Title: Monte Cristo Strata
Ingredients:
1. Softened butter
2. One 1 1/4-pound loaf bakery white bread—crusts removed, bread sliced (about 18 slices)
3. 1/4 cup grainy mustard
4. 1 pound thinly sliced Virginia ham
5. 2 tablespoons chopped tarragon, plus more for garnish
6. 3/4 pound Gruyère cheese, coarsely shredded (3 cups)
7. 3 cups milk
8. 4 large eggs
9. Freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375° and butter a 9-by-13-inch glass or ceramic baking dish. Arrange one-third of the bread in the bottom of the dish and spread with half of the mustard. Top with half of the ham, 1 tablespoon of the tarragon and one-third of the Gruyère cheese. Repeat the layering, leaving off the last layer of cheese.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk the milk with the eggs and season generously with black pepper. Pour the custard evenly over the dish, pressing the bread to absorb the liquid. Pat the remaining cheese on top and cover with a sheet of buttered parchment paper.
3. Bake the strata in the center of the oven for 30 to 35 minutes, until bubbling and browned around the edges. Remove the parchment and turn on the broiler. Broil for about 3 minutes, just until the top is golden and bubbling. Let rest for 10 minutes, then cut the strata into squares and serve.
Time:
Prep: 10 mins
Cook: 30 to 35 mins
Total: 1 hr
Notes: ['The unbaked strata can be refrigerated overnight.', 'Suggested Pairing: Fruity sparkling wine: NV Col Mesian Nove Cento Dieci Spumante Extra Dry.']
Author: Grace Parisi

Title: Crab Maison
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup mayonnaise
2. 2 tbsp. high quality olive oil
3. 2 tsp. white wine vinegar
4. 1 tsp. Creole or Dijon mustard
5. 1 tsp. small capers, rinsed, drained, and finely chopped
6. 1/2 tsp. finely chopped flat-leaf parsley
7. 1/2 tsp. kosher salt
8. 1/8 tsp. freshly ground white pepper
9. 3 green onions, finely chopped
10. 1 lb. jumbo lump crab meat
11. 12 large leaves butter lettuce
12. 4 slices tomato, halved
Instructions:
1. Whisk together mayonnaise, oil, vinegar, mustard, capers, parsley, salt, pepper, and scallions.
2. Add crabmeat and fold gently to combine.
3. Place 3 leaves of lettuce on each of 4 salad plates.
4. Divide crab mixture evenly among plates and garnish with two half slices of tomato.
Notes: A popular appetizer at Galatoire's restaurant in New Orleans, this light and satisfying salad is a great lunch on Mardi Gras with crusty French bread.
Author: Anna Stockwell

Title: World's Easiest Falafel and Tzatziki
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups dried chickpeas, rinsed well and soaked overnight
2. 1 small yellow onion
3. 3 cloves garlic, divided
4. 1 bunch mint, washed, divided
5. 1/2 bunch cilantro, rinsed and coarsely chopped
6. 1 egg (optional)
7. 1 piece bread
8. 2 pinches salt
9. 1/2 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper
10. 2 teaspoons ground cumin
11. 2 lemons, juiced, divided
12. 1 cup canola oil, for frying
13. 1 cucumber, peeled and seeded
14. 1 cup plain yogurt, Greek-style preferred
15. Salt and pepper to taste
16. 1 package pita or flatbread
Instructions:
1. Drain chickpeas and let air dry for 2 hours, or more.
2. Process chickpeas, onion, 2 cloves garlic, half bunch mint, cilantro, egg, bread, salt and pepper, cumin, and half the lemon juice on low speed until a thick paste forms. No chunks or your falafel balls will fall apart.
3. Form into 3-inch patties and let rest while tzatziki is made.
4. Rinse processor and pulse cucumber, yogurt, remaining mint, remaining lemon juice, and the last garlic clove on low just until blended. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Heat the canola oil in a high-sided, heavy-bottomed pan. When hot, fry patties on medium high heat for 3 minutes each side or until golden brown.
6. Serve with warm pita or flatbread.
Time:
Prep: 27 hours
Cook: 1 hour
Total: 28 hours
Notes: The recipe is noted as being easy and suitable for making with children. The food processor is used for both the falafel mixture and tzatziki, making the process efficient.
Author: marisab67

Title: Momofuku's Roasted Brussels Sprouts With Fish Sauce Vinaigrette
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons very thinly sliced cilantro stems, plus 1/2 cup leaves
2. 3 tablespoons chopped mint
3. 2 pounds brussels sprouts (smaller ones are better)
4. 2 tablespoons grapeseed or other neutral oil (or 3 cups if frying instead of roasting)
5. 1/2 cup fish sauce (adjust to taste -- some fish sauce brands are saltier)
6. 1/4 cup water
7. 2 tablespoons rice wine vinegar
8. 2 tablespoons lime juice (from 1 lime)
9. 1/4 cup sugar
10. 1 garlic clove, minced
11. 2 red bird’s-eye chiles, thinly sliced, seeds intact
Instructions:
1. Combine the vinaigrette (below), cilantro stems, and mint in a bowl, and set aside.
2. Peel away any loose or discolored outer leaves, trim the dry end of the stems with a knife, and cut the sprouts in half. Cut any especially large ones in quarters. Do not wash, especially if frying the sprouts. If roasting, and you must, dry very well.
3. To roast the brussels sprouts (recommended): Heat the oven to 400 degrees F. Heat 2 tablespoons grapeseed oil (or just enough to evenly coat the bottom of the pan) in 2 oven-safe wide skillets (12 to 14 inches) over medium heat. When the oil slides easily from side to side of the pan, add the brussels sprouts cut side down. When the cut faces of the sprouts begin to brown, transfer the pan to the oven to finish cooking, about 15 minutes. Alternately, if you don't have 2 large skillets or are cooking more sprouts for a larger crowd, roast them in the oven: toss them with 1 tablespoon of oil per pound and spread them on a baking sheet, cut sides down. Roast in the oven, checking for browning every 10-15 minutes, tossing them around with a spatula only once they start to brown nicely. The sprouts are ready when they are tender but not soft, with nice, dark brown color.
4. To fry the brussels sprouts: Heat 1 1/2 inches of oil in a deep saucepan over medium-high heat until a deep-fry or instant-read thermometer registers 375°F. Line a plate with paper towels. Fry in batches that don’t crowd the pan -- be careful, these will pop and spatter. Brussels sprouts will take about 5 minutes: when the outer leaves begin to hint at going black around the edges—i.e., after the sprouts have sizzled, shrunk, popped, and browned but before they burn—remove them to a paper towel–lined plate or tray.
5. Serve warm or at room temperature. When ready to serve, divide the brussels sprouts among four bowls (or serve it all out of one big bowl), top with the dressing to taste and cilantro leaves, and toss once or twice to coat.
6. Combine the fish sauce, water, vinegar, lime juice, sugar, garlic, and chiles in a jar. Taste; If too salty, add more water and/or lime juice. This vinaigrette will keep for up to a week in the refrigerator.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 35 minutes
Notes: This recipe will add much-needed spark to your winter diet (and your Thanksgiving spread). Adapted lightly from Momofuku by David Chang and Peter Meehan (Clarkson Potter, 2009).
Author: Genius Recipes

Title: Kale Caesar with Rye Croutons and Farro
Ingredients:
1. Kale
2. Rye croutons
3. Farro
4. Roasted garlic
5. Vinegar
6. Mustard
7. Worcestershire sauce
8. Mayonnaise
9. Toasted sesame oil
10. Soy sauce
11. Peanut butter
12. Rice wine vinegar
13. Honey
14. Nutritional yeast
15. Orange juice
16. Shallot
17. Garlic
18. Miso
19. Sesame seeds
20. Brown sugar
21. Yogurt
22. Red wine vinegar
23. Crushed red pepper
24. Tahini
25. Ginger
26. Sunflower seeds
27. Za’atar
Instructions:
1. Puree roasted garlic with vinegar, mustard, Worcestershire, and a little mayo for a creamy garlic dressing.
2. Combine toasted sesame oil, soy sauce, peanut butter, and rice wine vinegar for a nutty sesame dressing.
3. Mix vinegar, orange juice, mustard, shallot, and garlic for a citrus dressing.
4. Whisk miso with vinegar, sesame seeds, oil, and brown sugar for a miso dressing.
5. Blend garlic, red wine vinegar, yogurt, and crushed red pepper for a spicy yogurt dressing.
6. Mix tahini and ginger for a sesame-based tahini-ginger dressing.
7. Blend sunflower seeds with za’atar for a creamy sunflower seed vinaigrette.
Time:
Prep: 10-15 minutes
Cook: None
Total: 10-15 minutes
Notes: These dressings pair perfectly with kale and its accompaniments. Make a double batch of the tahini-ginger dressing to have enough for a full week of easy lunches.
Author: Kristin Donnelly

Title: Summer Guide 2013 - Beachcomber’s Handbook
Notes: The content provided does not contain a recipe. It is an article about the state of beaches post-Hurricane Sandy.
Author: Matthew Giles, Hana R. Alberts, Annie Karni

Title: Pkhali (Spinach and Walnut Salad)
Ingredients:
1. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
2. 1 1/2 lb. baby spinach
3. 1 1/2 cups toasted walnuts
4. 1/2 cup roughly chopped cilantro
5. 1/2 cup roughly chopped parsley
6. 1 tsp. hot paprika
7. 1/2 tsp. ground turmeric
8. 1/2 tsp. ground fenugreek
9. 3 tbsp. olive oil
10. 2 tbsp. red wine vinegar
11. 2 cloves garlic, roughly chopped
12. 1 small yellow onion, roughly chopped
13. Pomegranate seeds, for garnish
Instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add spinach; cook until wilted, about 1 minute.
2. Using a slotted spoon, transfer spinach to a bowl of ice water; drain and squeeze out as much water as possible.
3. Transfer to a food processor; purée until almost smooth, 1-2 minutes, and transfer to a bowl.
4. Add walnuts, cilantro, parsley, paprika, turmeric, fenugreek, oil, vinegar, garlic, onions, salt, and pepper to the food processor; purée until very smooth.
5. Mix walnut purée and spinach together; transfer to a serving platter and garnish with pomegranate seeds.
Notes: Almost any vegetable can be substituted for spinach in this vegetarian appetizer. In Georgia, roasted beet and green bean versions are common.
Author: Landon Nordeman

Title: Tiger Salad
Ingredients:
1. 1 tablespoon unseasoned rice vinegar
2. 2 teaspoons toasted sesame oil
3. 1 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
4. 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
5. 1 bunch cilantro leaves with tender stems, cut into 2 inches pieces
6. 4 celery stalks, thinly sliced on a diagonal
7. 2 small cucumbers or 1/2 large, thinly sliced
8. 6 scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal
9. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
Instructions:
1. Whisk vinegar, oil, sesame seeds, and red pepper flakes in a large bowl. Add cilantro, celery, cucumbers, and scallions. Season with salt and pepper and toss to combine.
2. Salad can be made 6 hours ahead. Cover and chill.
Notes: This cooling and refreshing salad—with cilantro standing in for the lettuce—is great alongside spicy foods.
Author: Alison Roman

Title: Pork Meatball Banh Mi
Ingredients:
1. 2/3 cup mayonnaise
2. 2 green onions, finely chopped
3. 1 tablespoon hot chili sauce (such as sriracha)
4. 1 pound ground pork
5. 1/4 cup finely chopped fresh basil
6. 4 garlic cloves, minced
7. 3 green onions, finely chopped
8. 1 tablespoon fish sauce (such as nam pla or nuoc nam)
9. 1 tablespoon hot chili sauce (such as sriracha)
10. 1 tablespoon sugar
11. 2 teaspoons cornstarch
12. 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
13. 1 teaspoon coarse kosher salt
14. 2 cups coarsely grated carrots
15. 2 cups coarsely grated peeled daikon (Japanese white radish)
16. 1/4 cup unseasoned rice vinegar
17. 1/4 cup sugar
18. 1 teaspoon coarse kosher salt
19. 1 tablespoon Asian sesame oil
20. 4 10-inch-long individual baguettes or four 10-inch-long pieces French-bread baguette (cut from 2 baguettes)
21. Thinly sliced jalapeño chiles
22. 16 large fresh cilantro sprigs
Instructions:
1. Stir all ingredients for hot chili mayo in a small bowl. Season with salt. Cover and chill if making ahead.
2. Line a large dinner plate or pie dish with plastic wrap. Gently mix all meatball ingredients in a large bowl. Using moistened hands, roll meat mixture into 1-inch meatballs. Arrange on plate. Cover and chill if making ahead.
3. Toss the first 5 sandwich ingredients in a medium bowl. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour, tossing occasionally.
4. Preheat oven to 300°F. Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add half of the meatballs. Sauté until brown and cooked through, turning meatballs often and lowering heat if browning too quickly, about 15 minutes. Transfer meatballs to a rimmed baking sheet. Place in oven to keep warm. Repeat with remaining meatballs.
5. Cut each baguette or baguette piece horizontally in half. Scoop out the inside of each piece of bread. Spread hot chili mayo on both sides of bread. Arrange jalapeños, then cilantro, on bottom halves. Top with 1/4 of meatballs. Drain pickled vegetables; place atop meatballs. Put top halves on each sandwich.
Time: Cook: 15 minutes
Notes: Can be made 1 day ahead.
Author: Jeanne Thiel Kelley

Title: Stuffed Eggplant Galatoire’s Restaurant
Ingredients:
1. 1 lb. eggplant
2. 1/4 cup minced onion
3. 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
4. 1/4 cup butter
5. Salt and pepper to taste
6. 1/2 cup chopped cooked crabmeat
7. 1/2 cup chopped cooked shrimps
8. 1/2 cup bechamel sauce
9. 2 tablespoons freshly grated Parmesan cheese
10. 2 tablespoons bread crumbs
11. 1 tablespoon melted butter
Instructions:
1. Halve lengthwise a 1-lb. eggplant and put halves, cut side down, into baking pan just large enough to hold them.
2. Cover pan with foil.
3. Bake eggplant in 375F for 50 min., or until it is tender.
4. Scoop out pulp, leaving shells intact, reserve shells. Chop pulp.
5. In skillet, saute 1/4 c. minced onion and 1 tbs. chopped parsley in 1/4 c. butter until onion is browned.
6. Stir in chopped eggplant, add salt and pepper to taste, and cook mixture, covered, over moderate heat for 5 min., or until eggplant is very soft.
7. Add 1/2 c. each chopped cooked crabmeat, chopped cooked shrimps, and bechamel sauce.
8. Put reserved shells in baking dish just large enough to hold them.
9. Divide stuffing between shells and sprinkle each shell with 2 tbs. each freshly grated Parmesan cheese and bread crumbs and 1 tbs. melted butter.
10. Bake at 425F for 10 min., or until topping is golden.
Time: Cook: 60 min
Notes: The recipe suggests adding additional seasoning like garlic or something spicy for enhanced flavor.
Author: Nancy Brown

Title: Chile-Braised Pork Shoulder Tacos
Ingredients:
1. 4 large dried ancho chiles (about 3/4 oz.), stemmed, seeded
2. 2 large dried chiles de árbol or japones chiles, stemmed, seeded
3. 2 Tbsp. sugar
4. 1 Tbsp. fresh lime juice
5. 1 5-lb. boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt)
6. Kosher salt
7. 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil
8. 1 large onion, chopped (about 2 cups)
9. 3 large garlic cloves, coarsely chopped
10. 2 bay leaves
11. 2 tsp. dried oregano, preferably Mexican
12. 2 tsp. ground coriander
13. 2 tsp. ground cumin
14. 1/2 tsp. ground allspice
15. 1 12-oz. bottle Negro Modelo or other dark beer
16. 24 (or more) 6' corn tortillas
17. 4 radishes, trimmed, thinly sliced
18. Tomato-Serrano Salsa
19. Tomatillo-Chipotle Salsa
20. Pickled Onions
21. Chopped fresh cilantro
Instructions:
1. Place chiles in a medium bowl. Add enough boiling water to cover, and set a small plate or bowl on chiles to keep submerged. Let soak until softened, about 30 minutes. Drain chiles, reserving 1 cup soaking liquid.
2. Preheat oven to 350°. Place chiles, sugar, lime juice, and ¼ cup reserved soaking liquid in a blender. Purée chile mixture, adding more soaking liquid as needed to form a smooth paste. Season pork shoulder generously with salt and spread paste over pork. DO AHEAD Can be rubbed 1-2 days ahead. Cover and chill. Let come to room temperature before continuing.
3. Heat oil in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, bay leaves, oregano, coriander, cumin, and allspice. Cook, stirring often, until onion is soft, about 8 minutes. Add beer; bring to a boil. Add pork to pot; cover and transfer to oven.
4. Braise pork, basting occasionally with pan juices, until very tender, about 2 ½ hours. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 days ahead. Let cool slightly. Chill uncovered until cold, then cover and keep chilled. Rewarm before continuing.
5. Transfer pork to a large platter. Let cool slightly. Pour pan juice mixture over pork; cover and keep warm. (Alternatively, shred the pork with 2 forks and pour pan juice mixture over pork.)
6. Meanwhile, working in batches, cook tortillas in a large heavy skillet over low heat until toasted, about 1 minute per side. Transfer to a large sheet of foil; wrap to keep warm.
7. Serve pork with tortillas, radishes, both salsas, pickled onions, and chopped cilantro, encouraging guests to fill and garnish tacos as desired.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 2 ½ hours
Total: 3 hours
Notes: Serve this succulent spiced pork shoulder recipe at the table with a pair of tongs or shred it in the kitchen and place on a platter.
Author: Bon Appétit Test Kitchen

Title: Slow-Roasted Green Beans With Sage
Ingredients:
1. 2 1/2 pounds tender green beans, trimmed
2. 3 bunches scallions, trimmed with 1-inch green tops still attached, halved lengthwise
3. 6 large garlic cloves, each cut lengthwise into 4 slices
4. 1/2 cup olive oil
5. 2 tablespoons fresh sage leaves
6. 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary leaves
7. 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves
8. 2 teaspoons kosher salt
9. Freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°.
2. Combine first 8 ingredients in a large bowl and season with pepper. Toss to evenly incorporate.
3. Transfer beans to a large rimmed baking sheet.
4. Roast beans, stirring every 10 minutes, until wilted, shrunken, and browned at edges, about 1 hour. (You may need to stir more often toward end for even browning.)
Time: Cook: 1 hour
Author: Suzanne Goin

Title: Chicken Khao Soi
Ingredients:
1. 4 large dried New Mexico or guajillo chiles, stemmed, halved, seeded
2. 2 medium shallots, halved
3. 8 garlic cloves
4. 1 2" piece ginger, peeled, sliced
5. ¼ cup chopped cilantro stems
6. 1 tablespoon ground coriander
7. 1 tablespoon ground turmeric
8. 1 teaspoon curry powder
9. 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
10. 2 14-oz. cans unsweetened coconut milk
11. 2 cups low-sodium chicken broth
12. 1½ lb skinless, boneless chicken thighs, halved lengthwise
13. 1 lb Chinese egg noodles
14. 3 tablespoons (or more) fish sauce (such as nam pla or nuoc nam)
15. 1 tablespoon (packed) palm sugar or light brown sugar
16. Kosher salt
17. Sliced red onion, bean sprouts, cilantro sprigs, crispy fried onions or shallots, chili oil, and lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Place chiles in a small heatproof bowl, add boiling water to cover, and let soak until softened, 25–30 minutes.
2. Drain chiles, reserving soaking liquid. Purée chiles, shallots, garlic, ginger, cilantro stems, coriander, turmeric, curry powder, and 2 Tbsp. soaking liquid in a food processor, adding more soaking liquid by tablespoonfuls, if needed, until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Add khao soi paste; cook, stirring constantly, until slightly darkened, 4–6 minutes. Add coconut milk and broth. Bring to a boil; add chicken. Reduce heat and simmer until chicken is fork-tender, 20–25 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate. Let cool slightly; shred meat.
4. Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package directions.
5. Add chicken, 3 Tbsp. fish sauce, and sugar to soup. Season with salt or more fish sauce, if needed. Divide soup and noodles among bowls and serve with toppings.
Time: Cook: 45 minutes
Notes: Dried chiles are available at Latin markets; Chinese egg noodles and chili oil are available at Asian markets. All can be found at many supermarkets.
Author: Ravin Nakjaroen of Long Grain in Camden, ME

Title: Slow Cooker Boeuf Bourguignon
Notes: Access to the webpage was denied, so the recipe details could not be extracted.

Title: Chunky Tomato Soup
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons olive oil
2. 1 large onion, chopped
3. 2 garlic cloves, minced
4. 1 tablespoon tomato paste
5. 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper
6. 2 pounds tomatoes, chopped
7. 4 cups vegetable broth
8. Salt and freshly ground pepper
9. 1/4 cup chopped fresh basil
Instructions:
1. 1. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. 2. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
3. 3. Stir in the garlic, tomato paste, and crushed red pepper; cook for 1 minute.
4. 4. Add the tomatoes and cook until they begin to break down, about 5 minutes.
5. 5. Stir in the vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
6. 6. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
7. 7. Puree half of the soup in a blender until smooth.
8. 8. Return the pureed soup to the pot and stir to combine.
9. 9. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. 10. Stir in the basil just before serving.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 40 minutes
Total: 50 minutes
Notes: For a creamier soup, you can add a splash of cream or a dollop of yogurt before serving.
Author: Steven Satterfield

Title: Hakka Salt-Baked Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 1 (3 1/2- to 4-pound) chicken, preferably free-range, rinsed and patted dry
2. 1 1/2 tablespoons Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
3. 5 quarter-size slices fresh ginger, lightly smashed
4. 2 scallions, lightly smashed and cut into 1 1/2-inch lengths
5. 5 cilantro sprigs, plus additional cilantro leaves for garnish
6. 1 star anise pod
7. 1 tablespoon Rose Dew Liqueur or extra-dry vermouth
8. 4 1/2 pounds kosher salt
9. 1 tablespoon Chinese chile sauce (such as Tso Hin Kee Chinese Chili Sauce)
10. 1 tablespoon hot water
11. 1 teaspoon rice vinegar
12. 1/2 teaspoon granulated sugar
13. 1 tablespoon finely chopped peeled fresh ginger
14. 2 tablespoons corn or peanut oil
15. 1/4 teaspoon kosher salt
Instructions:
1. Rub the cavity of the chicken with the rice wine and stuff with the ginger, scallions, cilantro sprigs, and star anise. Place the chicken, breast up, on a rack and brush the outside with the liqueur. Let the chicken stand in a cool, airy place until the skin is dry to the touch, about 2 hours.
2. Truss the chicken with string. Wrap the chicken in a single layer of cheesecloth; bring the ends around and tie them together on top of the chicken breast with string. The chicken should be tightly wrapped in a neat ball.
3. Pour the salt into a large heavy stockpot or spun-steel wok that will hold the chicken snugly. Turn the heat to moderately high and stir the salt frequently until it is very hot to the touch, about 10 minutes. Carefully pour all but 1 inch of the salt (about 6 cups) into a heatproof bowl. Set the chicken in the pot, breast up, and cover completely with the reserved hot salt. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to moderate, and cook the chicken undisturbed on top of the stove for 1 1/2 hours. Check for doneness by brushing off the salt and piercing a thigh with a sharp knife. If the juices are still pink, re-cover with salt and continue to cook for another 15 to 30 minutes.
4. Push the salt aside and carefully remove the chicken, holding it by the knotted cheesecloth; try not to tilt the chicken and spill the juices. Set the chicken on a large platter and remove the cheesecloth and trussing strings. Discard the salt. Carve the chicken or use a cleaver to chop it Chinese-style into bite-size pieces. Serve hot or at room temperature, garnished with cilantro leaves and accompanied by the Hakka dipping sauces.
5. For the chile sauce, whisk together all ingredients in a small bowl until sugar dissolves. Let stand at least 15 minutes; stir before serving.
6. For the ginger sauce, place ginger in a small, heavy serving saucer. Heat oil in a small saucepan over medium-high until it sends up a wisp of smoke, then pour over ginger. Stir to combine. Sprinkle with salt, and stir to combine. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Time:
Prep: 2 hrs (stand time)
Cook: 1 hr 45 mins
Total: 4 hrs 15 mins
Notes: The Chinese chile sauce called for in the Hakka chile sauce recipe is a fruity-hot blend of chiles and spices. Do not confuse chile sauce with hot bean paste or a product labeled 'chili paste with garlic.'
Author: Barbara Tropp

Title: Spicy Chicken Soup
Ingredients:
1. 1 prepared rotisserie chicken
2. 1 tablespoon olive oil
3. 1 medium onion, thinly sliced
4. 1/2 cup sliced shiitake mushrooms
5. 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped
6. 1 1" piece ginger, peeled, chopped
7. 2 quarts low-sodium chicken broth
8. 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper
9. 2 cups baby spinach
10. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
11. 2 scallions, thinly sliced
12. Lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Pick meat from chicken, shred, and place in a small bowl (you should have about 4 cups); discard skin and bones.
2. Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
3. Add onion and mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and golden, 8–10 minutes.
4. Add garlic and ginger and cook, stirring often, 2 minutes; season with salt and pepper.
5. Add shredded chicken, broth, and cayenne and bring to a boil.
6. Add spinach and divide soup among bowls.
7. Top with scallions and serve lime wedges alongside for squeezing over.
Time: Total: 40 minutes
Notes: Store-bought rotisserie chicken makes it easy to create a meal without having to cook a whole bird.
Author: Bon Appétit Test Kitchen

Title: Charred Romanesco with Anchovies and Mint
Ingredients:
1. ½ cup olive oil, divided
2. 1 large onion, minced
3. 1 large carrot, minced
4. 1 celery stalk, minced
5. 4 Fresno chiles or jalapeños, thinly sliced into rounds, seeded
6. Kosher salt
7. 3 pounds Romanesco or regular cauliflower (about 2 medium heads), cut into florets
8. 4 anchovy fillets packed in oil, drained
9. ½ cup dry white wine
10. Freshly ground black pepper
11. ½ cup torn fresh mint leaves
Instructions:
1. Heat ¼ cup oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add onion, carrot, celery, and chiles; season with salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are very soft but not brown, 12–15 minutes. Let soffritto cool.
2. Preheat oven to 450°. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add half of Romanesco and cook, undisturbed, until deeply browned, about 4 minutes. Transfer to a roasting pan; repeat with remaining 2 Tbsp. oil and remaining Romanesco. Add soffritto, anchovies, and wine to roasting pan; season with salt and pepper and toss to combine.
3. Roast, tossing halfway through, until Romanesco is soft and liquid is mostly evaporated, 25–30 minutes; season with salt and pepper and top with mint.
Time:
Prep: 12–15 minutes
Cook: 25–30 minutes
Total: 37–45 minutes
Notes: Gjelina chef Travis Lett says that getting a good, dark sear on the Romanesco cauliflower is critical to the flavor of the dish: 'There’s a nuttiness when you get that color on it.'
Author: Travis Lett of Gjelina in Venice CA

Title: Pork Shoulder Al’Diavolo
Ingredients:
1. 1 skinless, boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt; about 6 lb.)
2. Kosher salt
3. 1 tablespoon black peppercorns
4. 1 tablespoon coriander seeds
5. 1 tablespoon crushed red pepper flakes
6. 1 tablespoon dried oregano
7. 1 tablespoon yellow mustard seeds
8. ½ cup olive oil
9. 6 garlic cloves, peeled, crushed
10. 1 tablespoon finely grated lemon zest
11. 2 teaspoons smoked paprika
Instructions:
1. Using the tip of a knife, lightly score fatty side of pork; season all over with salt.
2. Coarsely grind peppercorns, coriander, red pepper flakes, oregano, and mustard seeds in spice mill or with mortar and pestle; set spice mixture aside.
3. Heat oil in a small saucepan over low heat; add garlic and cook, stirring, until fragrant and barely golden, about 5 minutes. Stir in lemon zest, paprika, and reserved spice mixture. Let marinade cool.
4. Rub marinade all over pork, working some marinade into interior of roast. Tie pork at 1” intervals with kitchen twine. Wrap tightly in plastic and chill at least 8 hours.
5. Let pork sit at room temperature 1 hour.
6. Preheat oven to 375°. Unwrap pork, place on a rack set inside a roasting pan, and roast until golden brown and fat has just started to render, 40–50 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 300° and continue to roast until meat is very tender, 1½–2 hours longer.
7. Transfer pork to a cutting board and let rest 30 minutes before slicing.
Time:
Prep: 9 hours (including chilling and resting time)
Cook: 2 to 2.5 hours
Total: 11 to 11.5 hours
Notes: Marinade can be made 1 week ahead; cover and chill. Pork can be seasoned 3 days ahead; keep chilled.
Author: Taylor Boetticher and Toponia Miller

Title: Roasted Carrots With Harissa and Crème Fraîche
Ingredients:
1. 2 pounds (900 g) medium carrots, ends trimmed, quartered lengthwise and cut into 3-inch segments
2. Kosher salt
3. 1/4 cup (60 ml) crème fraîche
4. 2 tablespoons (30 ml) harissa paste
5. 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
6. 1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
7. 1/4 cup (60 ml) extra-virgin olive oil
8. 2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh cilantro leaves
Instructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to center position and preheat oven to 375°F. Place carrots in a large pot, cover with water, and season heavily with salt. Heat over high heat until boiling. Reduce to a simmer and cook until barely tender, about 5 minutes. Drain and allow to dry for 5 minutes. Set aside. Season crème fraîche with salt to taste and refrigerate until ready to use.
2. Combine harissa, cumin, black pepper, and olive oil in a large bowl. Season to taste with salt. Transfer half of mixture to small bowl and set aside. Add carrots to remaining mixture and toss to coat. Transfer to foil-lined rimmed baking sheet and spread out in an even layer. Roast until caramelized, about 40 minutes, turning once or twice during roasting. Transfer carrots to a large bowl and toss with cilantro.
3. Spread crème fraîche on serving platter. Top with carrots and drizzle with remaining harissa mixture. Serve immediately.
Time:
Prep: 20 mins
Cook: 45 mins
Total: 50 mins
Notes: Harissa is a North African chili paste that can be found in specialty markets or ordered online.
Author: J. Kenji López-Alt

Title: Thai Shrimp-and-Coconut Soup with Lemongrass
Ingredients:
1. 5 Thai chiles, seeded and chopped
2. 4 stalks of fresh lemongrass, tender inner bulb only, finely chopped
3. 4 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped
4. 1/3 cup coarsely chopped peeled fresh ginger
5. 1 large shallot, coarsely chopped
6. 1 tablespoon coconut oil or vegetable oil
7. 3 1/2 cups unsweetened coconut milk
8. 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons dark brown sugar
9. 1/4 cup fish sauce
10. 1/4 cup tamarind concentrate
11. 6 fresh makrut lime leaves
12. 3 tablespoons fresh lime juice
13. Salt and freshly ground pepper
14. 3/4 pound medium shrimp, shelled and deveined
15. Cooked rice vermicelli or steamed rice, for serving (optional)
Instructions:
1. In a blender, combine the chiles, lemongrass, garlic, ginger, shallot and 1/4 cup of water and puree until smooth.
2. In a medium saucepan, heat the coconut oil. Add the lemongrass puree and cook over moderately high heat, stirring, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Whisk in the coconut milk, brown sugar, fish sauce, tamarind, lime leaves and 1 1/2 cups of water and bring to a gentle boil. Simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the soup is flavorful and slightly reduced, about 15 minutes.
3. Stir the lime juice into the soup and season with salt and pepper. Add the shrimp and simmer until slightly curled and just cooked through, about 1 minute. Ladle the soup into bowls, add the rice noodles and serve.
Time:
Prep: 20 mins
Cook: 25 mins
Total: 45 mins
Notes: Tamarind concentrate and makrut lime leaves are available at specialty food stores like New York City's Kalustyan's (kalustyans.com) and at many southeast Asian markets.
Author: Harold Dieterle

Title: Hot Toddy
Ingredients:
1. 1 1/2 ounces bourbon or rye (or any dark spirit including aged rum or cognac)
2. 3/4 ounces honey or maple syrup
3. 4-5 ounces hot water
4. Garnish: lemon wheel and cinnamon stick
Instructions:
1. Add spirit and honey or maple syrup to a mug.
2. Top with hot water and stir gently to dissolve honey or maple syrup.
3. Garnish with a lemon wheel and a cinnamon stick.
Notes: This version is a template prime for riffing with a base of any dark spirit, plus hot water, citrus, honey and spices.

Title: Galatoire's Rémoulade Blanc
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup mayonnaise
2. 2 tbsp. Creole mustard
3. 2 tbsp. dry white wine
4. 1 1/2 tbsp. fresh lemon juice
5. 1 tbsp. grated horseradish
6. 2 tsp. minced parsley, plus more
7. 1/4 tsp. cayenne
8. 1/4 tsp. freshly ground white pepper
9. 4 scallions, minced
10. Kosher salt, to taste
11. 2 lb. medium cooked shrimp, peeled and deveined, tails removed, chilled
12. 1/2 head iceberg lettuce, thinly sliced
Instructions:
1. Stir mayonnaise, mustard, wine, juice, horseradish, parsley, cayenne, pepper, scallions, and salt in a bowl; stir in shrimp.
2. Divide lettuce between plates; top with shrimp and garnish with parsley.
Notes: Inspired by a rémoulade served in New Orleans' Galatoire's, this white, mayonnaise-y blend of Creole mustard, horseradish, cayenne, and white pepper is rooted in the classic French recipe.
Author: Keith Pandolfi

Title: The Best General Tso's Chicken Recipe
Ingredients:
1. 1 1/2 pounds boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch chunks
2. 1 tablespoon soy sauce
3. 1 tablespoon vodka
4. 1 large egg white
5. 1/2 cup cornstarch
6. 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
7. 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
8. 1/4 cup water
9. 1 teaspoon kosher salt
10. 1/4 cup vegetable oil
11. 4 to 6 dried whole red chiles
12. 2 tablespoons minced fresh ginger
13. 2 tablespoons minced fresh garlic
14. 1/2 cup chicken stock
15. 1/4 cup soy sauce
16. 1/4 cup rice vinegar
17. 1/4 cup sugar
18. 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil
19. 2 teaspoons cornstarch mixed with 2 teaspoons water
20. 2 scallions, white and light green parts only, thinly sliced
Instructions:
1. 1. Combine chicken, soy sauce, vodka, and egg white in a large bowl and mix until chicken is well coated.
2. 2. In a separate bowl, combine cornstarch, flour, baking powder, and water to form a batter.
3. 3. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. 4. Dip chicken pieces in the batter and fry in batches until golden brown and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
5. 5. In the same wok, add dried chiles, ginger, and garlic and cook until fragrant.
6. 6. Add chicken stock, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and sesame oil. Bring to a boil.
7. 7. Add cornstarch mixture and cook until sauce thickens.
8. 8. Add the fried chicken and toss to coat with the sauce.
9. 9. Garnish with scallions before serving.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 45 minutes
Notes: For extra heat, add more dried chiles to the sauce.
Author: J. Kenji López-Alt

Title: Francis Mallmann’s Chimichurri
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup water
2. 1 tbsp. coarse salt
3. 1 head garlic, separated into cloves and peeled
4. 1 cup packed fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves
5. 1 cup fresh oregano leaves
6. 2 tsp. crushed red-pepper flakes
7. 1/4 cup red-wine vinegar
8. 1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil
Instructions:
1. Bring the water to a boil in a small saucepan. Add the salt, and stir until it dissolves. Remove from heat, and allow to cool.
2. Mince the garlic very finely, and put in a medium bowl. Mince the parsley and oregano, and add to the garlic, along with the red-pepper flakes.
3. Whisk in the red-wine vinegar, then the olive oil. Whisk in the salted water.
4. Transfer to a jar with a tight-fitting lid, and keep in the refrigerator. Let the flavors mingle for at least a day, and serve with grilled meats.
Yield: The sauce can be kept refrigerated for up to 3 weeks.
Notes: Adapted from Seven Fires: Grilling the Argentine Way, by Francis Mallmann with Peter Kaminsky; Artisan, 2009.
Author: Francis Mallmann

Notes: Failed to fetch recipe due to a connection error.

Title: Almost-Instant Soft-Serve
Ingredients:
1. 1 1/2 pounds frozen strawberries, mangoes, or blueberries
2. 3/4 cup sweetened condensed milk
3. 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
4. Kosher salt
Instructions:
1. In a food processor, pulse the fruit with the sweetened condensed milk, vanilla, and a generous pinch of salt until the fruit is finely chopped.
2. Puree until smooth, 2 to 3 minutes; scrape down the side of the bowl as needed.
3. Serve soft or transfer to a metal baking pan, cover and freeze until just firm.
Time: Total: 15 mins
Notes: The soft-serve can be frozen for up to 3 days. Let stand at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving.
Author: Justin Chapple

Title: Pappa al Pomodoro
Ingredients:
1. 2 pounds tomatoes, cored, quartered
2. 2 garlic cloves, smashed
3. ½ teaspoon fennel seeds
4. 6 tablespoons olive oil, divided, plus more
5. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
6. 4 sprigs basil
7. ¼ loaf country-style bread, crust removed, torn into 2" pieces (about 1 ½ cups), divided
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°. Toss tomatoes, garlic, fennel seeds, and 3 Tbsp. oil in a large baking dish; season with salt and pepper. Roast, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes are browned and the juices thicken, 30–35 minutes.
2. Transfer tomato mixture to a large sauce-pan; add basil, 1 cup torn bread, and 4 cups water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally and breaking up bread and tomatoes a little, until bread is softened and soup has thickened slightly, 8–10 minutes; season with salt and pepper.
3. Meanwhile, tear remaining ½ cup bread into smaller pieces; toss on a baking sheet with 3 Tbsp. oil; season with salt. Toast, tossing often, until crisp, 5–8 minutes.
4. Serve soup topped with toasted bread and drizzled with more oil.
Time: Total: 1 hour
Author: Alison Roman

Title: Wedding Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 10 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped
2. 4 serrano chiles, with seeds, thinly sliced
3. 1 3½” piece ginger, peeled, finely grated
4. 1 cup olive oil
5. ½ cup reduced-sodium soy sauce
6. ¼ cup apple cider vinegar
7. ¼ cup sugar
8. 3 tablespoons fish sauce (such as nam pla or nuoc nam)
9. 1 tablespoon kosher salt
10. 2 lemons, sliced, plus wedges for serving
11. 6 sprigs thyme
12. 8 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (about 3½ lb.)
Instructions:
1. Whisk garlic, chiles, ginger, oil, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, fish sauce, and salt in a medium bowl until sugar and salt dissolve. Add lemon slices and thyme. Place marinade and chicken in a large resealable plastic bag. Turn to coat, and chill at least 12 hours.
2. Heat oven to 450°. Remove chicken from marinade, shaking off excess, and place, skin side up, on a rimmed baking sheet. Roast until cooked through and an instant-read thermometer inserted in the thickest part of the thigh registers 165°, 25–30 minutes. Serve chicken with lemon wedges for squeezing over.
3. DO AHEAD: Chicken can be marinated 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.
Time:
Prep: 12 hours plus 45 minutes
Cook: 25–30 minutes
Total: 12 hours plus 45 minutes
Notes: Chicken can be marinated 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.
Author: Naomi Pomeroy, Expatriate, Portland, OR

Title: Three Onion Dip
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons plus 1 tsp. vegetable oil
2. 2 medium onions, thinly sliced
3. 2 anchovy fillets packed in oil, drained
4. 1 teaspoon mushroom powder (optional)
5. 4 garlic cloves, finely chopped, divided
6. 4 scallions, green and white parts separated
7. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
8. ⅓ cup mayonnaise
9. ⅓ cup plain whole-milk Greek yogurt
10. ⅓ cup sour cream
11. 1 tablespoon chopped fresh chives
12. 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
13. Potato chips (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a medium skillet over medium-low heat. Add onions, anchovies, mushroom powder, if using, and 1 Tbsp. garlic. Cook, stirring often, until onions are golden brown and softened, 40–45 minutes. If onions start to burn or stick, reduce heat and add water a splash at a time, scraping up browned bits. Let cool, then finely chop.
2. Meanwhile, heat a dry medium skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high heat. Toss scallion whites with remaining 1 tsp. oil; season with salt and pepper. Cook, turning occasionally, until charred, about 5 minutes. Let cool; finely chop. Thinly slice greens.
3. Mix onion mixture, scallion whites and greens, mayonnaise, yogurt, sour cream, chives, Worcestershire, and remaining garlic in a medium bowl; season with salt and pepper. Serve with chips.
Time: Cook: 45 minutes
Author: Alden & Harlow, Cambridge, MA

Title: Marinated Tofu with Peanuts and Charred Bean Sprouts
Ingredients:
1. 2 14-oz. packages firm tofu, drained, sliced ½” thick
2. 1 jalapeño, with seeds, thinly sliced
3. ½ cup reduced-sodium soy sauce
4. 2 tablespoons light brown sugar
5. 2 teaspoons grated peeled ginger
6. 2 teaspoons vegetable oil
7. 2 cups bean sprouts, divided
8. Kosher salt
9. Steamed white rice (for serving)
10. 6 scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal
11. ½ cup chopped salted, roasted peanuts
12. ¼ cup fresh mint leaves
13. Lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Place tofu on a baking sheet lined with several layers of paper towels; place several layers of paper towels on top and press gently to squeeze out liquid. Cut tofu into ¾”-wide pieces and place in a baking dish.
2. Whisk jalapeño, soy sauce, brown sugar, and ginger in a small bowl, pour over tofu, and toss to coat. Let sit at least 30 minutes.
3. Just before serving, heat oil in a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add 1 cup bean sprouts and cook, undisturbed, until charred, about 3 minutes; season with salt.
4. Spoon tofu over rice and top with charred and raw bean sprouts, scallions, peanuts, and mint. Serve with lime wedges.
5. Tofu can be marinated 1 hour ahead.
Time: Total: 50 minutes
Notes: Tofu can be marinated 1 hour ahead.
Author: Peter Serpico

Title: Arepas
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups arepa flour (precooked cornmeal)
2. 2 teaspoons kosher salt
3. 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
4. Desired fillings (such as shredded cooked chicken or pork, stewed black beans with cheese and lime, corn salad with onion and fresh herbs; for serving)
5. Lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Combine arepa flour and salt in a medium bowl. Make a well in the center and add 2½ cups warm water. Using a wooden spoon, gradually incorporate dry ingredients, stirring until no dry lumps remain. Let rest 5 minutes to hydrate.
2. Knead dough a few times in bowl, then divide into 8 pieces. Roll each piece on work surface into a ball, then gently flatten to about ½” thick.
3. Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add 4 arepas, cover, and cook until golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Uncover, flip, and cook (keep uncovered) until other side is golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Transfer arepas to a wire rack. Repeat with remaining 1 Tbsp. oil and dough.
4. Split arepas and stuff with desired fillings; serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.
Time: Total: 40 minutes
Notes: Arepa flour is precooked corn flour, not to be confused with masa harina. Sometimes sold as masarepa or harina precocida, it can be found in Latin markets and some supermarkets.
Author: Ryan Pera

Title: Rigatoni and Cauliflower al Forno
Ingredients:
1. 1 large head of cauliflower, cut into florets
2. 3 tablespoons olive oil
3. Salt and pepper to taste
4. 1 pound rigatoni
5. 2 cups marinara sauce
6. 1 cup grated Parmesan cheese
7. 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
8. 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
9. 2 cloves garlic, minced
10. 1 teaspoon chopped fresh rosemary
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Toss the cauliflower florets with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and pepper.
3. Spread the cauliflower on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes until golden and tender.
4. Cook the rigatoni according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
5. In a large bowl, combine the roasted cauliflower, cooked rigatoni, marinara sauce, half of the Parmesan cheese, mozzarella cheese, parsley, garlic, and rosemary.
6. Transfer the mixture to a greased baking dish.
7. Sprinkle the remaining Parmesan cheese over the top.
8. Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
9. Let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 45 minutes
Total: 1 hour
Notes: This dish can be made ahead and reheated before serving.
Author: David Tanis

Title: Waffle Iron Hash Browns
Ingredients:
1. 2 russet (baking) potatoes, about 10 ounces each, peeled and shredded
2. 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt
3. 3/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
4. 2 teaspoons unsalted butter
Instructions:
1. Preheat waffle iron. If it has temperature controls, set it to medium.
2. Squeeze shredded potato with a towel until it’s as dry as possible.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine shredded potato, salt, and pepper.
4. Using a silicone brush or paper towel, carefully grease both sides of waffle iron with butter.
5. Pile shredded potatoes into waffle iron, over-stuffing it a bit, and close lid. Cook for 2 minutes, then press down on lid to further compress potatoes.
6. Continue cooking potatoes for 5 minutes longer, then begin checking them: They are ready when potatoes are golden brown all over, about 1 to 2 minutes longer.
7. Serve hot with eggs or any other breakfast foods.
Time:
Prep: 10 mins
Cook: 30 mins
Total: 40 mins
Notes: The cooking time will depend on how finely your potatoes are shredded and the temperature of your waffle iron.
Author: Daniel Shumski

Title: Pulled Chicken With Red Rice
Ingredients:
1. 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil
2. 2 medium tomatoes, finely chopped
3. 1 large yellow onion, finely chopped
4. 1 large carrot, peeled and finely chopped
5. 1 tablespoon kosher salt
6. 8 cups water
7. 4 cilantro stems
8. 1 Thai bird chile
9. 2 skinless bone-in chicken breasts
10. 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil
11. 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh sage
12. 1 tablespoon finely chopped fresh thyme
13. 1 medium yellow onion, finely chopped
14. ¼ teaspoon kosher salt
15. 2 cups Wehani red rice (or long-grain brown rice)
16. ½ cup finely chopped fresh cilantro
Instructions:
1. Poach the chicken: In a large pot set over medium-high heat, add the olive oil, tomatoes, onion, carrot and salt. Reduce the heat to medium and cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture turns thick and pasty, about 10 minutes (don't let it brown). Add the water, cilantro stems and chile. Add the chicken breasts, rounded side up—they should just be barely covered with water. Reduce the heat to medium-low so the liquid is steaming but not bubbling (reduce the heat even more if necessary). Cook for 45 minutes and then turn off the heat (the chicken can be refrigerated overnight in the broth for a more full-flavored dish, or it can be strained right away).
2. Remove the chicken from the broth and set aside to cool, then shred the chicken. Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.
3. Make the rice: Add the olive oil to a large skillet set over medium-high heat. Once it shimmers, add the sage and thyme and let it sizzle for 10 seconds. Add the onion and salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is soft, about 4 minutes. Stir in the rice and add 4 cups of the broth (save the rest for another time). Bring to a simmer, reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet and cook until the rice is tender, 50 to 55 minutes.
4. Stir in the chicken and turn off the heat. Place a paper towel over the skillet and cover. Set aside for 10 minutes. Remove the paper towel and serve the rice sprinkled with the cilantro.
Time:
Prep: 25 minutes
Cook: 1 hour 50 minutes
Total: 2 hours 15 minutes
Notes: The chicken can be refrigerated overnight in the broth for a more full-flavored dish.
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: Pork and Squash Stew with Chiles
Ingredients:
1. 3 pounds boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt), cut into 2” pieces
2. 1 tablespoon ground coriander
3. 10 cloves garlic finely chopped, divided
4. 1 tablespoon kosher salt, plus more
5. Freshly ground black pepper
6. ½ cup raw shelled pumpkin seeds
7. 6 dried New Mexico or guajillo chiles
8. 2 chiles de árbol or ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
9. 2 large yellow onions, cut into ⅛”-thick wedges, divided
10. 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
11. 4 sprigs oregano
12. ½ kabocha squash (about 1 lb.), peeled, seeds removed, cut into 1” pieces
13. 1 delicata squash, seeds removed, cut into ½”-thick slices
14. ½ small red onion, thinly sliced
15. ¼ cup fresh lime juice
16. Cilantro sprigs (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Combine pork, coriander, half of garlic, and 1 Tbsp. salt in a large bowl; season with pepper and toss. Cover; chill at least 4 hours.
2. Preheat oven to 350°. Toast pumpkin seeds on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing occasionally, until golden, about 5 minutes; set aside.
3. Toast chiles on a clean baking sheet until slightly darkened, about 5 minutes. Let cool slightly, then remove stems, and seeds, if you prefer less heat. Place chiles, half of yellow onion, remaining garlic, and 1 cup hot water in a blender; let sit 10 minutes to soften chiles. Blend until smooth; set chile purée aside.
4. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Working in batches, cook pork, turning occasionally, until browned, 8–10 minutes; transfer to a plate.
5. Pour off fat from pot. Cook chile purée in pot over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until reduced by half, 8–10 minutes. Add pork, oregano, remaining yellow onion, and 10 cups water to pot; season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer, partially covered, skimming occasionally, until pork is very tender, 3–3½ hours.
6. Add squash to stew and cook, uncovered, until pork is falling apart and squash is soft, 30–35 minutes; season with salt and pepper.
7. Toss red onion and lime juice in a small bowl; let sit, tossing occasionally, 30 minutes.
8. Serve stew with red onion, cilantro, and reserved pumpkin seeds.
9. Pork can be marinated 2 days ahead; keep chilled. Stew can be made 3 days ahead; let cool, then cover and chill.
Time:
Prep: 4 hours (including chilling time)
Cook: 4–4½ hours
Total: 8–8½ hours
Notes: Pork can be marinated 2 days ahead; keep chilled. Stew can be made 3 days ahead; let cool, then cover and chill.
Author: Alison Roman

Title: Pa Amb Tomàquet (Catalan Tomato Bread)
Ingredients:
1. 8 large slices country-style bread, about 1/2 inch thick
2. 4 garlic cloves (optional)
3. 2 very ripe tomatoes, halved crosswise
4. Spanish extra-virgin olive oil
5. Coarse sea salt
6. Freshly ground pepper
Instructions:
1. Light a grill. When the fire is medium hot, grill the bread for 2 minutes per side, or until nicely browned and crisp.
2. Give each diner a clove of garlic and half of a tomato. Each diner should rub the grilled slices with the garlic, then with the cut tomato. (The idea is to apply a thin film of tomato on the bread.) Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with salt and pepper and eat.
Time:
Prep: 10 mins
Cook: 5 mins
Total: 15 mins
Notes: Choose a light-bodied Trappist ale, such as Orval from Belgium, to balance the sweet tomato flavor.
Author: Steven Raichlen
