Title: Paleo Coconut Flour Chicken Tenders (Low Carb, Keto)
Ingredients:
1. Chicken tenderloins
2. Coconut flour
3. Parmesan cheese or nutritional yeast
4. Spices (to taste)
5. Olive oil
Instructions:
1. Blend spices with coconut flour and either Parmesan cheese or nutritional yeast.
2. Dip each chicken tenderloin into olive oil.
3. Coat each tenderloin with the coconut flour mixture.
4. Place the coated tenderloins on a rack in a baking pan.
5. Bake at 400°F for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway through.
6. For extra crispness, place tenders about 6 inches from the broiler on high for about 1 minute each side.
Time:
Prep: 5 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 35 minutes
Notes: For a Paleo version, replace Parmesan cheese with nutritional yeast. Using a rack helps the coating crisp up better.
Author: Lisa MarcAurele

Title: Sara Jenkins' Italian Porchetta
Ingredients:
1. 1 4-pound boneless pork shoulder with the fat cap and skin still on or a 4-pound pork shoulder and a separate pork belly
2. 20 fresh sage leaves
3. 3 leafy sprigs fresh thyme, leaves removed
4. 3 leafy sprigs rosemary, stemmed
5. 2 garlic cloves, peeled and roughly chopped
6. 2 tablespoons fennel pollen (swap out for dried fennel if you can't get this)
7. 1 ½ teaspoons medium coarse sea salt
8. 1 ½ teaspoons freshly cracked black pepper
9. 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
Instructions:
1. Score the skin of the pork shoulder.
2. Stab the pork all over with a knife and insert a mixture of fresh herbs in the slits.
3. Tie the roast up.
4. Slow roast it for a few hours, basting it with white wine.
5. Serve as a sandwich on buns, or serve with cooked greens and crispy potatoes.
Time: Cook: a few hours
Notes: If you can't find fennel pollen, swap out for dried fennel. Optional: Add a couple of tablespoons of lemon zest to the herbs for added flavor.
Author: Sara Jenkins

Title: Frittata With Kasha, Leeks and Spinach
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup kasha
2. Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3. 2 tablespoons olive oil
4. 2 cups sliced leeks, white and light green parts only
5. 2 cups chopped fresh spinach
6. 6 eggs
7. 1/4 cup milk or cream
8. 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Cook the kasha in a small saucepan according to package instructions. Set aside.
3. Heat the olive oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium heat. Add the leeks and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
4. Add the spinach to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
5. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Stir the cooked kasha into the egg mixture.
7. Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables in the skillet. Cook on the stovetop until the edges begin to set, about 5 minutes.
8. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake until the frittata is set and the top is golden brown, about 15 minutes.
9. Let cool slightly before slicing and serving.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 25 minutes
Total: 40 minutes
Notes: This frittata is more vegetable than egg, making it a healthy and hearty meal.
Author: Mark Bittman

Title: Ultimate Vegan Ramen With Miso Broth
Ingredients:
1. Charred vegetables
2. Fresh vegetables
3. Dried mushrooms
4. Fresh mushrooms
5. Soy-based tare
6. Shiitake mushrooms
7. Aromatics
8. Soy sauce
9. Mirin
10. Roasted sweet potatoes
11. Pureed roasted garlic
12. Charred eggplant
13. Mushroom-scallion oil
Instructions:
1. Prepare a mix of charred and fresh vegetables along with dried and fresh mushrooms for the broth.
2. Create a soy-based tare by simmering shiitake mushrooms and aromatics in soy sauce and mirin.
3. Roast sweet potatoes and puree them with roasted garlic to thicken the broth.
4. Char eggplant to add smokiness.
5. Prepare mushroom-scallion oil to add fat and flavor to the dish.
6. Assemble the ramen by adding the tare to the bowl, followed by the broth, and then the toppings.
7. Drizzle with mushroom-scallion oil before serving.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 4 hours
Total: 4 hours 30 minutes
Notes: Set aside at least half a day for this project as it involves many moving parts and elements.
Author: J. Kenji López-Alt

Title: Kimchi Fried Rice Waffles
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup kimchi chopped into bits (use cabbage kimchi centers and not the leaves)
2. 1/4 cup kimchi juice
3. 3 cloves of garlic, minced
4. 1/2 small onion, chopped finely
5. 1/2 jalapeño, chopped finely
6. 1-2 tbsp gochujang/Korean chili paste (according to taste)
7. 3 tsp sesame oil
8. 2-3 cups day-old rice, cold
9. sesame seed, dried nori, green onion (optional)
10. fried egg
Instructions:
1. Fry garlic until fragrant, add onions and jalapeño and let it sweat a little (~2 mins).
2. Add kimchi and saute until softened (2-3 mins). Add gochujang and mix until homogenous.
3. Add cold rice and break it up thoroughly. Drizzle in kimchi juice.
4. Saute and toss frequently for 5 mins. Adding sesame oil and a handful of chopped green onion at the last minute (you want your rice to be a little more dry than usual - for waffling).
5. Let it cool or stick it in the fridge for a while (skipping this step is fine but waffles are more to fall apart).
6. Preheat waffle machine. I don't have fancy one so it is all 1 heat setting. Stuff the machine with more kimchi fried rice waffle 'batter' than usual. You will squish it all down and the outside will crisp up. I kept it in my waffle maker for 3-4 minutes.
7. Enjoy with a fried egg on top!
Author: Miss Hangrypants

Title: Croquembouche
Ingredients:
1. ½ cup whole milk
2. 6 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into pieces
3. 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
4. ½ teaspoon kosher salt
5. 1 cup all-purpose flour
6. 6 large eggs, room temperature
7. 2 cups whole milk
8. 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into pieces
9. ½ teaspoon kosher salt
10. ½ vanilla bean, split lengthwise
11. 5 large egg yolks
12. ½ cup granulated sugar
13. ¼ cup cornstarch
14. Powdered sugar (for dusting)
15. 2½ cups granulated sugar
16. 2 tablespoons light corn syrup
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425˚. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper. Heat milk, butter, granulated sugar, salt, and ½ cup water in a medium saucepan over medium, stirring occasionally to melt butter. As soon as butter is melted, add flour all at once and stir with a wooden spoon, carefully at first to incorporate, then vigorously, until dough forms a single mass. Continue stirring, slapping dough against sides of saucepan with spoon, until dough leaves a thick film on bottom of pan and pulls away from sides, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a medium bowl and let rest 3 minutes.
2. Using a wooden spoon, mix 5 eggs into dough one at a time, making sure to completely incorporate each egg before adding the next. Dough will look broken when eggs are added, but will come back together as you stir. The final texture should be smooth, glossy, and somewhat stretchy.
3. Fit pastry bag with round tip. Spoon dough into bag. Holding bag at an angle so the tip is just touching prepared baking sheet on one side, gently start to squeeze out dough. Without moving the tip, pipe dough until you have a 1½" round. Repeat, staggering rounds and spacing 2" apart.
4. Beat remaining egg in a small bowl until no streaks remain and lightly brush tops of dough. Bake 15 minutes, then lower heat to 350˚. Continue to bake until puffs are deep golden brown all over and sound hollow when tapped on the bottom, 10–15 minutes.
5. Place milk, butter, and salt in a medium saucepan, then scrape in vanilla seeds and add pod. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
6. Meanwhile, whisk egg yolks and granulated sugar in a medium bowl until very pale and light, about 3 minutes. Add cornstarch, whisking until no powdery dry spots remain. Whisking constantly, gradually add hot milk mixture to egg mixture. Transfer back to saucepan and cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until mixture is thick and holds whisk marks, about 2 minutes.
7. Strain custard through a fine-mesh sieve into a medium bowl. Cover with plastic, pressing directly onto surface; chill until cold, at least 2 hours.
8. Fit pastry bag with star tip. Transfer chilled pastry cream to bag. Working one at a time, poke a hole into the bottom of each puff with a paring knife. Gently pipe in pastry cream to fill.
9. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper and trace an 8" circle on parchment; turn it over.
10. Fill a large bowl with ice water. Bring sugar, corn syrup, and ½ cup water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring to dissolve sugar. Boil, brushing down sides with a damp pastry brush and swirling pan occasionally, until caramel turns a light amber color. Set bottom of saucepan in ice water to stop the cooking and firm caramel slightly.
11. Carefully dip tops of filled puffs in caramel and place, caramel side up, on an unlined rimmed baking sheet; let sit until caramel is set.
Notes: Puffs will begin to get soggy as soon as they are filled, so wait until just before serving to add pastry cream.
Author: Bon Appétit

Title: Green Strata With Goat Cheese and Herbs
Ingredients:
1. 1 tablespoon olive oil
2. 1 onion, thinly sliced
3. Salt and pepper
4. 8 ounces mixed greens (spinach, chard, kale), washed and roughly chopped
5. 8 large eggs
6. 2 cups milk
7. 1 teaspoon chopped thyme
8. 1 teaspoon chopped rosemary
9. 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg
10. 6 ounces goat cheese, crumbled
11. 6 cups cubed day-old bread
Instructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and cook until soft, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Add the greens and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, thyme, rosemary, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
5. Add the cooked greens and onions, goat cheese, and bread cubes to the egg mixture. Stir to combine.
6. Pour the mixture into a greased 9x13-inch baking dish.
7. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
8. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
9. Bake the strata, uncovered, until puffed and golden, about 45 to 50 minutes.
10. Let cool slightly before serving.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 50 minutes
Total: 3 hours (including chilling time)
Notes: This dish can be prepared a day in advance and baked just before serving.
Author: David Tanis

Title: BA's Best Cheesecake
Ingredients:
1. 9 oz. graham crackers, chocolate wafer cookies, vanilla wafer cookies, or gingersnap cookies
2. 1 Tbsp. raw sugar
3. 1/4 tsp. kosher salt
4. 1/2 cup (1 stick; 4 oz.) unsalted butter, melted, slightly cooled
5. 20 oz. cream cheese (2½ 8-oz. bars), room temperature
6. 3/4 cup granulated sugar
7. 1/4 tsp. kosher salt
8. 1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise, or 1 Tbsp. vanilla extract
9. 2/3 cup sour cream, room temperature
10. 1/4 tsp. finely grated lemon zest
11. 2 large eggs, room temperature
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Pulse 9 oz. graham crackers, chocolate wafer cookies, vanilla wafer cookies, or gingersnap cookies in the large bowl of a food processor until fine crumbs form (you should have about 2 cups). Add 1 Tbsp. raw sugar and 1/4 tsp. kosher salt and pulse just to combine. Add 1/2 cup (4 oz.) melted butter and pulse until mixture is the consistency of wet sand.
2. Transfer mixture to springform pan and, using a measuring cup, press crumbs firmly onto bottom and 2" up sides of pan. Bake crust until fragrant and edges just start to take on color, 12–15 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool.
3. Preheat oven to 325°F. Pulse 20 oz. cream cheese (2½ 8-oz. bars), room temperature, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, and 1/4 tsp. kosher salt in the large bowl of a food processor (no need to clean it first), occasionally scraping down sides of the bowl, until smooth. Scrape in the seeds from 1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise (save pod for another use), or add 1 Tbsp. vanilla extract. Add 2/3 cup sour cream, room temperature, and 1/4 tsp. finely grated lemon zest and pulse until smooth. Add 2 large eggs, room temperature, and pulse to combine. Scrape cheesecake batter into cooled crust and bake until edges are set but center is still wobbly, 35–40 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool. Chill until firm, at least 2 hours.
Time:
Cook: 47-55 minutes
Total: 3 hours (includes cooling)
Notes: Cheesecake can be made 3 days ahead. Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before serving.
Author: Dawn Perry

Title: Clam and Cod Chowder
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons olive oil
2. 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
3. 1½ pounds small Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled, halved, quartered if large
4. 2 large leeks, white and pale-green parts only, finely chopped
5. 4 celery stalks, finely chopped
6. 4 sprigs thyme, plus leaves for serving
7. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
8. ⅓ cup dry white wine
9. 4 cups fish stock or bottled clam juice
10. 2 cups heavy cream
11. 4 pounds littleneck clams, scrubbed
12. 2 pounds skinless cod or haddock, cut into large pieces
13. Hot sauce and lemon wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Heat oil and butter in a large heavy pot over medium. Add potatoes, leeks, celery, and thyme sprigs; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until leeks are tender, about 5 minutes. Add wine, bring to a boil, and cook until reduced by half, about 2 minutes. Add stock and cream, season with salt and pepper, and bring to a gentle simmer (if using clam juice, taste before adding salt, it’s very briny). Cook until potatoes are starting to fall apart (their starch will help give body to the soup), 50–60 minutes.
2. Add clams to pot and stir to coat. Cover and simmer until clams are just beginning to open, 10–12 minutes. Season cod with salt and pepper and add to pot. Reduce heat to low and cover. Let fish gently poach until cooked through, about 5 minutes (simmering too hard can make fish tough). Season with salt and pepper, if needed, and serve topped with thyme leaves, with hot sauce and lemon wedges.
Time: Cook: 60-77 minutes
Notes: Weeknight chowder? Use clam juice. Weekend? It’s worth making fish stock from scratch.
Author: Alison Roman

Title: BA's Best Cheesecake
Ingredients:
1. 9 oz. graham crackers, chocolate wafer cookies, vanilla wafer cookies, or gingersnap cookies
2. 1 Tbsp. raw sugar
3. 1/4 tsp. kosher salt
4. 1/2 cup (1 stick; 4 oz.) unsalted butter, melted, slightly cooled
5. 20 oz. cream cheese (2½ 8-oz. bars), room temperature
6. 3/4 cup granulated sugar
7. 1/4 tsp. kosher salt
8. 1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise, or 1 Tbsp. vanilla extract
9. 2/3 cup sour cream, room temperature
10. 1/4 tsp. finely grated lemon zest
11. 2 large eggs, room temperature
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°. Pulse 9 oz. graham crackers, chocolate wafer cookies, vanilla wafer cookies, or gingersnap cookies in the large bowl of a food processor until fine crumbs form (you should have about 2 cups). Add 1 Tbsp. raw sugar and 1/4 tsp. kosher salt and pulse just to combine. Add 1/2 cup (4 oz.) melted butter and pulse until mixture is the consistency of wet sand.
2. Transfer mixture to springform pan and, using a measuring cup, press crumbs firmly onto bottom and 2" up sides of pan. Bake crust until fragrant and edges just start to take on color, 12–15 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool.
3. Preheat oven to 325°. Pulse 20 oz. cream cheese (2½ 8-oz. bars), room temperature, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, and 1/4 tsp. kosher salt in the large bowl of a food processor (no need to clean it first), occasionally scraping down sides of the bowl, until smooth. Scrape in the seeds from 1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise (save pod for another use), or add 1 Tbsp. vanilla extract. Add 2/3 cup sour cream, room temperature and 1/4 tsp. finely grated lemon zest and pulse until smooth. Add 2 large eggs, room temperature and pulse to combine. Scrape cheesecake batter into cooled crust and bake until edges are set but center is still wobbly, 35–40 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool. Chill until firm, at least 2 hours.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: 35–40 minutes
Total: 3 hours (includes cooling)
Notes: Cheesecake can be made 3 days ahead. Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before serving.
Author: Dawn Perry

Title: Vadouvan-Roasted Cauliflower with Harissa Chickpea Curry
Ingredients:
1. 2 Fresno chiles, thinly sliced
2. ¼ cup Champagne vinegar
3. ¼ teaspoon sugar
4. 1½ teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more
5. 1 head of cauliflower, cut into small florets
6. ¼ cup olive oil
7. 2 tablespoons vadouvan or curry powder
8. 2 shallots, thinly sliced
9. ¾ cup vegetable oil, divided
10. 1 15.5-ounce can chickpeas, rinsed, patted dry
11. 1 medium onion, chopped
12. 1 1-inch piece ginger, peeled, finely grated
13. 1 garlic clove, finely grated
14. 1 teaspoon grated lemongrass
15. 2 teaspoons tomato paste
16. 1 teaspoon harissa paste
17. 1 13.5-ounce can unsweetened coconut milk
18. 1 tablespoon chopped cilantro stems, plus sprigs for serving
19. Steamed basmati rice (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Combine chiles, vinegar, sugar, ½ tsp. salt, and ¼ cup water in a small bowl. Let sit at room temperature until ready to use.
2. Preheat oven to 450°. Toss cauliflower with olive oil, vadouvan, and 1 tsp. salt on a rimmed baking sheet. Roast, tossing occasionally, until cauliflower is tender and browned, 30–35 minutes. Transfer to a paper towels to drain.
3. Meanwhile, cook shallots in ½ cup vegetable oil in a small saucepan over medium-high, stirring occasionally, until golden brown and crisp, 8–10 minutes. Transfer to paper towels with a slotted spoon; season with salt.
4. Heat remaining ¼ cup vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium. Add onion and cook, stirring often, until tender, 5–7 minutes. Add ginger, garlic, and lemongrass and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add tomato paste and harissa paste and cook, stirring constantly, until slightly darkened in color, about 2 minutes.
5. Whisk in coconut milk, bring to a boil, and cook, whisking occasionally, until curry thickens to the consistency of heavy cream, 5–7 minutes. Add chickpeas and ¼ cup water and cook, stirring occasionally, until mixture returns to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in cilantro stems; season with salt.
6. Serve curry over rice topped with drained pickled chiles, roasted cauliflower, fried shallots, and cilantro sprigs.
Notes: Faster! Faster! Skip the pickling and just top the curry with sliced fresh Fresno chiles. Or leave them out altogether if you’re spice-averse.
Author: Rick Martinez

Title: Caramelized Tomato Tarte Tatin
Ingredients:
1. 1 sheet of puff pastry, thawed
2. 2 tablespoons olive oil
3. 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar
4. 1 tablespoon sugar
5. Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
6. 1 1/2 pounds cherry tomatoes
7. 1/2 cup ricotta cheese
8. 1/4 cup pesto
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. In a 10-inch ovenproof skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
3. Add balsamic vinegar, sugar, salt, and pepper, stirring until sugar dissolves.
4. Add cherry tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to caramelize, about 10 minutes.
5. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
6. Roll out puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to smooth out any creases.
7. Place puff pastry over the tomatoes, tucking in the edges.
8. Bake in the preheated oven until the pastry is golden brown, about 25-30 minutes.
9. Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes.
10. Carefully invert the tart onto a serving plate.
11. Serve warm with dollops of ricotta and drizzles of pesto.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 40 minutes
Total: 55 minutes
Notes: This tart is best served warm, but can also be enjoyed at room temperature.
Author: Alexa Weibel

Title: Frisée-Lardon Salad
Ingredients:
1. 1 tablespoon distilled white vinegar
2. 4 large eggs
3. 2 tablespoons olive oil
4. 4 ounces slab bacon, cut into 1x¼-inch pieces
5. 1 medium shallot, finely chopped
6. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
7. ½ cup red wine vinegar
8. 2 large heads of frisée, torn into bite-size pieces
9. Fleur de sel
10. 2 tablespoons 1½-inch pieces fresh chives
Instructions:
1. Pour water into a large saucepan to a depth of 2" and bring to a boil. Reduce heat so water is at a gentle simmer and add white vinegar (it helps the egg whites stay compact). Crack an egg into a small bowl, then gently slide it into the water. Repeat with remaining eggs, waiting until the whites are starting to set before adding the next one (about 30 seconds apart). Cook eggs until whites are just set but yolks are still runny, about 3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer eggs to paper towels as they finish cooking.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium. Add bacon and cook, stirring occasionally, until most of the fat has rendered and bacon is starting to brown, 5–8 minutes. Add shallot, season with kosher salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally, until shallot is translucent and softened but hasn’t taken on any color, about 5 minutes. Add red wine vinegar. Bring to a boil and cook until reduced by three-quarters, 5–8 minutes. Taste bacon vinaigrette and adjust seasoning with kosher salt and pepper if needed.
3. Place frisée in a large bowl and drizzle warm bacon vinaigrette over top. Gently toss until frisée is evenly dressed and slightly wilted and season with fleur de sel and pepper.
4. Divide frisée salad among plates and carefully set an egg atop each. Season eggs with fleur de sel and pepper and scatter chives around.
5. Do Ahead: Eggs can be poached 4 hours ahead. Place in a bowl of ice water; cover and chill. Reheat in barely simmering water 1 minute before serving.
Notes: Eggs can be poached 4 hours ahead. Place in a bowl of ice water; cover and chill. Reheat in barely simmering water 1 minute before serving.

Title: Linguine with Green Olive Sauce and Zesty Breadcrumbs
Ingredients:
1. Linguine
2. Green olives
3. Homemade breadcrumbs
4. Olive oil
5. Anchovies
6. Tomatoes (preferably in season)
Instructions:
1. Sauté homemade breadcrumbs quickly in olive oil.
2. Toss linguine with green olives, anchovies, and tomatoes.
3. Top with zesty breadcrumbs.
Notes: For the best results, make this sauce at the height of tomato season.

Title: Garam Masala Roasted Chicken with Bread and Butter Cabbage
Ingredients:
1. 1 whole chicken
2. 2 tablespoons garam masala
3. Salt to taste
4. Pepper to taste
5. 1 head of cabbage, sliced
6. 4 tablespoons butter
7. Bread, for serving
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Rub the chicken with garam masala, salt, and pepper.
3. Place the chicken in a roasting pan and roast for about 1 hour, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
4. While the chicken is roasting, melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
5. Add the sliced cabbage to the skillet and cook until tender, about 10 minutes.
6. Season the cabbage with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Serve the roasted chicken with the bread and butter cabbage on the side.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 1 hour 10 minutes
Total: 1 hour 25 minutes
Notes: This dish pairs well with a side of crusty bread.
Author: Nathan Legare

Title: Pork Shoulder with Salsa Verde
Ingredients:
1. 3 anchovy fillets
2. 1 garlic clove, peeled
3. 3/4 cup chopped fresh Italian parsley
4. 1/3 cup (lightly packed) chopped fresh celery leaves
5. 1 1/2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
6. 1 tablespoon finely grated lemon peel
7. 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar
8. 1 1/2 teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary
9. 1 1/2 teaspoons chopped fresh sage
10. 1/2 cup olive oil
11. 6 garlic cloves, minced
12. 2 tablespoons chopped fresh sage
13. 2 tablespoons chopped fresh rosemary
14. 1 tablespoon coarse kosher salt
15. 2 teaspoons freshly ground black pepper
16. 1 tablespoon olive oil
17. 1 8-pound whole bone-in heritage pork shoulder roast (Boston butt)
Instructions:
1. With processor running, drop anchovies and garlic through feed tube and finely chop. Scrape down sides of bowl. Add parsley, celery leaves, lemon juice, lemon peel, red wine vinegar, chopped rosemary, and chopped sage. Using on/off turns, process until almost smooth. With machine running, gradually add olive oil. Transfer salsa verde to bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
2. Position rack in lowest third of oven; preheat to 450°F. Mix garlic, sage, rosemary, coarse kosher salt, and freshly ground black pepper in small bowl. Brush oil all over pork, then rub spice mixture all over. Place pork on rack set in roasting pan. Roast 20 minutes. Reduce heat to 300°F and continue to roast until instant read thermometer inserted into center registers 185°F, about 6 1/2 hours. Remove pork from oven; tent with foil to keep warm. Let rest 15 minutes. Cut into 1/2-inch-thick pieces and serve with salsa verde alongside.
Time: Cook: 6 hours 50 minutes
Notes: Salsa verde can be made 1 day ahead. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate.

Title: Seared Mushrooms With Garlic and Thyme
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons olive oil
2. 12 ounces mushrooms (such as maitake, oyster, and/or king trumpet), cut into large pieces
3. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
4. 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into pieces
5. 2 sprigs thyme
6. 2 garlic cloves, crushed
Instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high until just beginning to smoke. Arrange mushrooms in skillet in a single layer and cook, undisturbed, until bottom side is golden brown, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, toss mushrooms, and continue to cook, tossing often and reducing heat as needed to avoid scorching, until golden brown all over, about 5 minutes more.
2. Reduce heat to medium and add butter, thyme sprigs, and garlic to skillet. Tip skillet toward you so butter pools at bottom edge. Spoon foaming butter over mushrooms until butter smells nutty, about 4 minutes. Remove mushrooms from skillet with a slotted spoon.
Time: Cook: 12 minutes
Notes: Our idea of magic mushrooms is crisp, golden (legal) substances with the power to lend a bass note of flavor to grain salads, turn a piece of ricotta toast into a meal, or stand alone as a savory side.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Slow Cooker Thai Chicken Soup
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons red curry paste
2. 2-12 ounce cans of coconut milk
3. Chicken Broth
4. Fish sauce
5. Brown sugar
6. Peanut butter
7. Chicken breasts
8. Red bell pepper
9. Onion
10. Fresh ginger
11. Frozen peas
12. Lime juice
13. Cooked white rice
14. Fresh cilantro
Instructions:
1. Add all the ingredients but the lime juice, cilantro, rice, and peas to your slow cooker.
2. Pop the lid on, turn the slow cooker to High, and let the Thai soup cook for 4 hours.
3. After 4 hours, stir in the peas and let the crockpot chicken soup cook for another 30 minutes.
4. Serve the Thai coconut chicken soup over white rice with a sprinkle of fresh cilantro on top.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 4 hours 30 minutes
Total: 4 hours 40 minutes
Notes: ['For the best flavor, use freshly grated ginger and freshly squeezed lime juice.', 'To avoid coconut milk and broth separation, use full-fat coconut milk.']

Title: Zahav’s Hummus ‘Tehina’
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup dried chickpeas
2. 2 teaspoons baking soda, divided
3. 1 1/2 cups basic tehina sauce (recipe follows)
4. 1 tablespoon salt
5. 1/4 teaspoon ground cumin
6. 1/4 teaspoon sweet paprika
7. 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
8. 1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley
Instructions:
1. Step 1: Place the chickpeas in a large bowl with 1 teaspoon of the baking soda and cover with water by at least 4 inches. Cover the bowl and soak the chickpeas overnight at room temperature.
2. Step 2: Drain and rinse the chickpeas, then transfer them to a large pot. Add the remaining teaspoon of baking soda and enough water to cover the chickpeas by 2 inches.
3. Step 3: Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer, skimming off any foam and skins that rise to the surface, until the chickpeas are very tender, 45 minutes to 1 hour.
4. Step 4: Drain the chickpeas, reserving 1 cup of the cooking liquid. Transfer the chickpeas to a food processor and add the tehina sauce, salt, cumin, and paprika.
5. Step 5: Process the mixture until very smooth, adding the reserved cooking liquid a tablespoon at a time if needed to achieve a creamy consistency.
6. Step 6: Transfer the hummus to a bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and sprinkle with parsley before serving.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 1 hour
Total: 1 hour 10 minutes
Notes: This hummus is best served fresh but can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Author: Reem Kassis

Title: Blistered Green Beans With Fried Shallots
Ingredients:
1. Green beans
2. Shallots
3. Salt
4. Extra virgin olive oil
5. Red pepper flakes
6. Chopped capers
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
2. Add green beans and let them cook without stirring for a few minutes to develop black spots.
3. Stir in shallots and continue to cook until the shallots are crispy.
4. Season with salt, red pepper flakes, and chopped capers.
5. Serve warm.

Title: Sautéed Collards and Cabbage with Gremolata
Ingredients:
1. ¾ cup parsley (finely chopped)
2. 1 ½ teaspoons minced garlic (plus 2 thinly sliced garlic cloves)
3. 1 tablespoon lemon zest (plus 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice finely grated)
4. ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil (plus 2 tablespoons)
5. Kosher salt
6. Black pepper
7. 4 shallots (3/4 cup halved and thinly sliced)
8. 1 ½ pounds green cabbage (9 cups cored and sliced 1/4 inch thick)
9. 1 ½ pounds collard greens (12 cups stems discarded, leaves sliced 1/4 inch thick)
10. ¾ teaspoon crushed red pepper
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the parsley, minced garlic, lemon zest, 3 tablespoons of the lemon juice and 6 tablespoons of the olive oil. Season with salt and black pepper and mix well.
2. In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of the olive oil. Add the shallots and sliced garlic and cook over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until light golden, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the green cabbage, collard greens and the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil and season with salt and black pepper. Cook over moderately high heat, stirring, until the collards and cabbage are wilted and crisp-tender, 7 to 8 minutes.
4. Stir in the crushed red pepper and the remaining 1 tablespoon of lemon juice. Transfer the greens to a platter, top with the gremolata and serve.
Time: Total: 45 mins
Notes: The gremolata can be made up to 3 hours ahead and kept covered at room temperature.
Author: Carla Hall

Title: French Onion Soup Recipe
Ingredients:
1. 2 pounds sweet white onions (such as Vidalia), halved and thinly sliced (2 large onions)
2. ¾ pound shallots, thinly sliced (8 to 10 small shallots)
3. 2 thyme sprigs
4. 1 garlic clove, smashed
5. 1 bay leaf
6. 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
7. ¾ teaspoon kosher salt, plus more to taste
8. 8 cups low-sodium beef broth
9. Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
10. 1 large baguette, cut into ½-inch-thick slices
11. 8 ounces Gruyère cheese, thinly sliced or grated
Instructions:
1. In a slow cooker, combine the onions, shallots, thyme, garlic, bay leaf and butter, and season with salt. Turn the slow cooker on high and cook until the onions are very soft and the garlic has disintegrated, 3 hours.
2. Add the beef broth, season with a pinch of black pepper and continue to cook on high for 2 hours more. Makes about 10 cups French onion soup.
3. Meanwhile, about 30 minutes before serving, adjust the oven rack to the top portion and preheat the oven to 425°F.
4. On an aluminum foil-lined baking sheet, arrange 8 to 12 bread slices in a single layer and toast in the preheated oven until light golden brown, 5 minutes. Set aside and increase the oven temperature to the low broil setting.
5. On the baking sheet, place 4 to 6 oven-safe bowls and fill each to the rim with soup. Top with 1 or 2 slices of toasted bread and layer with cheese, completely covering the bread.
6. Broil in the oven until the cheese is melted, 2 to 3 minutes. Serve with any extra bread for dipping.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 5.08 hours
Total: 5 hours, 15 minutes
Notes: This French onion soup is extra rich, made with shallots and sweet white onions in the slow cooker.
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: Chocolate Babka
Ingredients:
1. 4 cups all-purpose flour, preferably King Arthur
2. 1/3 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar
3. 2 teaspoons fine sea salt
4. 1 cup whole milk, warmed
5. 1 packet dry active yeast
6. 1 large egg plus 1 large egg yolk
7. 1 stick plus 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into tablespoons, at room temperature
8. 9 ounces milk chocolate, finely chopped
9. 3 ounces bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
10. 1 1/2 sticks unsalted butter, cubed
11. 1 1/2 cups finely ground chocolate wafer cookies
12. 3 tablespoons honey
13. 12 ounces bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
14. 4 ounces milk chocolate, finely chopped
15. 1 1/2 sticks unsalted butter, cubed
16. 2 tablespoons light corn syrup
Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, whisk the flour with the sugar and salt. In a stand mixer fitted with the dough hook, combine the milk with the yeast and let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes. Add the egg and egg yolk and sprinkle the dry ingredients on top. Mix at low speed for 2 minutes. Scrape down the side of the bowl and mix at medium speed until all of the dry ingredients are incorporated and the dough is smooth, about 5 minutes.
2. Add all of the butter at once and mix at low speed until it is fully incorporated and a tacky dough forms, about 3 minutes; scrape down the side of the bowl as needed during mixing. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
3. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper and coat the paper generously with nonstick baking spray. Scrape the dough out onto the parchment paper and cut the dough in half. Pat each piece into a neat square. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
4. In a large heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water, melt both chocolates with the butter, stirring occasionally, until smooth. Let cool to room temperature, then stir in the cookie crumbs and honey.
5. Coat two 9- x 4-inch loaf pans with nonstick baking spray and line with parchment paper, allowing 2 inches of overhang on each of the long sides. Roll out each square of dough to a 16-inch square. Using an offset spatula, spread all but 1/2 cup of the filling in an even layer over the dough squares to within 1/2 inch of the edges.
6. Starting at the long edge nearest you, tightly roll up each dough square jelly roll–style into a tight log.
7. Using a sharp knife, cut the logs in half crosswise. Using an offset spatula, spread 1/4 cup of the reserved filling on the top and sides of two of the halves. Set the other halves on top in the opposite direction to form a cross.
8. Twist to form spirals and transfer to the prepared pans. Cover the loaves with a towel and let stand in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours.
9. Preheat the oven to 375°F. Bake the loaves in the center of the oven for about 45 minutes, until puffed and well browned. Let cool slightly, then use the parchment paper to lift the babkas out of the pans and onto a rack set over a baking sheet. Discard the paper.
10. In a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water, melt both chocolates with the butter; stir until smooth. Stir in the corn syrup. Spread the glaze on top of the warm babkas and let stand until set, about 30 minutes.
Time:
Prep: 1 hr 15 mins
Cook: 45 mins
Total: 14 hrs
Notes: This amazing babka cake gets extra flavor in the swirl from cookie crumbs. As a bonus, it's topped with a thick, luscious chocolate glaze.
Author: Melissa Weller

Title: Three-Chile Harissa
Ingredients:
1. 3 ounces dried guajillo chiles (about 7 large or 15 small), seeds removed, torn into 1-inch pieces
2. 1 ounce chipotle chiles (about 9 medium or 14 small), seeds removed, torn into 1-inch pieces
3. 1 tablespoon nigella seeds
4. 1 teaspoon coriander seeds
5. 2 garlic cloves
6. 1 tablespoon ground cumin
7. 1 teaspoon kosher salt
8. ½ teaspoon Aleppo pepper or ¼ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes
9. ½ cup olive oil
10. 2 tablespoons Champagne or white wine vinegar
Instructions:
1. Place guajillo and chipotle chiles in a large heatproof bowl and pour in boiling water to cover. Let chiles soak until softened, 40–45 minutes; drain.
2. Grind nigella seeds and coriander seeds in a spice mill or with a mortar and pestle. Transfer to a food processor and add garlic, cumin, salt, and Aleppo pepper and pulse until garlic is very finely chopped. Add chiles and pulse until they are chopped. Add oil and vinegar and pulse just until incorporated (mixture should have the texture of a coarse paste).
3. Harissa can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.
Notes: Nigella seeds can be found at Indian markets, some supermarkets, or online.
Author: Jeremy Fox

Title: Potato, Sausage, and Spinach Breakfast Casserole
Ingredients:
1. 16 large eggs
2. 1 1/4 cups heavy cream
3. 1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt plus more
4. 3/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper plus more
5. 5 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided
6. 12 ounces fresh breakfast sausage links
7. 2 cups sliced leeks, white and green parts only
8. 2 russet potatoes (1 1/2 lb.), peeled, shredded, and lightly squeezed
9. 1 pound frozen spinach, thawed, squeezed dry, roughly chopped
10. 1 1/2 cups (4 oz.) grated Gruyère
11. 1/2 cup (1 1/2 oz.) grated Parmesan
Instructions:
1. Position a rack in top third of the oven; preheat to 350°. Whisk eggs, cream, 1 1/2 tsp. salt, and 3/4 tsp. pepper in a large bowl; set aside.
2. Melt 1 Tbsp. butter in a 12” cast-iron or nonstick ovenproof skillet over medium heat. Add sausages and cook, turning often, until browned all over, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a plate. Add leeks to skillet. Season with salt; cook until wilted, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl.
3. Melt 3 Tbsp. butter in same skillet over medium heat. Add potatoes, spreading out in an even layer. Cook without stirring until bottom and edges are crisp and golden brown, about 10 minutes. Slide potato pancake onto a large plate. Invert onto another large plate. Melt 1 Tbsp. butter in skillet. Slide potato pancake into skillet browned side up; cook until bottom is golden brown, about 10 minutes.
4. Scatter spinach, leeks, and sausages over potato pancake, each in an even layer. Season with salt and pepper, then pour in egg mixture. Sprinkle both cheeses over.
5. Bake until casserole is puffed and golden brown, 35–40 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Time: Total: 1 hour
Author: Bon Appétit Test Kitchen

Title: Vegan General Tso's Cauliflower
Ingredients:
1. 2 quarts vegetable or peanut oil
2. 1 tablespoon sesame oil
3. 3 cloves garlic, minced
4. 4 scallions, white and light green parts only, minced
5. 1 tablespoon minced or grated ginger
6. 5 small dried chiles
7. 1/2 cup hoisin sauce
Instructions:
1. Heat the vegetable or peanut oil in a large pot over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
2. In a separate pan, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
3. Add the garlic, scallions, and ginger to the sesame oil and sauté until fragrant.
4. Add the dried chiles and hoisin sauce to the pan and stir to combine.
5. Once the oil is hot, fry the cauliflower florets until golden brown and crispy.
6. Remove the cauliflower from the oil and drain on paper towels.
7. Toss the fried cauliflower in the sauce until well coated.
8. Serve immediately, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 15 minutes
Total: 35 minutes
Notes: This recipe is a fusion of various General Tso's cauliflower interpretations and is not authentic to any single origin.
Author: Sarah Jampel

Title: Ginger Karaage Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces
2. 2 tablespoons soy sauce
3. 2 tablespoons sake
4. 1 tablespoon grated fresh ginger
5. 1 clove garlic, minced
6. 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
7. 1/2 cup cornstarch
8. Vegetable oil, for frying
9. Lemon wedges, for serving
Instructions:
1. 1. In a bowl, combine the soy sauce, sake, ginger, and garlic.
2. 2. Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and let them marinate for at least 30 minutes.
3. 3. In a separate bowl, mix the flour and cornstarch.
4. 4. Heat vegetable oil in a deep pan to 350°F (175°C).
5. 5. Dredge the marinated chicken pieces in the flour mixture, ensuring they are well-coated.
6. 6. Fry the chicken in batches until golden brown and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch.
7. 7. Drain the fried chicken on paper towels.
8. 8. Serve the chicken with lemon wedges.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 20 minutes
Total: 30 minutes
Notes: For extra flavor, you can add a pinch of salt to the flour mixture.
Author: Nathan Legare

Title: Baked Eggs With Onions and Cheese
Ingredients:
1. 2 tablespoons olive oil
2. 2 medium onions, thinly sliced
3. Salt and black pepper
4. 4 large eggs
5. 1/2 cup grated cheese (such as Gruyere or Cheddar)
6. Chopped fresh herbs (such as parsley or chives) for garnish
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large ovenproof skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
3. Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are soft and golden brown, about 15 minutes. Season with salt and black pepper.
4. Make four indentations in the onions and crack an egg into each indentation.
5. Sprinkle the grated cheese over the eggs and onions.
6. Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake until the eggs are just set, about 8 to 10 minutes.
7. Garnish with chopped fresh herbs before serving.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 25 minutes
Total: 35 minutes
Notes: You can use any type of cheese you prefer or have on hand.
Author: Naz Deravian

Title: Roast Chicken with Harissa and Schmaltz
Ingredients:
1. 3 garlic cloves, smashed, peeled
2. ⅓ cup sugar
3. ¼ cup coriander seeds
4. 1 cup kosher salt, plus more
5. 1 4–4½-pound chicken, halved, backbone removed
6. 1 cup Three-Chile Harissa
7. ¼ cup schmaltz (chicken fat) or olive oil
Instructions:
1. Bring garlic, sugar, coriander seeds, 1 cup kosher salt, and 8 cups water to a boil in a large saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar and salt. Transfer to a large bowl and add 1 cup ice. Let cool. (You can also refrigerate or freeze brine if you want to speed things up.)
2. While brine is cooling, bone chicken breasts, leaving leg and thigh quarters intact. Start by cutting off wing tips; discard. Place chicken, skin side down, on a cutting board. Working with 1 chicken half at a time, angle the blade of a thin, sharp knife flush against breast bone and cut along bone to separate the rib cage from flesh. The only bones remaining should be in the wing, thigh, and drumstick. Repeat on the other side (save bones for making your next pot of stock).
3. Place chicken halves in cooled brine. Cover tightly and chill 12 hours.
4. Transfer chicken to a rimmed baking sheet or baking pan and pick off coriander seeds. Spread harissa all over chicken. Cover tightly and chill at least 1 hour and up to 12 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 400° (if you have a convection oven, turn the convection fan on). Heat schmaltz in a large cast-iron pan over medium. Carefully place chicken halves, skin sides down, in pan, making sure all the skin is in the fat. Cook until skin darkens and starts to crisp, about 5 minutes. Transfer skillet to oven and roast chicken until skin is very dark and meat is more than halfway cooked through, 20–25 minutes.
6. Remove skillet from oven and carefully turn chicken. Return to oven and roast, skin side up, until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of thigh registers 165°, 8–12 minutes.
7. Transfer chicken to a large platter, placing skin side up. Drizzle some of the schmaltz over chicken and serve remaining schmaltz alongside.
Time:
Prep: 12 hours 5 minutes
Cook: 33-42 minutes
Total: 13-14 hours
Notes: The real magic in this roast chicken recipe is in the pool of schmaltz, AKA rendered chicken fat, sizzling in the pan.
Author: Jeremy Fox

Title: No-Bake Salted Chocolate Cheesecake
Ingredients:
1. 1⅓ cups (11 ounces) cream cheese
2. ½ cup sour cream
3. ¼ cup light brown sugar
4. ¼ cup cocoa powder, plus more for dusting
5. 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
6. 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
7. ½ teaspoon kosher salt
8. 2 cups (12 ounces) NESTLÉ® TOLL HOUSE® Semi–Sweet Chocolate Morsels, melted and cooled slightly
9. One 6-ounce ready-to-use graham cracker crust
10. Flaky sea salt, to garnish
Instructions:
1. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, combine the cream cheese, sour cream, brown sugar, cocoa powder, butter, vanilla and salt, and beat on medium speed until smooth and creamy, 5 minutes.
2. Reduce the speed to medium low, pour in the melted chocolate and mix until incorporated.
3. Spoon the mixture into the graham cracker crust, dust with cocoa powder and sprinkle with flaky sea salt.
4. Refrigerate until firm, at least 30 minutes, then serve.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 5 minutes
Total: 20 minutes
Notes: The cheesecake can be made and chilled up to 2 days in advance. For alternative melting instructions: Microwave morsels in a small microwave-safe bowl for 1 minute on medium-high power, then stir until smooth. Morsels may retain some of their original shape. If necessary, microwave at additional 10- to 20-second intervals, stirring until smooth.
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: How To Make Chilaquiles For Breakfast
Ingredients:
1. Stale tortillas
2. Oil for frying
3. Chipotle in adobo
4. Dried ancho pepper
5. Salsa or mole (for rojo)
6. Tomatillo-based salsa (for verde)
7. Chorizo
8. Avocado
9. Queso fresco
10. Black beans
11. Shredded chicken
12. Salt-cured steak
13. Fried egg
14. Optional toppings: diced onion, garlic, shredded mozzarella
Instructions:
1. Cut stale tortillas into small squares.
2. Fry the tortilla squares in oil until they are crispy. Do not salt them.
3. Prepare your sauce: For rojo, use chipotle in adobo and dried ancho pepper. For verde, use a tomatillo-based salsa.
4. Toss the fried tortilla chips in the sauce until they are well coated but not soggy.
5. Top the coated chips with your choice of toppings: chorizo, avocado, queso fresco, black beans, shredded chicken, or salt-cured steak.
6. Finish with a fried egg on top.
Notes: For the best texture, make your own chips and avoid oversoaking them in the sauce. Be creative with toppings, using leftovers you have on hand.
Author: Katy Peetz
