Title: Pizzoccheri della Valtellina
Ingredients:
1. 250g buckwheat flour
2. 100g all-purpose flour
3. 200g potatoes, peeled and cubed
4. 150g Savoy cabbage, chopped
5. 150g Valtellina Casera cheese, cubed
6. 100g Parmigiano-Reggiano, grated
7. 2 cloves garlic, minced
8. 100g butter
9. Salt, to taste
10. Pepper, to taste
Instructions:
1. Mix the buckwheat and all-purpose flour together and knead into a dough. Roll out and cut into strips.
2. Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, adding the cabbage halfway through cooking.
3. In a separate pot, cook the pasta strips in salted boiling water for about 10 minutes.
4. In a large pan, melt the butter and sauté the garlic until fragrant.
5. Drain the pasta and vegetables, then add them to the pan with the garlic butter.
6. Add the Valtellina Casera cheese and Parmigiano-Reggiano, stirring until melted and well combined.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste and serve hot.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 50 minutes
Notes: This traditional dish from the Valtellina region of Italy is rich and comforting, perfect for a cozy meal.
Author: Formaggio Kitchen

Title: Ginger Chicken and Rice Soup With Zucchini
Ingredients:
1. 1 tablespoon olive oil
2. 1 small onion, diced
3. 2 cloves garlic, minced
4. 1 tablespoon fresh ginger, grated
5. 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
6. 6 cups chicken broth
7. 1 cup jasmine rice
8. 2 medium zucchinis, diced
9. Salt and pepper to taste
10. 2 tablespoons soy sauce
11. 2 green onions, sliced
12. Fresh cilantro, for garnish
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in garlic and ginger, cooking for an additional minute.
4. Add chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides.
5. Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil.
6. Add jasmine rice, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Stir in zucchini and continue to simmer until rice is tender, about 10 more minutes.
8. Season with salt, pepper, and soy sauce.
9. Serve hot, garnished with green onions and cilantro.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 45 minutes
Author: Yossy Arefi

Title: Buttermilk Ranch Dressing
Ingredients:
1. ¾ cup whole-milk plain Greek yogurt
2. ⅔ cup buttermilk
3. 3 tablespoons mayonnaise
4. 1 tablespoon finely chopped chives
5. 1 teaspoon fresh lemon juice
6. 1 garlic clove, finely grated
7. ¼ teaspoon dried oregano
8. ¼ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
9. ¼ teaspoon mustard powder
10. ¼ teaspoon onion powder
11. Kosher salt
Instructions:
1. Whisk yogurt, buttermilk, mayonnaise, chives, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, pepper, mustard powder, and onion powder in a medium bowl; season with salt.
2. Chill at least 20 minutes to let flavors meld before serving.
Notes: Dressing can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and keep chilled.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Pasta e Fagioli With Escarole
Ingredients:
1. 1½ cups dried cannellini (white kidney) beans, soaked overnight
2. 1 Parmesan rind (about 2 ounces), plus shaved Parmesan for serving
3. 2 medium carrots, scrubbed, halved crosswise
4. 2 celery stalks, halved crosswise
5. 1 head of garlic, halved crosswise; plus 2 cloves, chopped
6. 6 sprigs parsley
7. 1 sprig rosemary
8. 2 bay leaves
9. 2 dried chiles de árbol or ½ teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, plus more crushed for serving
10. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
11. 3 tablespoons olive oil, plus more
12. 1 large onion, chopped
13. 1 14.5-ounce can whole peeled tomatoes
14. ¾ cup dry white wine
15. 3 ounces dried lasagna or other flat pasta, broken into 1–1½-inch pieces
16. ½ small head of escarole, leaves torn into 2-inch pieces
Instructions:
1. Bring beans, Parmesan rind, carrots, celery, head of garlic, parsley, rosemary, bay leaves, chiles, and 2 quarts water to a boil in a medium pot. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer, adding more water as needed, until beans are tender, about 1½ hours. Season with salt and pepper, remove from heat, and let sit 30 minutes. Discard vegetables, rind, and herbs.
2. Meanwhile, heat 3 Tbsp. oil in a large pot over medium. Cook onion and chopped garlic, stirring occasionally, until softened, 8–10 minutes. Add tomatoes, crushing with your hands, and cook, stirring often, until liquid is almost completely reduced, 12–15 minutes. Add wine, bring to a boil, and cook until almost completely evaporated, about 5 minutes.
3. Add beans and their liquid; cook until flavors meld, 12–15 minutes. Add pasta; cook, stirring and adding more water as needed, until al dente, 15–20 minutes. Add escarole and cook until wilted, about 1 minute; season with salt and pepper. Serve soup drizzled with oil and topped with Parmesan and more chile.
Time: Cook: 1 hour 50 minutes
Notes: Parmesan rind and a kitchen sink’s worth of aromatics give heady flavor to this classic pasta fagioli recipe with beans and pasta.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Sausage Meatball Sandwiches
Ingredients:
1. 1½ pounds hot and/or sweet Italian sausage, casings removed
2. 8 tablespoons olive oil, divided
3. 1 fennel bulb, core removed, thinly sliced
4. 1 large onion, chopped
5. 10 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, divided
6. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
7. 1 28-ounce can whole peeled tomatoes
8. 1 cup (packed) basil leaves
9. 4 soft hoagie rolls, split lengthwise down tops
10. 4 ounces fresh mozzarella, sliced
Instructions:
1. Using wet hands, lightly roll sausage into 12 golf ball–size balls (about 2 oz. each). Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium. Cook meatballs, turning occasionally, until browned all over, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
2. Add fennel, onion, half of garlic, and 2 Tbsp. oil to same pot; season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring often, until onion is translucent and golden brown around edges, 8–10 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes, crushing with your hands as you go, and bring to a simmer. Season with salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until sauce just begins to thicken, 5–8 minutes.
4. Return meatballs to pot, bring to a simmer, and cook, stirring occasionally, until sauce is reduced by half and meatballs are coated and cooked through, 15–20 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, heat broiler. Process basil, remaining garlic, and remaining 4 Tbsp. oil in a food processor to a smooth paste; season with salt and pepper. Smear half of basil mixture over cut sides of rolls and place on a rimmed baking sheet. Broil rolls until golden brown, about 2 minutes.
6. Place meatballs inside rolls, then top with sauce and mozzarella. Broil until mozzarella is melted and bubbling, about 5 minutes. Spoon remaining basil mixture over sandwiches.
Time: Total: 75 minutes
Notes: We like the combo of spicy and sweet Italian sausage, but use any uncooked sausage you like—merguez or chorizo would work.
Author: Claire Saffitz

Title: Always-Tender London Broil
Ingredients:
1. 4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced
2. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
3. 2 Tbsp. good-quality balsamic vinegar
4. 2 Tbsp. soy sauce
5. 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce
6. Freshly ground black pepper
7. ¾ tsp. baking soda
8. 1½–2 lb. London broil
9. 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil
10. Kosher salt
11. 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter
12. Flaky sea salt
Instructions:
1. Whisk 4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil, 2 Tbsp. good-quality balsamic vinegar, 2 Tbsp. soy sauce, and 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce in a medium bowl or baking dish to combine; season with freshly ground black pepper. Whisk in ¾ tsp. baking soda (don’t worry if marinade fizzes; that’s the baking soda working).
2. Prick one 1½–2 lb. London broil all over with a fork and add to marinade, turning to coat. Let sit at room temperature at least 1 hour or cover and chill up to 12 hours.
3. Heat 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil in a large cast-iron pan or other heavy-bottomed skillet over medium. Remove steak from marinade and allow excess to drip back into bowl. Pat steak dry with paper towels; reserve marinade. Season steak generously with kosher salt and cook, turning once, until deeply browned and an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part registers 125° for medium-rare, 8–12 minutes total. Using tongs, stand steak on its sides and cook until lightly browned, about 30 seconds per side. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest 10 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, transfer reserved marinade to a small saucepan, add ⅓ cup water, and bring to a boil (mixture will foam up). Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly to avoid overflow, until foaming subsides, about 3 minutes. Reduce heat to low and add 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter; whisk vigorously to emulsify sauce. Keep warm until ready to serve.
5. Thinly slice London broil against the grain and arrange on a platter. Spoon sauce over; sprinkle with flaky sea salt.
Time:
Prep: 1 hour plus marinating
Cook: 8–12 minutes
Total: 25 minutes plus marinating
Notes: This London Broil recipe was first printed online in December 2022.
Author: Zaynab Issa

Title: Spaghetti with Clams and Braised Greens
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil
2. 8 garlic cloves, crushed
3. 4 dozen littleneck clams
4. 2 cups dry white wine
5. 2 jarred roasted red peppers, drained
6. 1 teaspoon crushed red pepper
7. 1 pound Swiss chard or collard greens, stemmed and chopped (8 cups)
8. Kosher salt
9. Pepper
10. 1/2 pound spaghetti
11. 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, cubed and chilled
12. 1 teaspoon grated lemon zest
13. 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
14. 1/2 cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, plus more for garnish
Instructions:
1. In a large pot, heat 1/4 cup of the oil. Add half of the garlic and cook over moderate heat, stirring, for 1 minute. Stir in the clams. Add the wine and bring to a boil. Cover and cook over moderately high heat, stirring occasionally, 5 to 7 minutes; as the clams open, transfer them to a baking sheet. Discard any unopened clams. Strain the cooking liquid through a sieve into a blender. Add the roasted peppers and puree until smooth.
2. Wash out the pot, then heat the remaining 1/4 cup of oil in it. Add the remaining garlic and cook over moderate heat, stirring, for 1 minute. Stir in the crushed red pepper and Swiss chard in batches until the chard is just wilted, about 3 minutes. Stir in the roasted pepper broth and season with salt and pepper.
3. In a large pot of salted boiling water, cook the spaghetti until al dente; drain. Add the pasta to the Swiss chard mixture along with the butter, lemon zest, lemon juice and the 1/2 cup of grated cheese. Add the clams and toss to heat through. Transfer the pasta and broth to shallow bowls, garnish with grated cheese and serve.
Time: Total: 45 mins
Notes: We didn't think spaghetti and clams could get better than the garlicky classic until chef Ashley Christensen of Poole’s Diner in Raleigh, North Carolina, created a version that forever changed the way we look at this dish. Her method is to steam the clams in wine, then puree the resulting broth with roasted red peppers, creating a briny, rich, deeply flavorful sauce that clings to the pasta—no leaving any delicious sauce behind in the bowl. Adding a hefty load of Swiss chard helps to amp up the flavor, creating a fabulously satisfying one-dish wonder.
Author: Ashley Christensen

Title: Twice-Roasted Sweet Potatoes With Hot Honey
Ingredients:
1. 3 medium sweet potatoes or garnet yams, scrubbed (about 3 pounds)
2. 1 Fresno or other red chiles, thinly sliced
3. ¼ cup honey
4. 4 tablespoons unsalted butter
5. Kosher salt
6. 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°. Poke holes all over sweet potatoes and wrap each in foil. Place on a foil-lined rimmed baking sheet and roast until potatoes are tender, 60–70 minutes (if you have one, use a cake tester to poke right through foil). Unwrap and let sit until cool enough to handle. Increase oven temperature to 450°. Meanwhile, combine chile, honey, and butter in a small saucepan; season with salt. Bring to a simmer over low heat, stirring to combine. Remove from heat and stir in vinegar.
2. Smash sweet potatoes with your palm, then tear into bite-sized pieces (including skin), the more irregular, the better. Place in a large bowl and add half of hot honey (do not include chile as it will burn); season with salt. Arrange pieces, skin side down, in a single layer on an unlined rimmed baking sheet and roast until browned and crisp around the edges, 20–25 minutes. Drizzle with remaining hot honey with chile.
Time:
Cook: 2 hours
Total: 2 hours
Notes: This recipe embraces the incredible natural sweetness of sweet potatoes but gives it a spicy edge.
Author: Claire Saffitz

Title: Slow Cooker Pot Roast with Carrot Top Pesto
Ingredients:
1. 3-4 pound chuck roast
2. 1 bunch carrots, cut in 2 inch pieces with greens reserved
3. 3 ribs celery, cut into 1-2 inch pieces
4. 1 large onion, diced
5. 2 rutabagas, peeled and cubed
6. 1/2 oz dried mushrooms
7. 1 cup beef broth
8. 1/2 cup fresh basil
9. 1/2 cup fresh parsley
10. 1/4 cup fresh rosemary
11. 4 garlic cloves
12. 1/3 cup + 2 tablespoons olive oil
13. 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar
14. zest from one lemon
15. 1/3 cup pine nuts
16. kosher salt
17. black pepper
Instructions:
1. Soak the dried mushrooms in 1 cup of lukewarm water for at least 30 minutes. Strain the mushrooms, reserving the liquid, and rinse well. Pour the liquid through cheese cloth or a fine sieve and set aside.
2. Heat the pine nuts in a small skillet over low heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant and golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
3. Combine the pine nuts, fresh herbs, garlic, and lemon zest in a food processor and pulse. Turn the processor to a constant speed and drizzle in 1/3 cup of olive oil until a paste is formed. Season the roast liberally with salt and pepper and smear with the pesto.
4. Heat a tablespoon of olive oil over high heat. Sear the roast on all sides until brown. Transfer the roast to the slow cooker.
5. Pour the broth in the skillet and deglaze, scraping up any brown bits from the bottom of the pan. Pour the liquid over the roast.
6. Add the mushrooms, mushroom liquid, balsamic vinegar, and any remaining pesto to the slow cooker. Scatter the carrots, celery, onion, and rutabagas around the roast.
7. Cover and cook on low for 8-10 hours.
8. Remove the roast and vegetables from the slow cooker. Strain the juice and serve over the roast.
Time: Cook: 8 hours
Author: The Sophisticated Caveman

Title: Jewish-Style Braised Brisket With Onions and Carrots
Ingredients:
1. 1 whole brisket (about 5 pounds), first cut
2. Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3. 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
4. 4 large onions, thinly sliced
5. 6 carrots, peeled and cut into 1-inch pieces
6. 4 cloves garlic, minced
7. 1 cup chicken stock or low-sodium broth
8. 1 cup dry red wine
9. 2 tablespoons tomato paste
10. 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar
11. 2 tablespoons Worcestershire sauce
12. 2 bay leaves
13. 1 tablespoon fresh thyme leaves or 1 teaspoon dried thyme
Instructions:
1. 1. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
2. 2. Season the brisket generously with salt and pepper.
3. 3. In a large Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat until shimmering.
4. 4. Add the brisket and brown on both sides, about 6 minutes per side.
5. 5. Remove the brisket and set aside.
6. 6. Add onions and carrots to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are softened and starting to brown, about 10 minutes.
7. 7. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
8. 8. Stir in chicken stock, red wine, tomato paste, apple cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves, and thyme.
9. 9. Return the brisket to the pot, cover, and transfer to the oven.
10. 10. Braise until the brisket is fork-tender, about 3 to 4 hours.
11. 11. Remove the brisket from the pot and let rest for 15 minutes before slicing.
12. 12. Thinly slice the brisket against the grain and return to the pot with the braising liquid.
13. 13. Serve hot, with the onions, carrots, and sauce spooned over the top.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 3 to 4 hours
Total: 3 hours 30 minutes to 4 hours 30 minutes
Notes: For best results, use a brisket with both the first and second cuts.
Author: Daniel Gritzer

Title: Double-Pork Carnitas
Ingredients:
1. 3 pounds boneless pork shoulder (Boston butt), cut into 1½-inch pieces
2. 1 pound pork belly, cut into 1-inch pieces
3. 1 cup homemade chicken stock or low-sodium chicken broth
4. 1 tablespoon (heaping) kosher salt
5. 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
6. Warm corn tortillas, chopped white onion, lime wedges, sliced avocado, dried oregano, chopped cilantro, and shredded cabbage (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Place pork shoulder, pork belly, stock, salt, and pepper in a large heavy pot; cover and bring to a boil over medium-low. Reduce heat and simmer until pork is tender and shreds easily, about 2 hours. Uncover; cook 10 minutes to reduce liquid (but not completely). Remove from heat.
2. Transfer half of pork and pan juices to a large skillet, preferably nonstick, and cook over high until liquid evaporates and pork begins to fry in its rendered fat. Fry, stirring occasionally and pressing on pork with spoon to break up, until browned and just beginning to crisp, 10–15 minutes. Most of the pieces should be bite-size with some smaller shredded and super-crisp bits in the mix. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a paper towel–lined baking sheet. Cover with foil to keep warm and moist. Repeat with remaining pork.
3. Serve carnitas with tortillas, onion, limes, avocado, herbs, and cabbage.
4. Do Ahead: Pork can be braised (do not fry) 3 days ahead. Cover and chill.
Time: Cook: 2 hours 25 minutes
Notes: It’s worth seeking out pork belly for the amazing texture it lends, but if you can’t get it, substitute with another pound of shoulder.
Author: Rick Martinez

Title: Baked Rigatoni with Spinach, Ricotta, and Fontina
Ingredients:
1. 1 pound rigatoni
2. 3 tablespoons olive oil
3. 1 10-ounce package frozen spinach, thawed
4. 2 cups (about 1 pound) ricotta
5. 5 tablespoons grated Parmesan
6. 1/2 teaspoon grated nutmeg
7. 3/4 teaspoon salt
8. 1/4 teaspoon fresh-ground black pepper
9. 6 ounces fontina, grated (about 1 1/2 cups)
Instructions:
1. Heat the oven to 450°. Oil a 9-by-13-inch baking dish.
2. In a large pot of boiling, salted water, cook the rigatoni until almost done, about 12 minutes. Drain. Put the pasta in the prepared baking dish and toss with 1 tablespoon of the oil.
3. Meanwhile, squeeze as much of the water as possible from the spinach. Put the spinach in a food processor and puree with the ricotta, 3 tablespoons of the Parmesan, the nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Stir in half the fontina.
4. Stir the spinach mixture into the pasta. Top with the remaining fontina and Parmesan. Drizzle the remaining 2 tablespoons oil over the top. Bake the pasta until the top is golden brown, 15 to 20 minutes.
Time: Cook: 15 to 20 minutes
Notes: ['Variation: You can substitute another chunky pasta, such as penne rigate, penne, ziti, or fusilli. Boil all of these one or two minutes less.', 'Suggested Pairing: A delicately flavored white such as Orvieto will complement the mild ricotta nicely. And, because Orvieto is not oaky, the wine has no bitter tannin to clash with the slightly bitter spinach and nutmeg.']
Author: Food & Wine

Title: BA's Best Buffalo Wings
Ingredients:
1. 2 ounces mild blue cheese (such as Roquefort or Saint Agur), crumbled
2. ¼ cup buttermilk
3. ¼ cup mayonnaise
4. ¼ cup sour cream
5. 1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
6. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
7. 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
8. ½ cup hot pepper sauce (such as Frank’s)
9. ½ teaspoon cayenne pepper
10. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
11. Vegetable oil for frying (about 10 cups)
12. ¾ cup cornstarch
13. 3 pounds chicken wings, tips removed, drumettes and flats separated
14. 4 celery stalks, cut into thin sticks
Instructions:
1. Whisk together blue cheese, buttermilk, mayonnaise, sour cream, and lemon juice in a medium bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Preheat oven to 250°. Let wings sit at room temperature 30 minutes; this will take off the chill and help them cook evenly. Meanwhile, melt butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in hot sauce, cayenne, ½ tsp. pepper, and ¼ tsp. salt until combined. Remove pan from heat and set aside; rewarm just before tossing with wings.
3. Pour oil into a large heavy pot to a depth of 2” and fit with a deep-fry thermometer. Heat over medium-high heat until thermometer registers 375°.
4. Meanwhile, place cornstarch in a large bowl. Season wings with salt and pepper. Working in 3 batches, dredge wings in cornstarch, shaking off excess, then carefully lower wings into oil. Fry, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through and crust is golden brown and very crisp, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and transfer to oven to keep warm while you fry remaining wings.
5. Transfer wings to a large bowl, add warmed sauce, and toss to coat. Serve immediately with celery sticks and blue cheese dressing for dipping.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 10–12 minutes per batch
Total: Approximately 1 hour
Notes: If you like your wing sauce super spicy, increase the amount of cayenne. Dressing can be made up to 2 days ahead. Sauce can be made 1 week ahead.
Author: Dawn Perry

Title: Thai Smashed Cucumber Salad
Ingredients:
1. 6 small Persian or Japanese cucumbers (about 1 pound), ends trimmed
2. 2 garlic cloves, grated using a microplane
3. 2 tablespoons rice wine vinegar
4. 2 tablespoons sugar
5. 1 tablespoon chile oil
6. 1 tablespoon toasted sesame oil
7. 2½ teaspoons kosher salt, plus more to taste
8. 1 teaspoon finely grated ginger (1-to-2-inch piece)
9. 2 teaspoons finely grated lime zest
10. 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice
11. ¼ cup chopped cilantro
12. 2 scallions, halved lengthwise and thinly sliced on an angle
13. 1 tablespoon fish sauce (optional)
Instructions:
1. Cut the cucumbers into 1-inch rings and arrange them on a cutting board or kitchen surface. Using a large knife, gently smash down on the cucumber pieces so that they flatten out a bit.
2. In a large bowl, combine the garlic, rice wine vinegar, sugar, chile oil, sesame oil, fish sauce, salt and ginger. Add the cucumbers and toss. Refrigerate for at least 20 minutes or overnight (see note).
3. Right before serving, toss in the lime zest and juice, cilantro and scallions.
Time:
Prep: 35 minutes
Cook: 0 minutes
Total: 35 minutes
Notes: The salad can be prepared two ways: with a quick pickle for a crunchy texture or left overnight for more cohesive flavors.
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: Coconut Mango Rum Ice Pops with Thai Chile Salt
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup coconut milk
2. 1/2 cup mango puree
3. 1/4 cup rum
4. 2 tablespoons sugar
5. 1/4 teaspoon salt
6. 1/2 teaspoon Thai chile powder
Instructions:
1. Combine coconut milk, mango puree, rum, and sugar in a blender.
2. Blend until smooth.
3. Pour the mixture into ice pop molds.
4. Freeze for at least 4 hours or until solid.
5. Before serving, mix salt and Thai chile powder in a small bowl.
6. Sprinkle the Thai chile salt over the ice pops before serving.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 4 hours freezing time
Total: 4 hours 10 minutes
Notes: For a non-alcoholic version, replace rum with coconut water.
Author: Nathan Legare

Title: Cauliflower Cheese
Ingredients:
1. 1 medium head (about 2 to 2 1/4 pounds) cauliflower
2. 4 tablespoons (55 grams or 2 ounces) unsalted butter
3. 4 tablespoons (30 grams or 1 ounce) all-purpose flour
4. 2 teaspoons mustard powder
5. Salt, to taste
6. Freshly ground black pepper or ground cayenne
7. 2 cups (475 ml) milk, whole is best but low-fat will probably work just fine
8. 1 1/4 cups plus 2 tablespoons (about 155 grams or 5 1/2 ounces total) grated cheddar, the strongest you can get, preferably English or Irish
9. Chopped chives or flat-leaf parsley, for garnish (optional)
Instructions:
1. Heat oven to 400 degrees.
2. Trim cauliflower and remove tough core. Cut into 1 to 2-inch florets.
3. Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and add cauliflower florets. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes, until tender but not mushy. Drain and set aside.
4. In a medium saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Add flour and mustard powder, and whisk until smooth.
5. Slowly whisk in milk, a little at a time, until mixture is smooth. Bring to a simmer and cook for 2 to 3 minutes, until thickened.
6. Remove from heat and stir in 1 1/4 cups of cheddar cheese until melted. Season with salt and pepper or cayenne to taste.
7. Arrange cauliflower in a baking dish and pour cheese sauce over it.
8. Sprinkle with remaining 2 tablespoons of cheddar cheese.
9. Bake for 25 minutes, until bubbly and lightly browned.
10. Garnish with chopped chives or parsley, if desired, and serve.
Notes: You could infuse the milk with a bay leaf or minced clove of garlic. Stir chopped parsley into the sauce for color. Use Dijon or English mustard if dry mustard is unavailable. Add breadcrumbs on top for extra crunch. For a meal-in-one, add grilled pork sausages.
Author: Deb

Title: My Favorite Brownies
Ingredients:
1. 3 ounces unsweetened chocolate
2. 1 stick (4 ounces) unsalted butter
3. 1 1/3 cups granulated sugar
4. 2 large eggs
5. 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
6. 1/4 teaspoon salt
7. 2/3 cup all-purpose flour
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Line an 8x8-inch square baking pan with parchment paper or foil, leaving an overhang on two sides.
2. In a medium heatproof bowl over gently simmering water, melt the unsweetened chocolate and butter together, stirring until smooth.
3. Remove the bowl from the heat and whisk in the sugar, followed by the eggs, one at a time, then the vanilla and salt.
4. Stir in the flour until just incorporated.
5. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
6. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with moist crumbs.
7. Let the brownies cool completely in the pan on a rack.
8. Use the parchment or foil overhang to lift the brownies out of the pan before cutting into squares.
Yield: 16 to 25 brownies, depending on how you cut them
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 40 minutes
Notes: These brownies are bittersweet and chewy, and can be adapted with various additions like cream cheese or white chocolate mint ganache, but they are perfect on their own.
Author: Deb

Title: Salted Peanut Butter Cookies
Ingredients:
1. 1 3/4 cups (335 grams) packed light brown sugar
2. 2 large eggs, at room temperature
3. 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
4. 1 3/4 cups (450 grams) smooth peanut butter
5. Coarse-grained sea salt, to finish
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the light brown sugar and eggs until smooth.
3. Whisk in the vanilla extract, then the peanut butter until smooth and completely incorporated.
4. If you'd like to get those pretty striations across the top of the cookies, chill the dough.
Yield: 26 to 28 cookies with a 1 2/3 tablespoon or #40 scoop
Notes: The dough should have the consistency of Play-Doh, but it may be thinner and softer.
Author: Deb

Title: Salted Chocolate Chunk Cookies
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups all-purpose flour
2. 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
3. 3/4 teaspoon sea salt, plus more for sprinkling
4. 1/2 cup unsalted butter, at room temperature
5. 2 tablespoons olive oil
6. 1/2 cup granulated sugar
7. 1/2 cup packed light brown sugar
8. 1/4 cup turbinado sugar
9. 1 large egg
10. 1 large egg yolk
11. 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
12. 1 1/4 cups chopped chocolate or chocolate chips
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 360°F (180°C).
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and sea salt.
3. In a large bowl, beat the butter, olive oil, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and turbinado sugar until light and fluffy.
4. Add the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla extract, and mix until combined.
5. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined.
6. Fold in the chopped chocolate or chocolate chips.
7. Scoop dough onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. Sprinkle each cookie with a pinch of sea salt.
9. Bake for 12-14 minutes, until the edges are golden brown.
10. Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 12-14 minutes
Total: 27-29 minutes
Notes: The dough can be baked immediately or scooped and frozen for later use.
Author: Deb Perelman

Title: Adam and Maxine's Famous Latkes
Ingredients:
1. 3 lb. large russet potatoes (4–6)
2. 1 lb. Vidalia or yellow onions (about 2 medium)
3. 1/4 cup fine plain dried breadcrumbs
4. 3 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
5. 2 teaspoons baking powder
6. 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
7. 2–3 large eggs
8. 2 tablespoons (or more) schmaltz (chicken fat; optional)
9. 2-4 tablespoons (or more) vegetable oil
10. Applesauce and sour cream (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°. Peel potatoes. Using the large holes of a box grater or the grater disk on a food processor, grate potatoes and onions. Transfer to a large kitchen towel. Gather ends of towel; twist over sink and squeeze firmly to wring out as much liquid as possible. Open towel; toss mixture to loosen. Gather towel; wring out once more.
2. Whisk breadcrumbs, salt, baking powder, pepper, and 2 eggs in a medium bowl to blend. Add potato mixture. Using your fingers, mix until well coated. Latke mixture should be wet and thick, not soupy; mix in remaining egg if mixture is too dry and doesn't hold together.
3. Line a large rimmed baking sheet with several layers of paper towels. Set a wire rack inside another large rimmed baking sheet; set aside. Heat 2 tablespoons schmaltz, if using, and 2 tablespoons oil (or 4 tablespoons oil if not using schmaltz; fat should measure about 1/8 inches) in a 12 inches nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Drop a small amount of latke mixture into pan. If the fat sizzles around the edges, it's ready. (Do not let fat smoke.)
4. Working in batches and adding more schmaltz and oil to skillet as needed to maintain 1/8 inches fat, drop large spoonfuls of mixture into pan, pressing gently with the back of a spoon or spatula to flatten slightly. (If mixture becomes watery between batches, mix to incorporate; do not drain.)
5. Cook latkes, occasionally rotating pan for even browning, until golden brown and cooked through, 2 1/2-3 minutes per side. (If small pieces of potato floating in the oil start to burn, carefully strain out.)
6. Transfer latkes to paper towel-lined baking sheet to drain, then transfer to prepared wire rack. Place sheet with latkes in oven to keep warm and crisp while cooking remaining latkes.
7. Serve warm latkes with applesauce and sour cream.
Notes: Russets are ideal for this latke recipe. Their high starch content means you won't need flour to bind the pancakes. The result? More potato, and a crunchy (not cakey) texture.
Author: Adam Rapoport

Title: Kale Salad with Pecorino and Walnuts
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 cup (55 grams or 2 ounces) walnut halves or pieces
2. 1/4 cup (45 grams or 1 1/2 ounces) golden raisins
3. 1 tablespoon white wine vinegar
4. 1 tablespoon water
5. 1/4 cup panko (15 grams or 1/2 ounce) or slightly coarse homemade breadcrumbs
6. 1 tiny clove garlic, minced or pressed
7. Coarse or kosher salt
8. 3 tablespoons olive oil
9. 1 bunch (about 14 ounces or 400 grams) tuscan kale, washed and patted dry
10. 2 ounces (55 grams) pecorino cheese, grated or ground
11. Juice of half a lemon
12. Freshly ground black pepper or red pepper flakes, to taste
Instructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°F. Toast walnuts on a baking sheet for 10 minutes, tossing once. Let cool and coarsely chop.
2. In a small saucepan over low heat, simmer white wine vinegar, water, and raisins for 5 minutes, until plump and soft. Set aside in liquid.
3. Toast bread crumbs, garlic, and 2 teaspoons of the olive oil in a skillet with a pinch of salt until golden. Set aside.
4. Trim heavy stems off kale and remove ribs.
Notes: Consider toasting the nuts, the crumbs in olive oil, and plumping the raisins for more depth.
Author: Deb

Title: Rice-Stuffed Tomatoes [Pomodori Ripieni di Riso]
Ingredients:
1. 6 medium to large tomatoes
2. Salt
3. 1/2 cup arborio or another starchy rice
4. 1 small garlic clove, minced
5. 2 tablespoons minced onion
6. Olive oil
7. Black pepper
8. Fresh basil or parsley, chopped (optional)
Instructions:
1. 1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. 2. Cut the tops off the tomatoes and set aside. Scoop out the insides of the tomatoes into a bowl, leaving the shells intact.
3. 3. Strain the tomato pulp to remove seeds and excess liquid, then chop the pulp.
4. 4. Season the inside of the tomato shells with salt and place them upside down on a paper towel to drain.
5. 5. In a small saucepan, heat a tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and onion and cook until softened.
6. 6. Stir in the rice, cooking for another minute or two until it starts to turn golden.
7. 7. Add the chopped tomato pulp to the rice mixture, season with salt and pepper, and cook for a few more minutes.
8. 8. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Stir in fresh herbs if using.
9. 9. Fill the tomato shells with the rice mixture, place the tops back on, and arrange them in a baking dish.
10. 10. Drizzle a little olive oil over the tomatoes and bake for about 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until the tomatoes are tender and the rice is cooked through.
11. 11. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: Serves 6 as a side and 3 as a main
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 1 hour
Total: 1 hour 20 minutes
Notes: You can add ingredients like prosciutto, mozzarella, pecorino cheese, olives, or artichokes for variation. However, the dish is delightful with just rice and tomatoes.
Author: Deb

Title: Cornmeal-Fried Pork Chops and Goat Cheese-Smashed Potatoes
Ingredients:
1. 4 boneless pork chops, about 1-inch thick
2. Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3. 1 cup buttermilk
4. 1 cup finely ground cornmeal
5. 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
6. 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper
7. Vegetable oil, for frying
8. 1 1/2 pounds small Red Bliss potatoes
9. 1/4 cup goat cheese
10. 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
11. 2 tablespoons chopped fresh chives
Instructions:
1. Season pork chops with salt and pepper. Place in a dish and cover with buttermilk. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes or up to overnight in the refrigerator.
2. In a shallow dish, combine cornmeal, flour, and cayenne pepper.
3. Remove pork chops from buttermilk, allowing excess to drip off. Dredge in cornmeal mixture, pressing to adhere.
4. In a large skillet, heat 1/4 inch of oil over medium-high heat. Fry pork chops until golden brown and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
5. Meanwhile, place potatoes in a large pot and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil and cook until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and return to pot.
6. Add goat cheese, butter, and chives to potatoes. Smash with a potato masher until combined and chunky. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve pork chops with smashed potatoes.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 1 hour
Notes: Adapted from Heritage by Sean Brock.
Author: Deb

Title: Potato Kugel
Ingredients:
1. 1 large or 2 small yellow onions
2. 3 pounds or about 5 large baking — Russet or Idaho — potatoes, peeled
3. 1/3 cup potato starch
4. 2 teaspoons kosher salt
5. Lots of freshly ground black pepper
6. 3 large eggs
7. 5 tablespoons olive oil, schmaltz or another oil of your choice
Instructions:
1. Heat oven to 350°F.
2. By hand: Chop onions very finely and coarsely grate potatoes.
3. With a food processor: Blend onions in food processor with regular blade until finely ground. Switch to grating blade and grate potatoes.
Notes: In non-Semitic terms, think of the potato kugel as a massive hash brown with profoundly crispy edges, steamy-soft insides and the showstealing complement to a dinner roast or breakfast eggs.
Author: Deb

Title: liège waffles
Ingredients:
1. 1/4 cup (60 ml) whole milk, lukewarm
2. 2 teaspoons (6 grams) active dry yeast
3. 2 cups (250 grams) all-purpose flour
4. 1 tablespoon (12 grams) granulated sugar
5. 1/2 teaspoon fine sea or table salt
6. 2 large eggs
7. 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
8. 14 tablespoons (200 grams) unsalted butter, at room temperature
9. 1 cup (200 grams) pearl sugar
Instructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the milk and yeast and let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, granulated sugar, and salt.
3. Add the eggs and vanilla to the yeast mixture and whisk to combine.
4. Make a well in the center of the dry ingredients, pour in the wet ingredients, and stir until a dough forms.
5. Knead the dough in the bowl until smooth, about 5 minutes.
6. Add the butter, a tablespoon at a time, kneading until fully incorporated before adding more.
7. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
8. Punch down the dough, cover, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
9. When ready to cook, preheat the waffle iron.
10. Divide the dough into 10 pieces and gently knead in the pearl sugar.
11. Place a piece of dough in the center of the waffle iron and cook until golden brown, about 4 to 5 minutes.
12. Repeat with the remaining dough.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 5 minutes per waffle
Total: 3 hours including rise time
Notes: These waffles are best enjoyed fresh and warm. Leftovers can be reheated in a toaster.
Author: deb

Title: Chocolate Pavlova
Ingredients:
1. 6 large egg whites
2. 1 1/2 cups (300 grams) granulated sugar
3. A couple pinches of sea salt
4. 1 teaspoon (5 ml) balsamic or red wine vinegar
5. 1/4 cup (20 to 25 grams) cocoa powder, the best you have, sifted
6. 2 ounces (55 grams) semi- or bittersweet chocolate, finely chopped
7. 1 1/2 cups (355 ml) heavy cream
8. 2 teaspoons (10 grams) granulated sugar
9. 1 teaspoon (5 ml) vanilla extract
10. 4 cups mixed fresh berries
11. 1 ounce (30 grams) semi- or bittersweet chocolate, to finish
Instructions:
1. Prepare your pan: Heat your oven to 350 °F. Line your largest baking sheet with a sheet of parchment paper. Draw a 9-inch circle on it with a pencil and flip the paper over so that you can see the line but it won’t get into the pavlova.
2. Make the meringue: Beat the egg whites with a mixer until satiny peaks form and then beat in the sugar a spoonful at a time until the meringue is stiff and shiny.
3. Sprinkle in the salt, sift the cocoa over the meringue, and add the vinegar and chopped chocolate. Gently fold everything with a rubber spatula until the cocoa is evenly dispersed.
4. Mound onto your parchment circle, using the circle as a guide. Smooth the top and edges.
5. Place in the oven, then immediately turn the temperature down to 300 °F. Bake for 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 hours. The pavlova is done when it looks crisp and dry on top but is still soft in the center when you poke it with a skewer.
6. Turn the oven off, leave the door slightly ajar, and let the meringue cool completely inside.
7. To finish: Whip the cream with the sugar and vanilla until soft peaks form. Pile it on top of the meringue and scatter with berries and shaved chocolate.
Time: Cook: 1 3/4 to 2 hours
Notes: This recipe was adapted from Nigella Lawson. The author uses a little more chocolate, added salt, and less sugar.
Author: deb

Title: Short Rib Cottage Pie
Ingredients:
1. 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
2. 2½ pounds boneless short ribs, small dice
3. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
4. ⅓ cup all-purpose flour
5. 2 garlic cloves, minced
6. 1 medium yellow onion, thinly sliced
7. 1 medium carrot, small dice
8. 4 ounces button mushrooms, quartered
9. 2½ cups red wine (preferably a Burgundy Pinot Noir), divided
10. 2 teaspoons chopped fresh rosemary
11. 1 teaspoon chopped fresh thyme
12. 2 pounds (6 medium) Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and cut into 2-inch pieces
13. ½ cup grated Parmesan
14. 1 teaspoon chopped fresh rosemary
15. 5 garlic cloves, finely minced
16. 1½ sticks unsalted butter, softened
Instructions:
1. Make the braised short ribs: In a 6-quart dutch oven, melt the butter over medium high heat. Cook until it begins to brown, 2 minutes, then add the short ribs and season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring as needed, until golden brown, 6 to 8 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the short ribs to a bowl and toss with the flour to coat.
2. Add the garlic, onions and carrots to the pot, and sweat until they soften, 3 to 5 minutes. Add the mushrooms and cook another 2 minutes. Pour in ½ cup of red wine, scraping the bottom to deglaze the pan. Cook until the liquid has evaporated and the vegetables begin to caramelize, 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Add the reserved short ribs, the remaining wine, the rosemary and thyme to the pot and bring to a simmer. Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until tender 45 to 55 minutes. Preheat the oven to 375º.
4. Meanwhile, make the whipped potatoes: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the potatoes until tender, 10 to 12 minutes. Drain the potatoes well and transfer to the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Whip the potatoes until smooth and no longer steaming, 5 to 7 minutes, then add the remaining ingredients and mix until incorporated.
5. Spread the braised short ribs evenly in a 9-inch pie dish. Top with the whipped potatoes and place the dish on a baking sheet. Bake until the potatoes begin to caramelize, 20 to 25 minutes. Let cool slightly, then serve.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 1.33 hours
Total: 1 hour, 40 minutes
Notes: Rich, wine-braised short ribs are topped with whipped potatoes in the ultimate comfort food recipe from Dominique Ansel.
Author: Dominique Ansel

Title: BA’s Best Eggplant Parmesan
Ingredients:
1. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
2. 1 head of garlic, cloves crushed
3. 1 large red onion, chopped
4. 3 oil-packed anchovy fillets (optional)
5. ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
6. 1 Tbsp. tomato paste
7. ¼ cup dry white wine
8. 2 (28-oz.) cans whole peeled tomatoes
9. ¼ cup torn basil leaves
10. ½ tsp. dried oregano
11. Kosher salt
12. 4 lb. Italian eggplants (about 4 medium eggplants), peeled, sliced lengthwise ½"–¾" thick
13. Kosher salt
14. 3 cups panko breadcrumbs
15. 1½ tsp. dried oregano
16. 1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
17. 1½ cups finely grated Parmesan, divided
18. 1½ cups (187.5 g) all-purpose flour
19. 5 large eggs, beaten to blend
20. 1⅓ cups extra-virgin olive oil, divided
21. ½ cup finely chopped basil and parsley, plus basil leaves for serving
22. 6 oz. low-moisture mozzarella cheese, grated (about 1⅓ cups)
23. 8 oz. fresh mozzarella cheese, thinly sliced
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°. Place a Dutch oven or other large heavy ovenproof pot over medium heat. Add ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil and 1 head of garlic, cloves crushed, and cook, stirring often, until garlic is golden, about 4 minutes.
2. Add 1 large red onion, chopped, 3 oil-packed anchovy fillets (if using), and ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes and cook, stirring often, until onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in 1 Tbsp. tomato paste and cook, stirring often, until slightly darkened, about 2 minutes.
4. Add ¼ cup dry white wine, bring to a boil, and cook until almost completely evaporated, about 1 minute.
5. Add two 28-oz. cans whole peeled tomatoes, breaking up with your hands, and their juices; add ¼ cup torn basil leaves and ½ tsp. dried oregano and stir to combine.
6. Swirl 1½ cups water into one tomato can, then the other, to rinse, and add to pot; season with kosher salt.
7. Transfer pot to oven; roast sauce, stirring halfway through, until thick and tomatoes are browned on top and around edges of pot, 2–2½ hours.
8. Let sauce cool slightly. Pass through the large holes of a food mill or process in a food processor until mostly smooth. Taste and season with salt.
9. Lightly season 4 lb. Italian eggplants (about 4 medium eggplants), peeled, sliced lengthwise ½"–¾" thick, all over with kosher salt; place in a single layer on several layers of paper towels inside a rimmed baking sheet.
10. Top with another layer of paper towels and more slices; repeat as needed. Top with a final layer of paper towels, then another rimmed baking sheet; weigh down with a heavy pot. Let sliced eggplant sit until it has released excess liquid, 45–60 minutes.
11. Meanwhile, pulse 3 cups panko breadcrumbs, 1½ tsp. dried oregano, 1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper, and ¾ cup finely grated Parmesan cheese in a food processor until very finely ground. Transfer to a shallow bowl.
12. Preheat oven to 350°. Place 1½ cups (187.5 g) all-purpose flour in another shallow bowl and 5 large eggs, beaten to blend, in a third shallow bowl.
13. Working one at a time, dredge eggplant slices in flour, then dip in egg, allowing excess to drip off. Coat in breadcrumb mixture, packing all around, then shaking off excess.
Time: Total: 4 hours 30 minutes
Notes: The homemade marinara sauce makes all the difference, so don’t swap it out for store-bought. This one is rich with tomato, wine, tons of garlic, and anchovies (the secret to everything) and can be made up to two days ahead.
Author: Claire Saffitz

Title: Best Loaded Cauliflower Bake
Ingredients:
1. 2 small heads cauliflower, cut into florets
2. 2 Tbsp. butter
3. 3 cloves garlic, minced
4. 3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour
5. 2 cups whole milk
6. 2 oz. cream cheese, softened
7. 1 1/2 cups shredded cheddar, divided
8. Kosher salt
9. Freshly ground black pepper
10. 6 slices bacon, cooked and crumbled
11. 1/4 cup sliced green onions
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°. In a large pot of salted boiling water, blanch cauliflower, 3 minutes. Drain and squeeze cauliflower of water.
2. Make cheese sauce: In a large skillet, melt butter. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute, then add flour and stir until golden, 2 minutes. Add milk and bring to a low simmer, then add cream cheese, whisking until combined. Remove from heat and stir in 1 cup cheddar until melted, then season with salt and pepper.
3. In a 9”-x-13” dish, add drained cauliflower. Pour over cheese sauce and stir until combined. Stir in all but 1 tablespoon each cooked bacon and green onions until combined, then top with remaining cheddar, bacon, and green onions.
4. Bake until cauliflower is tender and cheese is melty, 30 minutes.
Time:
Prep: 15 mins
Cook: 45 mins
Total: 1 hr
Notes: You definitely want to cook the cauliflower a little first so that it doesn't take forever in the oven. Blanch it for around 3 minutes before draining it of any excess water.
Author: Lindsay Funston

Title: Pork Posole Recipe
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup all-purpose flour
2. ½ tablespoon kosher salt, plus more to taste
3. ½ tablespoon freshly ground black pepper
4. 1 teaspoon ground cumin
5. 3 pounds pork shoulder, cut into 3-inch pieces
6. 3 tablespoons lard, divided
7. 3 tablespoons olive oil, divided
8. 4 garlic cloves, minced
9. 1 yellow onion, finely chopped
10. 4 cups water
11. 1½ tablespoons minced oregano
12. ¾ tablespoon cumin seeds, toasted
13. 2 bay leaves
14. 1½ cups fresh corn kernels
15. 1 pound (6 medium) tomatillos
16. 2¼ cups canned hominy, rinsed
17. 1 cup roasted red peppers, diced
18. 1 jalapeno—stemmed, seeded and minced
19. Crumbled cotija cheese, for garnish
20. Cilantro leaves, for garnish
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350º. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, pepper and cumin. Toss the pork shoulder in the flour to dredge, shaking off any excess flour.
2. In a 6-quart Dutch oven, heat 1½ tablespoons of the lard and 2 tablespoons of the olive oil over medium heat. Working in 2 batches, sear the pork, turning as needed until golden brown, 7 to 10 minutes per batch. Watch the pork carefully to make sure any excess flour in the pan doesn't burn. Once seared, transfer to a plate lined with paper towels.
3. To the pot, add the remaining lard, the garlic and onions. Cook until translucent, 2 to 3 minutes. Return the pork to the pan with the water, oregano, cumin seeds and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a very low simmer and cook, covered, over low heat until the pork is tender, 2 hours.
4. Meanwhile, on a baking sheet toss the corn with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Bake until golden brown, 12 to 14 minutes, then set aside.
5. Once the pork has simmered for 2 hours, add the roasted corn, tomatillos, hominy, roasted red peppers and jalapeño. Return to a low simmer and cook, covered, until the pork begins to fall apart, 1 hour more.
6. Using 2 forks, shred the pork into bite-sized pieces. Ladle the posole into bowls and garnish with the cotija cheese and cilantro leaves, then serve.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 3.33 hours
Total: 3 hours, 40 minutes
Notes: Recipe adapted from 'Big Bad Breakfast,' by John Currence
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: BA's Best Guacamole
Ingredients:
1. 3 large ripe Hass avocados
2. ¼ white onion, finely chopped
3. 1 serrano chile, seeds removed if desired, finely chopped
4. 1 garlic clove, finely grated
5. 2 tablespoons (or more) fresh lime juice
6. 1 teaspoon kosher salt
7. ¼ cup chopped cilantro, plus more for serving
8. Toasted pepitas (pumpkin seeds; optional) and chips (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Smash avocados with a potato masher or fork in a medium bowl or with a molcajete (a Mexican mortar and pestle) until very coarsely mashed.
2. Mix in onion, chile, garlic, lime juice, salt, and ¼ cup cilantro.
3. Taste and add more lime juice, if desired.
4. Top with pepitas (if using) and more cilantro; serve with chips alongside.
Notes: This recipe was originally published in Bon Appétit's October 2016 issue.
Author: Rick Martinez

Title: Mille-Crepe Tiramisu Birthday Cake
Ingredients:
1. ¾ cup plus 2 tablespoons cake flour
2. ¾ cup plus 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour
3. ¼ cup granulated sugar
4. 2 teaspoons kosher salt
5. 4 large eggs
6. 5 large egg yolks
7. 1 stick (8 tablespoons) unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly
8. 2 cups whole milk
9. 3 tablespoons Armagnac or cognac
10. 1¼ cups confectioners' sugar
11. 4 cups mascarpone cheese
12. ½ cup sweet Marsala wine (or half as much rum or coffee liqueur)
13. ¼ cup natural cocoa powder
Instructions:
1. Make the crepe batter: In a fine-mesh sieve set over a medium bowl, sift together the cake flour, all-purpose flour and sugar. Whisk in the salt. In another medium bowl, whisk together the eggs and egg yolks. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and whisk until smooth and webby (it will be very thick). In a slow, steady stream, whisk in the butter, then the milk and the Armagnac, until the batter is smooth (if there are any lumps, strain the batter through a fine-mesh sieve and into a medium bowl). Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Set an 8-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Gently stir the crêpe batter, then pour ¼ cup of batter into the skillet. Holding the skillet by the handle, tilt and turn the skillet to quickly disperse the batter. Cook the crepe until the underside is golden-brown, about 1½ minutes. Lightly jerk the skillet to loosen the crepe, then flip the crepe over using a spatula, a more vigorous jerking motion or your fingers. Cook on the other side until golden-brown, about 30 seconds, then slide the crepe onto the parchment-paper-lined baking sheet. Repeat until all of the batter is used (you'll end up with about 20 crepes total, and will need 18 for the cake). Refrigerate the crepes on the baking sheet until completely chilled, at least 2 hours or overnight.
3. Make the tiramisu filling: In a fine-mesh sieve set over the bowl of a stand mixer, sift the confectioners' sugar. Add the mascarpone cheese and the Marsala wine. Use the paddle attachment to combine the mixture on low speed until well combined.
4. Remove the crepes from the refrigerator. Add 1 crepe to the bottom of an 8-inch springform pan so it lies flat. Add ¼ cup of the tiramisu filling to the center of the crepe and use a small offset spatula to evenly spread the filling over the crêpe. Repeat with the filling and 17 more crêpes. Refrigerate the leftover filling; you'll use it to finish the cake. Chill the cake in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours or overnight.
5. Remove the cake from the refrigerator, undo the springform pan and set it on a cake plate or platter. Spread the remaining tiramisu cream on top of the cake. Add the cocoa powder to a fine-mesh sieve and sprinkle it over the top of the cake. Slice and serve.
Notes: The recipe gives an illusion of complexity with its many layers.
Author: Tasting Table Staff

Title: Skillet-Roasted Lemon Chicken
Ingredients:
1. 2 teaspoons fresh thyme leaves
2. 1 teaspoon whole fennel seeds
3. kosher salt, to taste
4. freshly ground black pepper, to taste
5. ⅓ cup good olive oil
6. 1 lemon, halved and sliced ¼ inch thick
7. 1 yellow onion, halved and sliced ¼ inch thick
8. 2 large garlic cloves, thinly sliced
9. 1 (4-pound) chicken, backbone removed and butterflied
10. ½ cup dry white wine, such as Pinot Grigio
11. juice of 1 lemon
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450 F.
2. Place the thyme, fennel seeds, 1 tablespoon salt, and 1 teaspoon pepper in a mini food processor and process until ground.
3. Pour the olive oil into a small glass measuring cup, stir in the herb mixture, and set aside.
4. Distribute the lemon slices in a 12-inch cast-iron skillet and distribute the onion and garlic on top. Place the chicken, skin side down, on top of the onion and brush with about half the oil and herb mixture. Turn the chicken skin side up, pat it dry with paper towels (very important!), and brush it all over with the rest of the oil and herb mixture.
5. Roast the chicken for 30 minutes.
6. Pour the wine into the pan (not on the chicken!) and roast for another 10 to 15 minutes, until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the breast registers 155 to 160 F.
7. Remove the chicken from the oven, sprinkle it with the lemon juice, cover the skillet tightly with aluminum foil, and allow to rest for 10 to 15 minutes.
8. Cut the chicken into quarters or eighths, sprinkle with salt, and serve hot with the pan juices, cooked lemon, and onion.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 40 minutes
Total: 55 minutes
Notes: Garnish with minced fresh rosemary before resting. You can also assemble the chicken in advance in the pan then refrigerate to save time on the day of serving.
Author: Ina Garten

Title: Pico de Gallo Verde
Ingredients:
1. 4 medium Persian cucumbers, finely chopped
2. 1 avocado, finely chopped
3. 1 celery stalk, finely chopped, plus ¼ cup finely chopped celery leaves
4. 2 scallions, green parts only, thinly sliced
5. 1 small poblano chile, seeds removed, finely chopped
6. 1 jalapeño, seeds removed, finely chopped
7. 1 garlic clove, finely grated
8. ¼ cup finely chopped unsalted, roasted pistachios
9. ¼ cup fresh lime juice
10. 2 tablespoons finely chopped cilantro
11. 2 tablespoons finely chopped mint
12. Olive oil (for drizzling)
13. Kosher salt
14. Tortilla chips or plantain chips (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Toss cucumbers, avocado, celery and celery leaves, scallions, poblano, jalapeño, garlic, pistachios, lime juice, cilantro, and mint in a large bowl. (Mixture should be coated just enough to look glossy.)
2. Drizzle with oil, season with salt, and toss again.
3. Cover salsa and chill 20 minutes before serving with chips.
Notes: The lime juice and oil will keep the avocado from turning brown, but it’s a good idea to make this salsa right before you serve it.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: White Lasagna With Sausage And Ricotta
Ingredients:
1. Uncooked Italian sausage
2. Baby spinach
3. Yellow onion
4. Fresh thyme
5. Fresh sage
6. Crushed red pepper flakes
7. White wine
8. Balsamic glaze
9. Unsalted butter
10. Flour
11. Whole milk
12. Whole milk ricotta
13. Salt
14. Pepper
15. Nutmeg
16. Garlic powder
17. Finely grated Parmesan
18. Shredded mozzarella
19. Lasagna noodles
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 F.
2. Remove the sausage from its casing and cook in a non-stick pan, breaking it up with a wooden spoon. Once cooked, remove from pan and chop into smaller pieces if needed.
3. In the same pan, melt butter with the sausage fat. Add thinly sliced onions, fresh herbs, and red pepper flakes. Cook until onions are golden brown.
4. Add white wine to the onions, bring to a boil, and let it evaporate. Stir in balsamic glaze and remove onions from the pan.
5. Place spinach in the pan and cook until wilted.
6. For the béchamel, melt butter and add flour to make a blonde roux. Slowly whisk in milk until smooth.
7. Remove béchamel from heat and fold in ricotta, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and garlic powder.
8. Spread a quarter of the ricotta béchamel over the bottom of a baking dish. Top with a layer of lasagna sheets.
9. Add a layer of cooked sausage, caramelized onions, and spinach. Sprinkle with Parmesan and mozzarella.
10. Repeat layers, ending with béchamel and cheese on top.
11. Bake in the oven until golden and bubbly.
Notes: Lasagna is delicious on its own or served with Caesar salad.
Author: Ali Fagan

Title: Classic Tiramisu Recipe
Ingredients:
1. 2 egg yolks
2. ¼ cup sugar
3. 1 cup mascarpone, softened
4. ¾ cup heavy cream
5. 10 ladyfingers
6. 1 cup strong brewed coffee or espresso at room temperature
7. 2 tablespoons unsweetened cacao or cocoa powder
Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, whisk the egg yolks with sugar until pale and fluffy.
2. Mix mascarpone into the egg mixture until smooth.
3. In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream to form stiff peaks then fold it into the mascarpone mixture.
4. Dip the ladyfingers into the cold coffee and layer them in an 8x5-inch serving dish that is 2 inches in height.
5. Cover the ladyfingers with the mascarpone cream and smooth out the top.
6. Refrigerate the tiramisu for at least 6 hours.
7. Dust the chilled tiramisu with cacao powder before serving.
Time:
Prep: 6 hours, 20 minutes
Cook: 0 minutes
Total: 6 hours, 20 minutes
Notes: Tiramisu is best eaten within a day or two, although it will last up to four days in the fridge. If you can't eat it all within that time frame, you can also wrap it tightly in plastic wrap and stick it in the freezer. In this case, freeze it before sprinkling on the cacao powder.
Author: Eric Ngo and Tasting Table Staff

Title: Manicotti
Ingredients:
1. 6 large eggs
2. 1¼ (156 g) cups all-purpose flour
3. ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt
4. Nonstick vegetable oil spray
5. 2 large egg yolks, beaten to blend
6. 1½ lb. whole-milk ricotta
7. 8 oz. low-moisture mozzarella, grated
8. 1 oz. Parmesan, grated, plus more for serving
9. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
10. 2 10-oz. boxes frozen chopped spinach, thawed, drained (optional)
11. 3 cups marinara sauce, preferably homemade, divided
12. Extra-virgin olive oil for serving (optional)
Instructions:
1. Using an electric mixer on high speed, beat 6 large eggs in a large bowl until light, airy, pale in color, and can nearly hold peaks, 6–8 minutes.
2. Reduce mixer speed to low. Gradually add 1¼ (156 g) cups all-purpose flour and ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, beating well between additions, until no clumps of flour remain. With mixer speed on low, slowly pour in 1½ cups water and continue to beat until well incorporated. Cover and let batter rest at least 2 hours.
3. Heat a small nonstick skillet over medium and spray with nonstick vegetable oil spray. Pour 2 Tbsp. batter into skillet and cook, swirling skillet constantly to encourage crepe to form a nice round shape, until set, 15–20 seconds. Tap out crepe onto a clean towel; gently flatten any creases. Repeat with remaining batter.
4. Preheat oven to 350°. Mix 2 large egg yolks, beaten to blend, 1½ lb. ricotta, 8 oz. mozzarella, grated, and 1 oz. Parmesan, grated, in a large bowl; season with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Squeeze out excess liquid from two 10-oz. boxes frozen chopped spinach, thawed, drained (if using), and add to egg mixture; toss to coat.
5. Spread 1 cup marinara sauce, preferably homemade, in a 13x9" baking dish. Working one at a time, spoon 2 Tbsp. cheese mixture in center of crepe, spreading all the way to the ends. Roll up crepe, then flatten slightly with your hands. Transfer seam side down to prepared dish. Working in batches if needed, repeat with remaining crepes and filling, leaving a small gap in between each. Top with remaining sauce (2 cups marinara) and cover with foil.
6. Bake manicotti until cheese is melted and sauce is bubbling, 35–45 minutes. Let manicotti cool slightly in dish to set, about 15 minutes. Top with a drizzle of extra-virgin olive oil (if using), Parmesan, and freshly ground black pepper before serving.
Time: Total: 3 hours 30 minutes
Notes: Making crespelle may take a bit of getting used to, so don’t be discouraged if the first few tries aren’t perfect. The work is easily split up over two days: On the first, make the ricotta mixture and the pasta sauce and refrigerate both. On the second, make the crespelle and assemble. Double the recipe for maximum efficiency, then pop one in the freezer to bake a few weeks later.
Author: Brad Leone

Title: Herby Avocado Hummus
Notes: The webpage primarily discusses a taste test of tortilla chips rather than providing a specific recipe. The mention of 'Herby Avocado Hummus' suggests a recipe, but no details are provided.
Author: Ashley Mason
