Add 1 cup basil and toss to combine. Arrange on one side of a large platter. Cover lettuce and herbs with damp paper towels and chill until ready to use.
7. Rinse 1 bunch radishes, trim, and slice in half (or into ½" wedges if large). Transfer to a medium bowl. Tear 1 cup whole peperoncini into bowl. Mix in 1 Tbsp. dressing; season with salt. Let sit at room temperature until ready to use.
8. Slice chicken into ¼"-thick slices. It might be the tiiiiniest bit pink inside, which is okay!
9. Halve 1 avocado, remove pit, and thinly slice. Fan out slices.
10. Remove platter with lettuce from fridge. Arrange green beans, radish salad, avocado, and chicken separately on platter. Drizzle more dressing over and serve remaining dressing alongside.
Notes: Properly-poached chicken breast is juicy, tender, and a blank canvas for big flavors. The trick is to cook the chicken very low and slow.
Author: Kat Boytsova

Title: Zha Jiang Mian Recipe
Ingredients:
1. shallots
2. garlic
3. ground pork
4. hoisin sauce
5. oyster sauce
6. dark soy sauce
7. sugar
8. noodles
9. cucumbers
Instructions:
1. Fry the aromatics. Fry some shallots and garlic in oil until soft and fragrant.
2. Cook the pork. Add the ground pork and cook it, breaking up, until browned.
3. Fry the sauces. Add hoisin, oyster, dark soy sauce, sugar, and a bit of water and simmer until the sauce thickens and all the flavors meld.
4. Mix. While the sauce is doing its thing, cook your noodles and drain well. When the sauce is ready, mix a generous portion with the noodles.
5. Enjoy. Slurp and enjoy.
Time:
Cook: 15 minutes
Total: 30 minutes or less
Notes: The sauce keeps well in the fridge, allowing for quick preparation of future meals.
Author: Stephanie

Title: Clams With Spicy Tomato Broth and Garlic Mayo
Ingredients:
1. ½ lemon
2. 5 garlic cloves, 1 whole, 4 thinly sliced
3. ½ cup mayonnaise
4. Kosher salt
5. ¼ cup plus 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
6. 2 large shallots, thinly sliced
7. 1 red chile (such as Holland or Fresno), thinly sliced, or ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
8. 2 Tbsp. tomato paste
9. 2 cups cherry tomatoes
10. 1 cup dry white wine
11. 36 littleneck clams, scrubbed
12. 6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces
13. 3 Tbsp. finely chopped chives
14. 4 thick slices country-style bread
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for medium heat. Finely grate zest from lemon half into a small bowl, then squeeze in juice. Finely grate whole garlic clove into bowl and mix in mayonnaise. Season garlic mayo with salt and set aside.
2. Place a large cast-iron skillet on grill and heat ¼ cup oil in skillet. Add sliced garlic, shallots, and chile and cook, stirring often, until just softened, about 2 minutes. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring often, until paste darkens slightly, about 1 minute. Add tomatoes and a pinch of salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes soften and release their juices, about 4 minutes. Add wine and cook until it is almost reduced by half and no longer smells boozy, about 3 minutes.
3. Add clams and butter to skillet and cover (if you don’t have a lid that fits, use a sheet of foil). Cook until clams have opened, 6–10 minutes, depending on size of clams and heat level. Remove skillet from grill; discard any clams that don’t open. Sprinkle with chives.
4. Meanwhile, drizzle bread with remaining 3 Tbsp. oil and season lightly with salt. Grill until golden brown and crisp, about 3 minutes per side.
5. Serve clams with toasted bread and reserved garlic mayo.
Notes: If you don't have a grill, don't sweat it: Just heat the cast-iron skillet over medium on your stovetop. Inside or out, you’ll still have buttery clams to enjoy.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Ramen Noodles With Miso Pesto
Ingredients:
1. 4 cups baby spinach
2. 2 cups cilantro leaves with tender stems
3. 1 Tbsp. white miso
4. 1 garlic clove
5. ½ cup grapeseed or sunflower oil
6. 1 tsp. toasted sesame oil
7. 1 tsp. fresh lemon juice
8. Kosher salt
9. 2 5-oz. packages fresh ramen noodles
10. 1 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
11. Toasted sesame seeds (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Bring a medium pot of water to a boil.
2. Meanwhile, purée spinach, cilantro, miso, garlic, grapeseed oil, sesame oil, and lemon juice in a blender until mixture is smooth and very green. Season with salt and pour pesto into a medium bowl.
3. Cook noodles according to package directions. Drain and add to bowl with pesto. Add butter and toss until butter is melted and noodles are coated in sauce.
4. Divide noodles between bowls and top with sesame seeds.
5. Pesto can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Color might darken slightly.
Notes: Pesto can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Color might darken slightly.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Cucumber and Onion Salad
Ingredients:
1. 6 Persian cucumbers, thinly sliced
2. ½ small red onion, thinly sliced
3. 1 tsp. kosher salt, plus more
4. ¼ cup unseasoned rice vinegar
5. 2 tsp. sugar
6. Freshly ground black pepper
7. Fresh mint leaves (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Toss cucumbers, onion, and 1 tsp. salt in a medium bowl.
2. Let sit until cucumbers begin to release liquid.
3. Gently press down on cucumbers to force out more liquid, being careful not to break apart.
4. Remove from bowl, shaking off liquid, and place vegetables in a clean bowl.
5. Toss with vinegar and sugar; season with salt and pepper.
6. Top with mint.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: Not specified
Total: Not specified
Notes: Cool, crunchy, and a bit sweet, this salad is meant to quench the fire from all the other dishes on the table.
Author: Tom Cunanan

Title: Lentil Burgers
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup dried green or brown lentils
2. ½ cup whole-milk Greek yogurt
3. ¼ tsp. finely grated lemon zest
4. 1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
5. 2 garlic cloves, finely grated, divided
6. Kosher salt
7. 6 oz. crimini mushrooms, stems trimmed
8. 2 Tbsp. white miso
9. ¼ tsp. smoked paprika
10. 4 Tbsp. (or more) extra-virgin olive oil, divided
11. ¼ cup (or more) oat, gluten-free 1:1 blend, or all-purpose flour
12. Whole wheat buns, sprouts and/or lettuce, and sliced pickles (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Pour water into a large saucepan to cover lentils by 4". Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender but not mushy, about 10 minutes from the time the water reaches a boil. Drain very well in a fine-mesh sieve, then let cool at least 10 minutes (spread them out on a baking sheet, which will help them cool faster).
2. Meanwhile, combine yogurt, lemon zest, lemon juice, and half of garlic in a small bowl. Season with salt; set aside.
3. Finely chop mushrooms and transfer to a medium bowl. Add miso, paprika, 2 Tbsp. oil, and remaining garlic clove. Add lentils (you should have 2 cups cooked) and stir and mash them with the back of a spoon or a potato masher until lentils are partly mashed but with lots of whole lentils still remaining. Vigorously stir in flour until mixture holds together when squeezed; if it doesn’t, continue to mash until it does and add 1–2 Tbsp. flour if needed. Form into 6 patties about ¾" thick.
4. Working in 2 batches, heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet (nonstick is essential since these will definitely stick to a regular skillet) over medium. Cook patties until deeply browned and very crisp on bottom side, about 3 minutes. Carefully turn and repeat on second side, adding more oil as needed to maintain a light coating around patties in skillet.
5. Spread reserved yogurt mixture on buns. Top with burgers, sprouts, and pickles.
6. Patties can be made 4 days ahead. Tightly wrap in plastic and chill.
Notes: Patties can be made 4 days ahead. Tightly wrap in plastic and chill.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Spicy Chicken Lettuce Wraps
Ingredients:
1. 2 Tbsp. soy sauce
2. 1 Tbsp. dark brown sugar
3. 1 tsp. fish sauce
4. 1 Tbsp. sambal oelek or Sriracha, plus more for serving
5. 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil
6. 3 scallions, white and pale green parts only, thinly sliced
7. 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped
8. 1 lb. ground chicken
9. Kosher salt
10. Bibb lettuce leaves, lime wedges, and tender herbs (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Mix soy sauce, sambal oelek, brown sugar, and fish sauce in a small bowl; set aside.
2. Heat oil in a skillet over medium. Add scallions and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened (a little color is okay), about 3 minutes. Add chicken and lightly season with salt. Cook, breaking up with a wooden spoon and tossing occasionally, until chicken is cooked through, 5–7 minutes. Add reserved soy sauce mixture and cook, tossing occasionally, until liquid is almost completely reduced, about 2 minutes.
3. Serve chicken mixture with lettuce, herbs, lime wedges, and more sambal oelek for making lettuce wraps.
Time: Cook: 10-12 minutes
Notes: Low investment and high reward, let this not-so-traditional larb become your next late-night go-to meal.
Author: Christina Chaey

Title: Grilled Shrimp with Turmeric Mojo Sauce
Ingredients:
1. 6 garlic cloves, smashed
2. 2 habanero chiles, seeds removed, chopped
3. 1 (3") piece ginger, peeled, thinly sliced
4. 1/3 cup fresh lime juice
5. 1/3 cup fresh orange juice
6. 1 Tbsp. sugar
7. 2 tsp. unseasoned rice vinegar
8. 1/2 tsp. ground turmeric
9. 1/3 cup vegetable oil, plus more for grill
10. Kosher salt
11. 1 1/2 lb. large shrimp, peeled, deveined
12. Cooked short-grain white rice (for serving; optional)
13. Flaky sea salt
Instructions:
1. Prepare a grill for medium-high heat. Pulse garlic, chiles, ginger, lime juice, orange juice, sugar, vinegar, and turmeric in a food processor until combined and almost smooth. With the motor running, gradually stream in 1/3 cup oil and process until emulsified.
2. Pour half of the sauce into a small bowl; season with kosher salt. Set aside for serving. Transfer remaining sauce to a medium bowl and add shrimp. Season with kosher salt and toss to coat.
3. Clean and oil grate, then immediately arrange shrimp on grill. Grill until bright pink and lightly charred, about 1 minute per side. Divide shrimp among bowls. Spoon reserved sauce over; sprinkle with sea salt. Serve with rice if desired.
Notes: Shrimp can take on the flavors of a bright, acidic marinade in minutes, making it a good choice for last-minute grilling. Do not let shrimp sit in the marinade for too long because the acid in the citrus will start to firm up and cook the flesh.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Always-Great Homemade Salsa
Ingredients:
1. 2 lb. tomatoes, cored, cut in half crosswise
2. 1 medium white onion, sliced into ¼"-thick rounds
3. 3 serrano chiles or small jalapeño peppers
4. 3 garlic cloves, unpeeled
5. Kosher salt
6. 2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice
7. 2 Tbsp. finely chopped fresh cilantro
8. Tortilla chips (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Heat broiler. Place 2 lb. tomatoes, cored, cut in half crosswise, cut side down on a foil-lined rimmed baking sheet. Fit 1 medium white onion, sliced into ¼"-thick rounds, 3 serrano chiles, and 3 garlic cloves, unpeeled, around tomatoes so everything is snug but not overlapping. Broil, turning onion and chiles once, until lightly charred, about 6 minutes for chiles and garlic and 15–18 minutes for tomatoes and onion.
2. Peel garlic and place in a food processor along with half of tomatoes. Pulse until very smooth. Add remaining tomatoes and pulse until tomatoes are mostly broken up but mixture still has some texture. Transfer to a medium bowl. Finely chop onion and chiles and mix into purée; season with kosher salt. Let cool. Stir in 2 Tbsp. fresh lime juice and 2 Tbsp. finely chopped cilantro. Season salsa with more salt if needed. Serve with tortilla chips.
Time: Total: 30 minutes
Notes: This fresh salsa recipe was first printed in our August 2019 issue as “The Only Salsa You Need.”
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Nut Free Keto Bread
Ingredients:
1. 2 Cups Sesame Seed Flour (Ground up sesame seeds work best) 200g / 7 oz
2. 7 Large Eggs
3. 1/2 Cup unsalted butter, melted 120g / 4 oz
4. 1 teaspoon baking powder
5. 1/2 tsp salt
Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 180C (355F).
2. In a large bowl, separate the egg yolks from the egg whites, beating the egg whites with an electric mixer until white and fluffy.
3. In another bowl, mix together the melted butter, egg yolks, sesame seed flour, salt and baking powder.
4. Gently fold the now complete egg yolk mixture into the egg whites, and gently mix together until it forms a uniform colour.
5. In a bread loaf tin lined with baking paper, pour the mixture in and bake for 45 mins, checking the centre of the loaf with a skewer to ensure that it's done.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 45 minutes
Total: 55 minutes
Notes: For a dairy-free version, replace butter with 1/2 cup olive oil.
Author: FatForWeightLoss (Aaron Day)

Title: Fettuccini with Zucchini, Garlic Scapes, and Lemon
Ingredients:
1. 4 small zucchini, julienned
2. 1 pound fettuccine
3. 3 tablespoons olive oil
4. 10 cloves garlic, peeled and finely chopped
5. 1 1/2 teaspoons crushed red pepper flakes, plus more for garnish
6. 1 bunch garlic scapes, thinly sliced
7. 1/4 cup Pecorino Romano cheese, plus more for serving
8. Juice and zest of 1 lemon
9. Kosher salt
10. 1/4 cup bread crumbs
11. 4 sprigs mint, leaves only, torn
12. Freshly cracked black pepper
Instructions:
1. With a mandoline adjusted to a thick setting and fit with a medium julienne tool, slice the zucchini, avoiding the seedy core. Be sure to cut only the green meaty part, stopping on each side before you get to the seeds. Reserve the seedy core of the zucchini for another use.
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and season generously with salt. Place the pasta in the water and cook until al dente, 7 to 8 minutes (2 to 3 minutes if using fresh pasta).
3. As the pasta cooks, warm the olive oil in a large sauté pan over low heat. Add the garlic and sweat until fragrant but without color, about 1 minute. Stir in the red pepper flakes.
4. Use tongs to transfer the pasta to the sauté pan. Add 1 cup pasta water, the garlic scapes, and zucchini. Toss over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes, until the fettuccine has absorbed the sauce and the zucchini has softened slightly.
5. Add the cheese and toss once more. If needed, add more pasta water to achieve desired consistency.
Time: Cook: 10-15 minutes
Notes: Garlic scapes offer a delicate, sweeter garlic flavor, while garlic cloves are more pungent. Adding the scapes at the end keeps them crunchy and bright, and builds textural dimension in the dish.
Author: Missy Robbins

Title: Brabant Potatoes (Crispy Garlic Butter Potatoes)
Ingredients:
1. Potatoes
2. Salt
3. Oil
4. Butter
5. Garlic
6. Parsley
7. Black pepper
Instructions:
1. Cut the potatoes into roughly ½ inch cubes. No need to peel them.
2. Boil the potatoes in heavily salted water for 5 minutes until softened but not fully cooked.
3. Fry the potatoes in a skillet until they start to become crispy.
4. Transfer the skillet to the oven to finish cooking the potatoes until they are crispy and golden.
5. Prepare the garlic butter by starting with a cold pan. Melt the butter slowly and cook the garlic gently.
6. Allow the garlic butter to cool slightly before adding freshly chopped parsley.
7. Once the potatoes are fully cooked and crispy, finish them with melted garlic butter, salt, and black pepper.
Time:
Prep: 5 minutes
Cook: Varies based on frying and baking time
Total: Varies
Notes: Some people add green onions to theirs but the recipe prefers just the taste of the garlic butter. The garlic butter can be made up to 24 hours in advance and kept in the refrigerator.
Author: Claire | Sprinkles and Sprouts

Title: Tomato-and-Cheese Cobbler
Ingredients:
1. 2 1/2 lb. mixed heirloom tomatoes, cored, sliced 1/4" thick
2. 1 Tbsp. plus 1/2 tsp. kosher salt
3. 2 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
4. 1 1/2 tsp. baking powder
5. 1/2 tsp. baking soda
6. 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for surface
7. 5 Tbsp. cold unsalted butter, cut into 1/2" pieces
8. 2/3 cup buttermilk
9. 1/4 cup finely chopped chives
10. 4 oz. coarsely grated sharp cheddar (about 1 1/2 cups), divided
11. 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
12. 6 sprigs thyme
13. 1 large egg
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Gently toss tomato slices with 1 Tbsp. salt in a medium bowl, then arrange in an even layer on paper towels. Let sit until most of the juices are released, about 15 minutes. Pat tomatoes dry with more paper towels.
2. Meanwhile, whisk pepper, baking powder, baking soda, 1 1/2 cups flour, and 1/2 tsp. salt in a large bowl to combine. Cut in butter with a pastry cutter or 2 butter knives until pea-size pieces form. Stir in buttermilk, chives, and 3/4 cup cheese with a wooden spoon until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Turn out dough onto a lightly floured work surface. Gently knead 2–3 times until dough just comes together, being careful not to overwork. Roll out dough to 3/4" thick. Cut with a 2" flour-dipped biscuit cutter or small glass. Transfer to a plate or baking sheet and chill until ready to use.
4. Pour oil into a large deep heatproof skillet or braising dish. Arrange tomato slices in even layers in pan (you’ll have about 3 layers); top with thyme. Roast until tomato juices start to bubble, 15–20 minutes.
5. Discard thyme sprigs. Flatten tomatoes into an even layer with a spatula, then sprinkle with remaining 3/4 cup cheese. Arrange biscuits over, spacing about 1" apart. Whisk egg and 1 Tbsp. water in a small bowl. Brush biscuits with egg wash. Bake until biscuits are golden brown and firm to the touch, 18–20 minutes. Let cool 5–10 minutes before serving.
6. Dough can be formed into biscuits 8 hours ahead. Keep chilled.
Time: Total: 1 hour
Notes: Salting the tomatoes for 15 minutes before roasting seasons them properly and helps remove any excess liquid that could potentially waterlog the filling.
Author: Anna Stockwell

Title: Tomato Salad with Warm Basil Dressing
Ingredients:
1. ½ cup extra-virgin olive oil
2. 1 large shallot, thinly sliced into rings
3. 3 garlic cloves, thinly sliced
4. ¾ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
5. 3 oil-packed anchovy fillets, chopped (optional)
6. 1 cup basil leaves (purple or green)
7. 1½ lb. heirloom tomatoes, some sliced, some cut into wedges
8. 2 Tbsp. red wine vinegar
9. Kosher salt
10. ½ lemon
11. Flaky sea salt
Instructions:
1. Cook oil, shallot, garlic, and red pepper flakes in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until shallots and garlic begin to turn golden, 7–9 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in anchovies, if using (they’ll dissolve in the oil quickly), and basil.
2. Toss tomatoes with vinegar and a pinch of kosher salt in a medium bowl. Transfer to a platter and pour warm dressing over. Finely grate lemon zest on top and sprinkle with sea salt.
Time: Cook: 7–9 minutes
Notes: Not just for salad: Drizzle this savory infused oil over grilled vegetables, steak, or roast chicken.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Next-Level Turkey Burgers with Lemon Aioli
Ingredients:
1. 8 cups warm water
2. 2/3 cup plus 1 teaspoon kosher salt, divided, plus more
3. 1 tablespoon light brown sugar
4. 1 tablespoon lemon zest plus 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice (from 2 lemons), divided
Time:
Prep: 1 hr 10 mins
Total: 2 hrs 40 mins
Notes: A quick brine produces super-moist burgers with chin-dripping juices. The brining also helps the burgers cook faster, so flip them often to help them cook evenly and prevent charring.
Author: Dan Kluger

Title: Green Goddess Crunch Sandwich
Ingredients:
1. ¼ cup finely chopped chives
2. ¼ cup mayonnaise
3. ¼ cup tarragon
4. ¼ cup whole-milk Greek yogurt
5. 1 lemon, halved
6. 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
7. Kosher salt, freshly ground pepper
8. ½ head of butter or Bibb lettuce (about 2 ounces), large leaves torn
9. ¼ English hothouse cucumber, thinly sliced
10. 1 avocado, thinly sliced
11. 4 slices grainy bread
12. 8 ounces fresh mozzarella, sliced ¼ inch thick
13. 2 cups sprouts (such as broccoli sprouts or alfalfa)
Instructions:
1. Place chives, mayonnaise, tarragon, and yogurt in a blender. Add zest and juice of 1 lemon half, then add 2 Tbsp. oil; season with salt and pepper. Purée until smooth.
2. Squeeze juice from remaining lemon half over lettuce and cucumbers in a medium bowl. Drizzle with oil; season with salt and pepper. Toss to coat.
3. Divide and smash avocado between 2 slices of bread; season with salt and pepper. Arrange lettuce and cucumbers over. Top with mozzarella and sprouts. Spread remaining 2 slices of bread with ¼ cup green goddess dressing and close sandwiches.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Spaghetti with Crab and Zucchini
Ingredients:
1. 1/2 pound picked crab
2. 1 jalapeño, seeded and minced
3. 3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
4. 1/4 cup loosely packed mint leaves
5. 2 medium summer squash (yellow or zucchini)
6. 1 tablespoon red wine vinegar
7. 1/4 teaspoon minced garlic
8. Kosher salt
9. Freshly ground black pepper
10. 1/3 cup loosely packed basil leaves, cut in slivers
11. 12 ounces thick spaghetti or bucatini
12. Juice of 1/2 lemon
Instructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat.
2. Combine the crab and jalapeño in a small bowl. Add 1 tablespoon of oil. Chop half the mint and add that to the crab. Mix well and set aside.
3. Sliver the remaining mint and put that in a second bowl.
4. Trim the squash, then grate them using a large-holed grater, stopping short of the seedy core. Add the squash to the bowl with the slivered mint.
5. Add the remaining oil, vinegar, and garlic to the squash and mint. Season with salt and pepper.
6. When the water boils, add salt and the pasta. Cook it until it is al dente, about 7 minutes.
7. In the meantime, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated crab and zucchini and the basil.
8. Add 2 tablespoons of pasta cooking water and the pasta to the skillet. Heat everything together, tossing to mix well.
9. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice and serve.
Notes: I like to use the squash cores to make a simple vegetarian pasta.
Author: Chris Fischer

Title: Buffalo Wings
Ingredients:
1. Peanut or canola oil for deep-frying
2. 5 pounds chicken wings (about 30 whole wings), split into two pieces, wing tips removed
3. Salt and freshly ground black pepper
4. 1 cup Anchor Bar Wing Sauce (or other hot sauce such as Frank’s Red Hot Original Cayenne Pepper Sauce)
5. 1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted
6. Celery sticks for serving
7. 1 cup mayonnaise
8. 1/2 cup sour cream
9. 1/4 cup crumbled blue cheese
10. Juice of 1/2 lemon
11. 1 garlic clove, minced
Instructions:
1. Make the dip: in a large bowl, mix the mayonnaise, sour cream, cheese, lemon juice, garlic, and salt and pepper until thoroughly combined. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving.
2. Make the wings: pour 2 inches of oil into a Dutch oven or deep, heavy pot and heat to 350°F.
3. Working in batches, immerse the wings in the hot oil, being careful not to crowd the pot, which will cause the temperature to dip.
4. Deep-fry at 350°F for 12 to 15 minutes, or until completely cooked and crispy. (When the chicken is fully cooked, the juices will run clear.)
5. Drain on paper towels. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Whisk the hot sauce and melted butter in a large bowl.
7. Toss the fried wings in the hot sauce mixture until completely coated.
8. Serve immediately with celery sticks and blue cheese dip.
Time:
Prep: 1 hour (for dip refrigeration)
Cook: 12 to 15 minutes per batch
Total: Approximately 1 hour 30 minutes
Notes: For milder wings, use more butter; for spicier wings, use less.
Author: James Beard Foundation

Title: Grilled Whole Eggplant With Harissa Vinaigrette
Ingredients:
1. 1 large eggplant (about 1 1/2 lb.)
2. 1 tsp. kosher salt, divided
3. 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
4. 1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
5. 1 Tbsp. harissa paste
6. 1 Tbsp. honey
7. 1/4 cup chopped parsley
8. 4–6 (1"-thick) slices crusty bread, toasted on grill if desired
Instructions:
1. Prepare a charcoal fire in a grill. Let coals cool to medium heat (coals should be covered with ash and glowing red with no black remaining).
2. Grill eggplant directly on coals, turning occasionally, until skin is blackened and flesh has collapsed, 15–20 minutes. (Alternatively, grill on the grate of a gas or charcoal grill over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, 20–25 minutes.) Transfer to a wire rack set inside a rimmed baking sheet and let cool slightly.
3. Carefully remove skin from eggplant, leaving stem intact. Season on all sides with 1/2 tsp. salt. Let sit on rack until excess water is drained, 20–30 minutes.
4. Whisk oil, lemon juice, harissa, honey, and remaining 1/2 tsp. salt in a small bowl to combine.
5. Using a paring knife, make several slits down length of eggplant on each side. Transfer to a plate and pour dressing over. Top with parsley and serve with bread alongside.
6. Eggplant (without parsley) can be dressed 8 hours ahead. Let sit at room temperature up to 4 hours. Chill, if waiting longer, and let sit at room temperature 1 hour before serving.
Time: Total: 45 minutes
Author: Anna Stockwell

Title: Coconut Milk–Braised Chicken Legs
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup unsweetened coconut milk
2. 1 Tbsp. Thai curry paste
3. 1 lemongrass stalk, tough outer layer removed, lightly crushed
4. 1 1" piece ginger, peeled, smashed
5. 4 garlic cloves, peeled, smashed
6. 2 chicken legs (thigh and drumstick; about 1½ lb. total)
7. Kosher salt
8. Toasted unsweetened coconut flakes, cilantro leaves with tender stems, cooked rice, and lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to 400°.
2. Stir coconut milk and curry paste in a 2-qt. baking dish to combine (or, use a medium skillet if that’s what you’ve got).
3. Add lemongrass, ginger, and garlic.
4. Season chicken with salt (hold back a bit since curry pastes often have a lot of salt).
5. Place in baking dish and spoon some liquid over.
6. Bake, occasionally spooning liquid over, until chicken is browned, tender, and cooked throughout (the joint should be reasonably easy to flex), 60–75 minutes.
7. Divide chicken between plates.
8. Top with coconut flakes and cilantro.
9. Serve with rice and lime wedges alongside.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 60–75 minutes
Total: 70–85 minutes
Notes: If you don’t have ginger, garlic, and lemongrass on hand, a combination of any two will be plenty to make this chicken over-the-top delicious.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Chaffle Recipe (5 Flavors, Not Eggy!)
Ingredients:
1. Shredded Cheese (e.g., mozzarella, cheddar, Gruyere)
2. Eggs
3. Almond Flour
4. Psyllium Husk Powder
5. Baking Powder
Instructions:
1. Preheat your chaffle maker. A regular waffle iron works just fine, too. You just want it hot to help you get that crisp exterior.
2. Mix the batter. Just whisk or stir the ingredients in a bowl. You can make my basic version above, or one of the flavors below.
3. Cook until golden and crispy.
Yield: Varies based on chaffle maker size
Time:
Prep: A few minutes
Cook: Varies based on chaffle maker
Total: A few minutes
Notes: The basic version is neutral and bread-like, making them great for sandwiches. Vegan cheese substitutes should work in place of shredded cheese or cream cheese.
Author: Maya Krampf

Title: Through Hardship, These Kreplach Endured
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups all-purpose flour
2. 1/2 teaspoon salt
3. 2 large eggs
4. 1/4 cup water
5. 1 tablespoon vegetable oil
6. 1 pound ground beef
7. 1 onion, finely chopped
8. Salt and pepper to taste
9. 1 tablespoon vegetable oil
10. Chicken broth or water for boiling
Instructions:
1. 1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt.
2. 2. Make a well in the center and add the eggs, water, and oil.
3. 3. Mix until a dough forms, then knead on a floured surface until smooth.
4. 4. Cover the dough and let it rest for 30 minutes.
5. 5. In a skillet, heat oil over medium heat and add the onion.
6. 6. Cook until the onion is soft, then add the ground beef.
7. 7. Season with salt and pepper and cook until the beef is browned.
8. 8. Roll out the dough on a floured surface to about 1/8 inch thick.
9. 9. Cut the dough into 3-inch squares.
10. 10. Place a spoonful of the beef mixture in the center of each square.
11. 11. Fold the dough over to form a triangle and seal the edges.
12. 12. Bring a pot of chicken broth or water to a boil.
13. 13. Drop the kreplach into the boiling liquid and cook until they float to the top, about 5-7 minutes.
Time:
Prep: 45 minutes
Cook: 15 minutes
Total: 1 hour
Notes: These kreplach can be served in soup or as a side dish.
Author: Angela Josephsberg-Achsen

Title: Crispy Farro and Tomato Salad
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup cooked farro (leftover or freshly prepared and fully cooled; see headnote)
2. sea salt, to taste
3. 2 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for drizzling
4. 1 tablespoon finely chopped parsley (dried well if damp)
5. 2 tablespoons finely grated Parmesan, plus more for finishing
6. 1 teaspoon finely grated lemon zest, or to taste
7. 2 large tomatoes (1 to 1 1/4 pounds), cored and thinly sliced--or use a mix of different colors, shapes, and sizes
Instructions:
1. Heat the broiler with an oven rack about 4 to 5 inches from the heating element.
2. Toss the cooked farro on a sheet pan with the olive oil and a few pinches of salt, then spread into an even layer.
3. Broil for a few minutes, or until you hear a faint sizzle and popping sound. Stir, spread out, and broil for a few minutes longer. Repeat until the farro is lightly toasted and crispy-chewy in texture, about 5 minutes in total. (Better to err on under- vs. over-toasting; broil too long and the farro will get too hard and dry.) Let cool for 5 to 10 minutes.
4. When ready to serve, toss the crispy farro with the parsley, Parmesan, and lemon zest.
5. Arrange the tomatoes on a large platter and season with sea salt.
6. Sprinkle some of the crispy farro over the tomatoes (making sure that the tomatoes aren’t hidden), and spoon the rest around the tomatoes.
7. To finish the salad, grate a little Parmesan and drizzle a little more olive oil over the tomatoes, if desired.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 5 minutes
Total: 15 minutes
Notes: For a whole new summer grain salad experience, broil farro until it's crackly and crispy, toss with parsley, parmesan, and lemon zest, then scatter over the ripest, juiciest tomatoes you have. Repeat all summer long. Substitute other grains for the farro (freekeh, wheat berries, spelt, and quinoa are favorites of mine). Use leftover or freshly prepared grains (fully cooled). For best results, cook the grains like pasta in a pot of heavily salted water to minimize the amount of moisture they retain. They’ll crisp better this way, and cook faster, too.
Author: EmilyC

Title: Farro and Escarole Soup
Ingredients:
1. 4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced
2. 3 Tbsp. capers
3. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
4. 1 medium onion, finely chopped
5. 1 cup unhulled farro or spelt
6. ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
7. 6 celery stalks, thinly sliced on a diagonal
8. 4 cups torn escarole or kale
9. 1 oz. finely grated Parmesan (about ¼ cup)
10. Kosher salt
Instructions:
1. Cook garlic, capers, and oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, stirring often, until garlic is golden, 4–6 minutes. Add onion, farro, and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is slightly softened, about 3 minutes.
2. Add 6 cups cold water to pot and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, partially covered, until farro is tender, 20–25 minutes. Add celery and cook, uncovered, until almost tender, about 5 minutes. Stir escarole and Parmesan into soup and continue to cook until escarole is wilted, another 4 minutes. Remove from heat and season with salt.
3. Soup can be made 4 days ahead. Transfer to an airtight container and chill.
Time: Cook: 33-40 minutes
Notes: If you have really good homemade stock on hand, you can use it instead of the water called for here; the soup will only benefit from the addition.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Chicken Pot Pie with Leeks and Thyme
Ingredients:
1. 1 large egg
2. 1 tablespoon water
3. 1/2 (17.3-ounce) package frozen puff pastry sheets, thawed
4. 1/2 cup (4 ounces) unsalted butter
5. 2 cups thinly sliced leek (from 1 large leek)
6. 1 cup chopped carrots (from 3 medium carrots)
7. 1/2 cup all-purpose flour (about 2 1/8 ounces), plus more for work surface
8. 2 1/2 cups chicken stock
9. 4 cups shredded rotisserie chicken
10. 1 cup frozen petite sweet peas, thawed
11. 1/4 cup heavy cream
12. 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
13. 1 tablespoon chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
14. 2 teaspoons chopped fresh thyme, plus thyme leaves for garnish
15. 1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
16. 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
Instructions:
1. Gather the ingredients.
2. Whisk together egg and 1 tablespoon water in a small bowl.
3. Roll pastry sheet into a 12-inch square on a lightly floured surface. Cut into 16 (3-inch) squares. Set egg wash and pastry squares aside.
4. Preheat oven to 400°F with rack in lower third of oven. Melt butter in a deep 10-inch ovenproof skillet over medium-high. Add leek and carrots; cook, stirring often, until softened, about 6 minutes. Sprinkle with flour; cook, stirring constantly, 1 minute.
5. Stir in stock; bring mixture to a simmer.
6. Simmer, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens, 1 to 2 minutes.
7. Stir in chicken, peas, cream, Dijon, parsley, thyme, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat.
8. Brush pastry squares with egg wash; arrange squares on top of hot chicken mixture in skillet, slightly overlapping so entire surface of chicken mixture is covered. Place skillet on a rimmed baking sheet.
9. Transfer baking sheet with skillet to preheated oven. Bake until top is browned and filling is bubbly, about 20 minutes. Remove from oven and let stand 10 minutes. Sprinkle with fresh thyme leaves and serve.
Time:
Prep: 20 mins
Cook: 50 mins
Total: 1 hr 10 mins
Notes: The recipe uses precooked chicken, and rotisserie chicken is recommended. The dish is made in one skillet and uses store-bought puff pastry for the top crust.
Author: Robby Melvin

Title: Creamy Mushroom Pasta
Ingredients:
1. 4 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
2. 1 lb. mixed mushrooms (such as maitake, oyster, crimini, and/or shiitake), torn into bite-size pieces
3. Kosher salt
4. 2 medium shallots, finely chopped
5. 1 lb. spaghetti or bucatini
6. ½ cup heavy cream
7. ⅓ cup finely chopped parsley
8. Zest and juice of ½ lemon
9. 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces
10. ½ oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about ½ cup), plus more for serving
11. Freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
1. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a Dutch oven or other large pot over medium-high. Cook half of mushrooms in a single layer, undisturbed, until edges are brown and starting to crisp, about 3 minutes. Give mushrooms a toss and continue to cook, tossing occasionally, until all sides are brown and crisp, about 5 minutes more. Using a slotted spoon, transfer mushrooms to a plate; season with salt. Repeat with remaining 2 Tbsp. oil and mushrooms and more salt.
2. Reduce heat to medium-low and return all of the mushrooms to the pot. Add shallots and cook, stirring often, until shallots are translucent and softened, about 2 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, cook pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until very al dente, about 2 minutes less than package directions.
4. Using tongs, transfer pasta to pot with mushrooms and add cream and 1 cup pasta cooking liquid. Increase heat to medium, bring to a simmer, and cook, tossing constantly, until pasta is al dente and liquid is slightly thickened, about 3 minutes.
5. Remove pot from heat. Add lemon zest and juice, parsley, butter, ½ oz. Parmesan, and lots of pepper and toss to combine. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
6. Divide pasta among bowls and top with more Parmesan.
Time: Total: 25 minutes
Notes: Garnish the dish with red pepper flakes and extra Parmesan cheese if you like, and serve with a big salad.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Za’atar Roasted Chicken over Sumac Potatoes
Ingredients:
1. 4 to 5 medium red potatoes (1½ pounds), scrubbed
2. 4 medium shallots, quartered
3. 4 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
4. 2 tablespoons sumac
5. Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
6. 1 small roasting chicken (about 3½ to 4 pounds), patted dry
7. 1 small lemon
8. 5 tablespoons Za’atar Spice Blend
9. ¼ teaspoon dried red pepper flakes
10. 2 garlic cloves
11. 6 thyme sprigs
Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Cut each potato into 6 wedges. In a 9 x 13-inch metal or glass baking dish, toss the potatoes and shallots with 1 tablespoon of the olive oil and the sumac, salt, and black pepper.
3. Season the cavity and exterior of the chicken well with salt and pepper.
4. Zest the lemon into a small bowl, halve the lemon and set aside.
5. Add the remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil to the bowl along with 4 tablespoons of the za’atar and the red pepper flakes and gently stir.
6. Stuff the lemon halves, garlic, and thyme sprigs inside the chicken, then rub the chicken all over with the za’atar mixture.
7. Sprinkle with the remaining tablespoon of za’atar.
8. Place the chicken, breast-side up, on top of the potatoes.
9. Roast the chicken for 15 minutes, then reduce the heat to 350°F and continue to roast the chicken until a chicken leg jiggles when pulled, the juices run clear, and the potatoes underneath the chicken are soft and the ones on the edges are crisp and golden, about another hour and 20 minutes.
10. Remove the chicken from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes.
11. Remove the lemon, garlic, and thyme springs from the cavity, discarding the garlic and thyme.
12. Carve the chicken right on top of the potatoes, letting the juice coat the potatoes, then squeeze one or both halves of the reserved lemon on the chicken and potatoes.
Time:
Prep: 20 minutes
Cook: 1 hour 35 minutes
Total: 2 hours 5 minutes
Notes: If you’re having a crowd, throw this in the oven during cocktail hour, then pull it out for oohs and aahs. If you’re feeding a smaller group, be happy because this chicken, pulled off the bone and tossed into a salad, makes a killer next-day lunch.
Author: Adeena Sussman

Title: Classic Garlic Bread
Ingredients:
1. ½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter
2. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
3. 2 cloves garlic, peeled, crushed
4. Large handful of fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves, finely chopped
5. 1 baguette
6. Kosher salt
7. Finely grated Parmesan
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°. Heat butter and oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add garlic and parsley and cook, stirring occasionally, until butter is completely melted.
2. Slice bread in half lengthwise, then place on a baking sheet and brush cut sides with butter mixture.
3. Slice crosswise 1" thick, without cutting all the way through; season with salt and top with Parmesan. Bake until golden, 10–15 minutes.
Time: Total: 25 minutes
Notes: You can cut and butter the bread well in advance, but don’t bake it until guests arrive.
Author: Melissa Hamilton and Christopher Hirsheimer

Title: Nancy's Chopped Salad
Ingredients:
1. 2 1/2 tablespoons red wine vinegar
2. 2 tablespoons dried oregano
3. Freshly squeezed juice from 1/2 lemon (1 tablespoon), or more to taste
4. 2 medium cloves garlic, 1 smashed flat and 1 grated
5. 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more to taste
6. 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, plus more to taste
7. 1 1/2 cups extra-virgin olive oil
8. 1/2 small red onion, cut in half from top to bottom
9. 1 head (22 ounces) iceberg lettuce
10. 1 head (11 ounces) radicchio
11. 1 pint small, sweet cherry tomatoes, such as Sun Golds or Sweet 100s, cut into quarters
12. Kosher salt
13. 1 1/2 cups no-salt-added chickpeas, drained
14. 1/4 pound aged provolone, cut into 1/8-inch-thick slices, then cut into 1/4-inch-wide strips
15. 1/4 pound Genoa salami, cut into 1/8-inch-thick slices, then cut into 1/4-inch-wide strips
16. 5 pepperoncini (stems discarded), cut into thin slices (about 1/4 cup)
17. Freshly squeezed juice from 1/2 lemon (1 tablespoon), or more to taste
18. Dried oregano (preferably Sicilian oregano on the branch), for sprinkling
Instructions:
1. Whisk together the vinegar, oregano, lemon juice, the smashed garlic and grated garlic and the salt and pepper in a medium bowl. Let the mixture rest for 5 minutes (to marinate the oregano).
2. Add the oil in a slow, steady stream, whisking constantly to form an emulsified vinaigrette. Taste for seasoning, and add salt or lemon juice as needed. The yield is a generous 1 1/2 cups; you'll use up to 1/2 cup for this salad, and the remainder can be refrigerated for another use (up to 3 days).
3. Separate the layers of the onion and stack two or three layers on top of one another, then cut them lengthwise into 1/16-inch-wide strips. Repeat with the remaining onion layers. Place the onion in a small bowl of ice water to sit while you prepare the rest of the ingredients. Drain the onion and pat dry with paper towels before adding to the salad.
4. Cut the iceberg lettuce in half through the core. Remove and discard the outer leaves, and discard the core. Separate the lettuce leaves, stack two or three leaves on top of one another, then cut them lengthwise into 1/4-inch-wide strips. Repeat with the remaining leaves; thinly slice the radicchio in the same way.
5. Cut the tomatoes in half, season them with salt to taste, and toss gently.
6. Combine the lettuce, radicchio, tomatoes, chickpeas, provolone, salami, peperoncini and onion in a large, wide bowl. Season with salt to taste, and toss to thoroughly combine.
7. Drizzle 6 tablespoons of the vinaigrette over the salad, then sprinkle with the lemon juice; toss gently to coat the salad evenly. Taste, and add the remaining 2 tablespoons of the vinaigrette, plus salt and/or lemon juice as needed.
8. Transfer the salad to a large platter or divide it among individual plates, piling it like a mountain. Sprinkle the dried oregano leaves on top and serve.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Total: 30 minutes
Notes: Recipe adapted slightly from The Mozza Cookbook (Knopf, 2011) and The Washington Post.
Author: Genius Recipes

Title: Tomato, Caper & Celery Salad
Ingredients:
1. 1 small onion, finely chopped
2. 4 big tomatoes
3. 2oz/60g capers (soaked in water to get rid of the saltiness)
4. 5 celery stalks, peeled and finely diced
5. Half bunch cilantro, finely chopped
6. 5 tablespoons Signature Harissa
7. 2 tablespoons olive oil
8. 1 tablespoon Preserved Lemon Paste
9. salt to taste
Instructions:
1. Using a serrated peeler, peel the tomatoes. Cut in half and remove the seeds and liquid.
2. Finely chop the tomatoes and place in a mixing bowl.
3. Drain the capers, roughly chop, and add to the mixing bowl with the rest of the ingredients.
4. Taste for salt and serve.
Notes: Our Preserved Lemon Paste is an all-natural product and is therefore subject to slight variations due to the amount of natural pectin in each batch. We recommend that customers who receive Preserved Lemon Paste with a more gel-like texture either blitz their desired amount in a blender or use a knife to gently run through the product on a cutting board until it reaches their desired texture.

Title: Charred Brussels Sprouts With Warm Honey Glaze
Ingredients:
1. 1½ lb. brussels sprouts, trimmed, halved
2. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
3. ½ tsp. kosher salt, plus more
4. Freshly ground black pepper
5. ¼ cup honey
6. ⅓ cup sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar
7. ¾ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes (optional)
8. 3 Tbsp. unsalted butter
9. 3 scallions, thinly sliced on a diagonal
10. 1 tsp. finely grated lemon zest
Instructions:
1. Place a rimmed baking sheet on bottom rack of oven; preheat to 450°. Toss brussels sprouts and oil in a large bowl; season with salt and black pepper.
2. Carefully remove baking sheet from oven. Using tongs, arrange brussels cut side down on baking sheet. Roast brussels on bottom rack until softened and deeply browned, 20–25 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, bring honey to a simmer in a small saucepan over medium-high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook, stirring often, until honey is a deep amber color but not burnt (it will be foamy, that’s okay), 3–4 minutes.
4. Remove from heat and add vinegar and red pepper flakes, if using, and whisk until sauce is smooth (it will bubble up quite aggressively when you add the vinegar before settling). Return saucepan to medium heat, add butter and ½ tsp. salt, and cook, whisking constantly, until glaze is glossy, bubbling, and slightly thickened, 3–4 minutes.
5. Transfer brussels sprouts to a large bowl. Add glaze and scallions and toss to combine. Transfer to a platter and top with lemon zest.
Time: Total: 40 minutes
Notes: This recipe was originally published in September 2018.
Author: Molly Baz

Title: Glazed Leeks With Pine Nut Salsa Verde
Ingredients:
1. 1 fresno chile (or red pepper flakes)
2. Honey
3. Sherry or red wine vinegar
4. Finely chopped parsley
5. Pine nuts
6. Capers
7. Olive oil
8. Salt
9. Leeks
Instructions:
1. Bring fresno chile, honey, and sherry or red wine vinegar to a boil.
2. Reduce the mixture halfway for about five minutes to create a glaze.
3. Cook leeks until tender.
4. Pour the chile-honey-vinegar glaze over the leeks.
5. Prepare the salsa verde by mixing finely chopped parsley, pine nuts, capers, olive oil, and a pinch of salt.
6. Drizzle the salsa verde over the glazed leeks.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: Not specified
Total: Not specified
Notes: The glaze is slightly spicy-sweet and ultra vinegary, while the salsa verde adds a nutty, salty, herby bite.
Author: Emily Schultz

Title: Big-Flavor Broccoli
Ingredients:
1. 1 lb. broccoli (about 1 large or 2 medium heads)
2. 5 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided
3. Kosher salt
4. 1 small red onion, cut lengthwise into ½"-thick slices
5. 4 garlic cloves, sliced
6. 6 oil-packed anchovy fillets
7. 1 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about ¼ cup)
8. Lemon wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°. Trim only the very bottom, woody part of broccoli stem. Peel tough outer layer from stem, from the florets down to the end of the stalk. Starting from stem end, cut broccoli at a 45° angle into ¾"-thick slices until you reach the florets. Break florets apart with your hands into bite-size pieces.
2. Heat 3 Tbsp. oil in a large heatproof skillet over medium-high until shimmering. Add broccoli; season with salt. Cook, tossing occasionally, until broccoli is bright green and lightly charred, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a plate. Wipe out skillet.
3. Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in same skillet over medium. Cook onion and garlic, stirring often, until onion is beginning to soften, about 3 minutes. Add anchovies and cook, breaking apart with a spoon, until broken down and garlic is beginning to turn golden around the edges, about 2 minutes.
4. Return broccoli to skillet and toss to coat with oil. Transfer to oven and roast, tossing once, until broccoli is browned and tender, 20–25 minutes. Wrap handle of skillet with a towel before you forget it’s REALLY HOT.
5. Scatter Parmesan over hot broccoli. Divide among plates. Serve with lemon wedges alongside.
Time: Cook: 25-30 minutes
Notes: Chances are you’re trimming off and discarding way too much of your broccoli stems. The stems are so flavorful, they should be their own vegetable. This is our hands-down favorite way to cook the whole plant.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Lemon Meringue Pie
Ingredients:
1. ½-inch chunks of butter
2. ⅔ cup ice water
3. Sugar
4. Water
5. Cornstarch
6. Eggs
7. Egg yolks
8. Lemon juice
9. Lemon zest
Instructions:
1. Squish ½-inch chunks of butter between your fingers to make the pie dough.
2. Add ⅔ cup of ice water to hydrate the dough.
3. Smash the dough together, cut it into quarters, stack them up, and press them down to create layers.
4. Blind-bake the pie crust until it's brown all over.
5. On the stove, stir together sugar, water, cornstarch, eggs, egg yolks, lemon juice, and lemon zest to make the filling.
6. Pour the filling into the crust and chill for two hours until the curd sets.
7. Pipe or plop the meringue over the top of the pie.
8. Brûlée the meringue using a kitchen torch.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: Not specified
Total: Not specified
Notes: This recipe makes two pie crusts. You can halve the quantities if desired. The pie can be made a couple of days in advance.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Sweet Potatoes With Charred Lemons and Crunchies
Ingredients:
1. 4 medium sweet potatoes (about 2 lb. total)
2. 3 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided
3. Kosher salt
4. ½ cup blanched hazelnuts
5. 1½ tsp. fennel seeds
6. 1½ tsp. cumin seeds
7. 2 lemons
8. 1 cup whole-milk Greek yogurt
9. 3 Tbsp. unsalted butter
10. 2 Tbsp. raw sesame seeds
11. ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
Instructions:
1. Place a rack on bottom third of oven; preheat to 425°. Cut 4 medium sweet potatoes in half lengthwise and transfer to a large rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with 3 Tbsp. olive oil, season with 1 tsp. kosher salt, and toss to coat. Roast potatoes until very tender all the way through and deeply charred on cut sides, 25–30 minutes. You can check for tenderness by piercing the flesh with a paring knife or cake tester. Using a spatula, release potatoes from pan.
2. Meanwhile, prep your crunchies and lemony yogurt: Coarsely chop ½ cup blanched hazelnuts. (No hazelnuts? Pistachios, walnuts, and pecans all make good substitutes here.) Pile 1½ tsp. fennels seeds and 1½ tsp. cumin seeds on cutting board in a small mound and finely chop with a chef’s knife. Alternatively, break out your mortar and pestle.
3. Cut 2 lemons in half crosswise. Squeeze juice of 2 lemon halves into a small bowl. Add 1 cup whole-milk Greek yogurt and stir to combine. Season with salt.
4. Heat remaining 1 tsp. olive oil in a small skillet over medium. Cook 2 unsqueezed lemon halves (cut sides down), undisturbed, until well caramelized around the edges, about 3 minutes. Transfer lemons to baking sheet with potatoes.
5. Melt 3 Tbsp. butter in same skillet over medium heat. Add hazelnuts and 2 Tbsp. raw sesame seeds and cook, stirring often, until nuts are golden brown, 3–5 minutes. Add fennel, cumin, and ½ tsp. red pepper flakes and cook, stirring, to bloom spices, about 1 minute longer. Remove skillet from heat; season with ½ tsp. salt.
6. Spread lemony yogurt on a platter. Tear potatoes in half and arrange over yogurt. Spoon crunchies and browned butter over potatoes. Arrange charred lemons on platter for squeezing over.
Time: Cook: 25-30 minutes
Notes: With custardy insides and charred exteriors, these sweet potatoes are everything you always wish roasted sweet potatoes could be. A crunchy mixture of toasted nuts and seeds provides pops of texture and spice, and a cooling lemony yogurt sauce cuts through the richness. Don't skip charring the lemons—it's not just a cute party trick: Caramelizing the sugars tames some of the acidity even as the juice remains fresh and bright. Plus, you'll use that lemon-infused oil to crisp the nuts. Arrange the sweet potatoes on a platter and serve with bread, pita, or crunchy vegetables, like carrots and radishes, for swiping up any leftover yogurt.
Author: Molly Baz

Title: Gingerbread Waffles and Cream
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups all-purpose flour
2. 1 tablespoon baking powder
3. 1 teaspoon ground ginger
4. 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
5. 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
6. 1/2 teaspoon salt
7. 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves
8. 1/4 cup molasses
9. 1/4 cup packed brown sugar
10. 2 large eggs
11. 1 1/2 cups whole milk
12. 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted
13. 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
14. Whipped cream, for serving
Instructions:
1. Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, ginger, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and cloves.
3. In another bowl, whisk together the molasses, brown sugar, eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract until smooth.
4. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Be careful not to overmix.
5. Ladle the batter into the preheated waffle iron and cook according to the manufacturer's instructions until the waffles are golden brown and crisp.
6. Serve the waffles warm with whipped cream.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 20 minutes
Total: 35 minutes
Notes: This recipe is inspired by Abby Fisher's 1881 cookbook 'What Mrs. Fisher Knows About Old Southern Cooking' and Carole and Norma Jean Darden's 'Spoonbread & Strawberry Wine'.
Author: Toni Tipton-Martin

Notes: The webpage content could not be retrieved due to a 404 error.

Title: Cheesy Cabbage Gratin
Ingredients:
1. 1 medium head of green or savoy cabbage (about 3½ lb. total), cut through core into 8 wedges
2. 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
3. 1½ tsp. kosher salt, plus more
4. 1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper, plus more
5. 2 medium shallots, quartered through root end
6. 4 garlic cloves
7. 2 cups heavy cream
8. 1 Tbsp. thyme leaves, plus more for serving
9. 1 Tbsp. unsalted butter
10. 3 oz. Gruyère, grated (about 1½ cups)
11. ½ oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about ½ cup)
Instructions:
1. Place oven racks in upper third and middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Place cabbage on a rimmed baking sheet and drizzle with oil; season lightly with salt and pepper. Roast until tender and edges are golden, 40–45 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, bring shallots, garlic, cream, 1 Tbsp. thyme, 1½ tsp. salt, and 1 tsp. pepper to a simmer in a small saucepan over low heat. Cook, stirring occasionally, until shallots and garlic are very soft, 15–20 minutes. Let cool slightly. Transfer to a blender; blend until smooth.
3. Rub the inside of a 3-qt. shallow baking dish with butter. Arrange cabbage in dish so wedges are lying on a cut side; pour cream mixture over. Bake on middle rack, uncovered, until cream thickens, 30–40 minutes. Let cool 30 minutes.
4. Heat broiler. Sprinkle Gruyère and Parmesan evenly over cabbage. Broil until cheese is bubbling and gratin is deeply browned, about 4 minutes. Top with more thyme.
5. Gratin (without cheese) can be baked 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before sprinkling with cheese and broiling.
Time:
Prep: 15-20 minutes
Cook: 70-85 minutes
Total: 85-105 minutes
Notes: Gratin (without cheese) can be baked 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before sprinkling with cheese and broiling.
Author: Andy Baraghani

Title: Fideos With Chicken Thighs and Kale
Ingredients:
1. 2½ tsp. kosher salt, divided, plus more
2. ¼ tsp. plus 1 Tbsp. hot smoked paprika, divided
3. 2 lb. skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (4–6)
4. 6 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided
5. 8 oz. spaghetti (preferably thin spaghetti)
6. 2 medium onions
7. 6 garlic cloves
8. 4 oil-packed anchovy fillets
9. ¼ cup tomato paste
10. 1 bunch Tuscan kale
11. 1 lemon
12. ⅓ cup Castelvetrano olives
Instructions:
1. Place a rack in top third of oven; preheat to 375°. Pat chicken dry. Combine 2 tsp. salt and ¼ tsp. hot smoked paprika in a small bowl. Sprinkle all over chicken thighs.
2. Pour ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil into a large (12") skillet. Arrange chicken skin side down in oil. Turn heat to medium and cook, undisturbed, until chicken has rendered some fat and skin is golden brown, 10–12 minutes. Remove from heat and return to plate skin side up, leaving all that schmaltzy fat in skillet.
3. While chicken sears, break 8 oz. thin spaghetti into a bowl into ½"–1" pieces. Using your hands, scrunch broken pasta in bowl to further break it up. Peel and grate 2 onions on the large holes of a box grater. Peel and grate 6 garlic cloves on the small holes of grater. Run your knife through grated onion and garlic a couple of times to make sure no big pieces remain.
4. Add broken pasta to skillet and toss to coat in fat; season with ½ tsp. salt. Cook over medium heat, tossing constantly, until golden brown, 2–3 minutes. Cut the heat, tilt skillet away from you so the fat pools at one end, then use a slotted spoon to scoop out toasted pasta and transfer back to bowl, leaving as much fat in pan as possible.
5. Return skillet to medium-low heat, add onion, garlic, 4 oil-packed anchovy fillets, ¼ cup tomato paste, and 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil to skillet and cook, breaking up anchovies and stirring often, until completely softened, jammy, dark red, and fat has broken out of the mixture, 13–15 minutes. Reduce heat if the veg are browning too quickly and be sure to take your time—the soffrito is what provides the fideos with depth and richness.
6. Meanwhile, strip leaves off stems from 1 bunch Tuscan kale; discard stems. Rinse kale (no need to dry). Coarsely tear leaves. Crush ⅓ cup Castelvetrano olives, pry out their pits, and tear in half. Cut 1 lemon in half and set aside.
7. Add remaining 1 Tbsp. hot smoked paprika and cook, stirring frequently, until aromatic, about 30 seconds. Squeeze in juice of ½ lemon, scraping up any darkened bits on bottom of pan. Add 2 cups water, increase heat to medium-high, and bring to a simmer. Add kale and cook, tossing occasionally, until wilted, about 2 minutes.
8. Stir in olives and toasted pasta, along with any fat still clinging to bowl. Stir to combine, season with salt, and spread in an even layer. Arrange chicken on top (skin side up), pressing gently to tuck into pasta while leaving skin exposed. If needed, add just enough water to barely reach top of pasta, leaving the tops sticking out. Drizzle remaining 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil over pasta and chicken.
9. Transfer skillet to oven.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: Not specified
Total: Not specified
Notes: This one-skillet meal is anything but one-note. There's flavor at every stage, and the not-so-secret secret ingredient is the anchovies.
Author: Sohla El-Waylly

Title: Lion's Mane Mushroom Crab Cakes
Ingredients:
1. 1 pound lion’s mane mushrooms, cleaned and chopped
2. 2 tablespoons butter
3. 1/4 cup onion, finely chopped
4. 1/4 cup celery, finely chopped
5. 1/4 cup red bell pepper, finely chopped
6. 1/4 cup mayonnaise
7. 1 egg, beaten
8. 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
9. 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
10. 1 tablespoon lemon juice
11. 1 teaspoon Old Bay seasoning
12. 1/2 teaspoon salt
13. 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
14. 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
15. 2 tablespoons olive oil
Instructions:
1. 1. Heat butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped lion’s mane mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. 2. In a large bowl, combine the cooked mushrooms, onion, celery, red bell pepper, mayonnaise, egg, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, Old Bay seasoning, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
3. 3. Stir in breadcrumbs until the mixture holds together. Form into patties.
4. 4. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook the patties until golden brown on each side, about 3-4 minutes per side.
5. 5. Serve hot with your choice of sauce or garnish.
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 15 minutes
Total: 30 minutes
Notes: Lion’s mane mushrooms have a flavor and texture reminiscent of crab or lobster meat when cooked. Do not eat them raw.
Author: Aaron von Frank

Title: Ginger-Scallion Noodles With Tahini and Sesame Seeds
Ingredients:
1. ⅓ cup raw white sesame seeds
2. 16 oz. fresh or 10 oz. dried ramen noodles
3. 4 garlic cloves
4. 3 2”x1” pieces of ginger
5. 8 large scallions
6. ½ cup canola oil
7. Black pepper
8. 2 Tbsp. soy sauce
9. 1 Tbsp. sambal oelek
10. 2 Tbsp. tahini
11. 1 tsp. sugar
12. 2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar
Instructions:
1. Toast about ⅓ cup raw white sesame seeds in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat until deep brown, about 3–5 minutes, shaking the pan every minute.
2. Cook 16 oz. fresh or 10 oz. dried ramen noodles in a pot of salted boiling water.
3. Grate 4 garlic cloves and 3 pieces of ginger into a large heatproof mixing bowl.
4. Thinly slice 8 large scallions, separating the green parts from the white parts.
5. Mix the garlic, scallion whites, ½ the scallion greens, and ginger in the bowl. Reserve the other half of the scallion greens.
6. Heat ½ cup canola oil in a skillet over high heat until shimmering, about 3–4 minutes.
7. Pour the hot oil into the bowl with the garlic and scallions, stirring to intensify the scallions' color.
8. Add black pepper, 2 Tbsp. soy sauce, 1 Tbsp. sambal oelek, 2 Tbsp. tahini, 1 tsp. sugar, ¾ of the sesame seeds, and 2 Tbsp. unseasoned rice vinegar to the bowl and stir.
9. Cook noodles according to package instructions, reserving ¼ cup cooking water.
10. Stir reserved water and scallion greens into the sauce, then add cooked noodles and toss to coat.
11. Serve noodles topped with remaining toasted sesame seeds.
Notes: You can eat this sauce with rice, roasted vegetables, or crispy seared meat, but it's best with ramen noodles.
Author: Alex Delany

Title: Pernil (Slow-Roasted Pork)
Ingredients:
1. 2 ripe plum tomatoes, cored, coarsely chopped (about 1½ cups)
2. 1 medium onion, coarsely chopped (about 1 cup)
3. 2 Cubanelle peppers, seeds removed, coarsely chopped (about 1 cup), or 1 medium green bell pepper, ribs and seeds removed, coarsely chopped
4. 1 medium red bell pepper, ribs and seeds removed, coarsely chopped (about 1 cup)
5. 1 large bunch cilantro, coarsely chopped (about 1 cup)
6. 1 head of garlic, cloves separated, peeled
7. 1 Tbsp. black peppercorns
8. 2 heads of garlic, cloves separated, peeled
9. 2 1.4-oz. packets Sazón con Culantro y Achiote (such as Goya)
10. ½ cup Diamond Crystal or ¼ cup plus 1½ tsp. Morton kosher salt
11. 2 Tbsp. dried oregano
12. 2 Tbsp. white wine vinegar
13. 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for brushing
14. 1 8–10-lb. skin-on, bone-in pork shoulder
15. Arroz con Gandules (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Purée tomatoes, onion, Cubanelle peppers, bell pepper, cilantro, and garlic in a food processor until smooth. Scoop out ½ cup soffritto and set aside. Reserve remaining soffritto for another use (you can use it for the arroz con gandules).
2. Soffritto can be made 3 days ahead. Transfer to an airtight container, cover, and chill, or freeze up to 3 months.
3. Finely grind peppercorns in spice mill or with mortar and pestle. Transfer to a food processor. Add garlic, Sazón, salt, oregano, vinegar, 2 Tbsp. oil, and reserved ½ cup soffritto and purée until smooth.
4. Push a small paring knife (about 3½" long) into pork through the skin, working the blade all the way in; twist knife to make a small hole in the meat. Repeat, making holes spaced about 1½" apart on all sides of pork.
5. Rub pork all over with adobo mixture, pushing into holes and covering any exposed meat and skin. Try to get as much adobo mixture into meat and not just on the surface, where it may burn when roasting. Be sure to use all of it! Wrap pork tightly and chill at least 3 hours and up to 3 days.
6. Place a rack in lower third of oven; preheat to 300°. Line a large roasting pan with 2 layers of heavy-duty foil. Place a roasting rack on top. Set pork, skin side up, on rack. Scrape any adobo mixture off skin and thoroughly pat dry with paper towels. Liberally brush cleaned skin with oil. Transfer pan to oven and carefully pour 2 cups water into pan. Roast pork until an instant-read thermometer inserted into thickest part of shoulder registers 180°, meat is pulling away from bone, and skin is crisp, 5–6 hours.
7. Let pork sit, uncovered, at room temperature at least 30 minutes and up to 5 hours.
8. Before serving, preheat oven to 500° and reheat roast until skin crisps (don’t let it take on any more color), 5–10 minutes.
9. Remove chicharrón (crispy skin) and cut or break into smaller pieces. Slice pork and transfer to a platter. Serve with chicharrón and arroz y gandules alongside.
Time:
Prep: At least 3 hours (including chilling time)
Cook: 5–6 hours
Total: 8–9 hours
Notes: If you can’t find Sazón, you can use any other seasoning salt, preferably one with a Latin American flavor profile.
Author: Rick Martinez

Title: Cheesy Spiced Cauliflower Steak Pizzas
Ingredients:
1. 3 medium cauliflower heads (about 1 1/2 to 2 pounds each)
2. 5 tablespoons olive oil
3. 1 1/2 teaspoons fennel seeds
4. 1 teaspoon fine sea salt
5. 1 teaspoon black pepper
6. 1/2 teaspoon orange zest
7. 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder
8. 1 cup best-quality marinara sauce
9. 8 ounces fontina cheese, shredded (about 2 cups), or 8 (2/3-ounce) provolone cheese slices
10. 1/4 ounces Parmesan cheese, grated (about 2 tablespoons)
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F with rack in upper third of oven. Cut each cauliflower head vertically through core into 2 to 3 (3/4-inch-thick) steaks to form 8 steaks. (Reserve remaining cauliflower for another use.) Arrange steaks in a single layer on a large rimmed baking sheet. Brush oil evenly on both sides of steaks.
2. Cook fennel seeds in a small saucepan over medium-high, stirring occasionally, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Transfer to a mortar, and crush using a pestle (or coarsely grind in a spice grinder). Stir in salt, pepper, orange zest, and garlic powder. Sprinkle mixture evenly over both sides of cauliflower steaks.
3. Roast cauliflower steaks in preheated oven until golden brown and tender, about 30 minutes, flipping halfway through roasting time. Remove from oven; increase oven temperature to broil.
4. Spread cauliflower steaks evenly with marinara; top with cheeses. Broil until cheeses melt and start to brown, 6 to 8 minutes.
Time:
Prep: 25 mins
Cook: 25 mins
Total: 50 mins
Notes: ['Be sure to flip the cauliflower during roasting to ensure there are plenty of caramelized florets under the marinara and melty cheese.', 'Use leftover cauliflower florets for soups and stews or in a fresh winter salad.', 'The cauliflower may be cut one day ahead, wrapped in plastic wrap or stored in an airtight container, and kept in the refrigerator.', 'The sausage spice blend can be made several days ahead and stored in an airtight container at room temperature.']
Author: Anna Theoktisto

Title: Chewy Ginger-Rye Cookies
Ingredients:
1. 1 cup (128 g) all-purpose flour
2. 1¼ tsp. baking soda
3. 1½ tsp. ground cinnamon
4. ¼ tsp. ground cloves
5. ¾ cup (110 g) plus 6 Tbsp. (60 g) rye flour, divided
6. 3 tsp. ground ginger, divided
7. 1¼ tsp. kosher salt, divided
8. ⅓ cup (50 g) finely chopped crystallized ginger
9. ⅔ cup robust-flavored (dark) molasses
10. ¼ cup (packed; 50 g) dark brown sugar
11. ¾ cup (1½ sticks) unsalted butter, melted, plus 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter, room temperature
12. 2 large egg yolks
13. 6 Tbsp. (90 g) raw sugar
Instructions:
1. Whisk all-purpose flour, baking soda, cinnamon, cloves, ¾ cup rye flour, 2 tsp. ground ginger, and 1 tsp. salt in a medium bowl to combine; add crystallized ginger and toss until coated and evenly distributed. Whisk molasses, brown sugar, and ¾ cup melted butter in a medium bowl to combine. Add egg yolks and whisk just until egg yolks are absorbed but mixture is still very dark, about 10 seconds. Add dry ingredients and fold until no streaks of flour remain. Let sit, uncovered, at room temperature 30 minutes (batter will thicken as it sits).
2. Meanwhile, place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 375°. Whisk raw sugar, remaining 6 Tbsp. rye flour, remaining 1 tsp. ground ginger, and remaining ¼ tsp. salt in a small bowl to combine. Add remaining room-temperature butter and smash together until mixture resembles clumpy wet sand; this is your streusel topping.
3. Using a 1-oz. ice cream scoop, portion out dough (about 2 Tbsp. each) and roll into balls between your palms. Working one at a time, toss balls in streusel mixture to coat and place on 2 parchment-lined baking sheets, spacing 3" apart. Bake 1 sheet of cookies until they have spread and are slightly cracked and just set around the edges and streusel is lightly browned, 8–10 minutes. Let cool 10 minutes on baking sheet. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely. Bake remaining sheet of cookies.
4. Dough can be rolled into balls 2 weeks ahead. Freeze on a baking sheet; transfer to resealable plastic freezer bags. Let sit at room temperature 30 minutes before rolling in streusel and baking.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 8–10 minutes per sheet
Total: Approximately 1 hour
Notes: Dough can be rolled into balls 2 weeks ahead. Freeze on a baking sheet; transfer to resealable plastic freezer bags. Let sit at room temperature 30 minutes before rolling in streusel and baking.
Author: Rick Martinez

Title: Tahini Rice Crispy Treats
Ingredients:
1. 3¼ cups (130 g) crispy brown or white rice cereal
2. ¼ cup (30 g) cacao nibs
3. 1 Tbsp. virgin coconut oil
4. ¾ cup (185 g) tahini
5. ¼ cup plus 1 Tbsp. (90 g) honey
6. ¾ tsp. kosher salt
7. ½ tsp. vanilla extract
Instructions:
1. Line an 8" or 9" square pan with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on two sides. Toss cereal and cacao nibs in a large bowl to combine.
2. Melt oil in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Add tahini, honey, and salt and whisk until warmed through, smooth, and slightly thinner, 45–60 seconds. (Don’t be tempted to cook this longer—the tahini can start to crystallize, which will make it grainy.) Remove from heat and whisk in vanilla.
3. Pour tahini mixture over cereal mixture and stir to coat. Transfer to prepared pan and press down very firmly (seriously, give it your all!) with the bottom of a heavy glass or measuring cup to compact. Chill bars until firm, 30–40 minutes.
4. Lift out bars using parchment overhang and cut into 16 squares.
5. Bars can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.
Time:
Prep: Not specified
Cook: 45–60 seconds
Total: 30–40 minutes plus chilling time
Notes: Use whatever brand of crispy brown rice cereal you can find, avoiding the “puffed” varieties, which have a tendency to be more squishy than crunchy. You can even use regular Rice Krispies cereal instead of crispy brown rice cereal, but since Rice Krispies are smaller and less dense, your bars might be a little bit softer than intended, though still delicious. For the sturdiest bars, be sure to really compact them and keep them in the fridge so they stay firm.
Author: Sarah Jampel

Title: Tofu Crumbles With Rice and Avocado
Ingredients:
1. 1 14-oz. block firm or extra-firm tofu
2. ¼ cup extra-virgin olive oil
3. Kosher salt
4. 2 garlic cloves, smashed
5. 2 tsp. ground coriander
6. 1 tsp. ancho powder or sweet smoked paprika
7. 1 tsp. ground cumin
8. ½ tsp. ground fennel
9. 1 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste
10. 2 tsp. hot sauce
11. Cooked white rice
12. Shredded red cabbage
13. Sliced avocado
14. Crumbled queso fresco (optional)
15. Cilantro
16. Lime wedges (for serving)
Instructions:
1. Firmly squeeze tofu to expel as much excess water as possible (don’t worry if it begins to crack or come apart). Tear into 1"–2" pieces.
2. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high. Add tofu, season with salt, and cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown and crisp around the edges, 5–7 minutes. Using a rubber spatula, break up tofu into ½"–1" pieces. Add garlic and cook, tossing occasionally, until tofu pieces are crisp all over, about 3 minutes.
3. Reduce heat to medium. Add coriander, ancho powder, cumin, and fennel. Cook, stirring, just until spices deepen in color, about 20 seconds. Add tomato paste and cook, stirring with rubber spatula, until paste slightly darkens in color, about 1 minute. Add hot sauce and 1 cup water. Bring to a simmer and cook, uncovered and stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens, 5–7 minutes. Season with salt.
4. Divide rice among bowls. Top with tofu mixture, cabbage, avocado, queso fresco, and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.
Time: Cook: 15-20 minutes
Notes: This recipe is vegan if you omit the queso fresco.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Pillowy Soft Cinnamon Rolls
Ingredients:
1. 3/4 cup warm milk
2. 2 1/4 teaspoons instant yeast
3. 1/4 cup granulated sugar
4. 1/4 cup honey
5. 4 cups all-purpose flour
6. 1 teaspoon salt
7. 1/2 cup unsalted butter, softened
8. 2 large eggs
9. 3/4 cup brown sugar
10. 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
11. 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted
12. 1 cup powdered sugar
13. 2-3 tablespoons milk or cream
Instructions:
1. 1. In a small bowl, combine warm milk and yeast. Let sit until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. 2. In a large bowl, mix together sugar, honey, flour, and salt.
3. 3. Add softened butter, eggs, and yeast mixture to the flour mixture. Stir until a sticky dough forms.
4. 4. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 10 minutes until smooth.
5. 5. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 2 hours or until doubled in size.
6. 6. Punch down the dough and roll it out into a rectangle.
7. 7. Spread melted butter over the dough and sprinkle with brown sugar and cinnamon.
8. 8. Roll the dough tightly into a log and cut into 10 rolls.
9. 9. Place rolls in a greased baking dish, cover, and let rise for 30 minutes.
10. 10. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
11. 11. Bake the rolls for 30 minutes or until golden brown.
12. 12. In a small bowl, whisk together powdered sugar and milk to make the icing.
13. 13. Drizzle icing over warm rolls and serve.
Time:
Prep: 30 minutes
Cook: 30 minutes
Total: 4 hours 30 minutes
Notes: For extra gooey rolls, apply a thin layer of icing while rolls are warm.
Author: Sarah Kieffer

Title: Vietnamese Canh Soup
Ingredients:
1. a splash of neutral oil
2. half a medium onion, thinly sliced
3. 5 to 6 cups water
4. 1/2 tsp salt
5. 1 Tbsp fish sauce
6. 8 ounces chopped greens (mustard greens, turnip greens, radish tops, kale, or mature spinach)
7. 1 1/2 teaspoons finely chopped ginger
8. 1 dozen peeled and deveined raw shrimp, cut into chunks
Instructions:
1. Warm a splash of neutral oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
2. Thinly slice half a medium onion and add it to the pot. Cook gently, stirring occasionally, until soft and no longer smells raw, about 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Pour in 5 to 6 cups of water. Season with 1/2 tsp salt and 1 Tbsp fish sauce.
4. Increase the heat to bring to a boil and cook vigorously for 3 to 5 minutes.
5. Add 8 ounces of chopped greens. Cook until the greens wilt and soften, about 5 minutes.
6. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons finely chopped ginger and 1 dozen peeled and deveined raw shrimp. Cook until the shrimp is opaque and cooked through.
7. Slide the pot to a cool burner and let it rest, uncovered, for 5 to 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
8. Taste and adjust seasoning with extra salt or fish sauce if needed. Splash in water if the soup is too strong.
9. Serve in individual soup bowls or a large bowl to share.
Time:
Prep: 10 minutes
Cook: 20 minutes
Total: 30 minutes
Notes: ['Instead of shrimp, you can substitute a 6-ounce fillet of mild fish, such as rockfish, sea bass, or tilapia, cut into bite-size pieces.', 'For a non-seafood option, add 5 to 6 ounces of ground pork or chicken after sautéing the onion.', 'To turn leftovers into a noodle soup, add glass noodles (soaked and snipped) when reheating.']
Author: Andrea Nguyen

Title: Dairy-Free Cashew Queso
Ingredients:
1. 2 cups water (480 mL)
2. 2 medium garlic cloves
3. 1 ½ cups raw cashews (225 g)
4. 3 tablespoons nutritional yeast
5. 1 ¼ teaspoons ground cumin
6. ½ teaspoon chipotle powder
7. ½ teaspoon smoked paprika
8. 1 ½ teaspoons chili powder
9. 1 ¼ teaspoons kosher salt, plus more to taste
10. ½ cup canned diced tomatoes with green chiles (100 g)
11. tortilla chips, for serving
Instructions:
1. In a small saucepan, bring the water to a simmer over medium-high heat. Add the garlic cloves and blanch for 60 seconds. Remove the garlic, then remove the pot from the heat and reserve the hot water.
2. To the bowl of a food processor, add the cashews, blanched garlic cloves, nutritional yeast, cumin, chipotle powder, paprika, chili powder, and salt. Starting with 1¼ cups (300 g), add the reserved hot water. Blend on medium-high speed to combine.
3. If the mixture looks too thick and isn’t blending well, add another ¼ cup (60 ml) of hot water and continue blending. Add more hot water, 1-2 tablespoons at a time, as needed until the mixture is smooth, silky, and spreadable.
4. Transfer the queso to a serving bowl and fold in the diced tomatoes and green chiles.
5. Serve with tortilla chips. Enjoy!
Time:
Prep: 15 minutes
Cook: 5 minutes
Total: 20 minutes
Notes: This dairy-free cashew queso is creamy, flavorful, and perfect for any vegan or lactose intolerant individual.
Author: Crystal Hatch & Karlee Rotoly

Title: Italian Chopped Salad
Ingredients:
1. 1 Tbsp. unsalted butter
2. 7 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, divided
3. 1 14.5-oz. can chickpeas, rinsed
4. 2 small garlic cloves, divided
5. Kosher salt
6. 3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar
7. 1½ tsp. honey
8. 1 tsp. Dijon mustard
9. ½ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
10. Freshly ground black pepper
11. 1 head of radicchio, thinly sliced
12. 2 endive, thinly sliced
13. 2½ oz. sharp provolone cheese, thinly sliced, torn
14. 2 oz. cured fennel salami, thinly sliced, cut into half-moons
Instructions:
1. Heat butter and 2 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high until butter melts. Add chickpeas and 1 garlic clove; season with salt. Cook, tossing occasionally, until chickpeas are browned and crisp, 6–8 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, whisk vinegar, honey, mustard, red pepper flakes, and remaining 5 Tbsp. oil in a large bowl to combine. Finely chop half of remaining garlic clove (discard the rest unless you really, really like garlic) and add to dressing; season with salt and black pepper.
3. Add radicchio, endive, provolone, and salami to dressing and toss to coat; season with salt. Add chickpeas and toss again to combine.
Time: Cook: 6–8 minutes
Notes: This winter salad with peak season chicories, salami, and crispy chickpeas is hearty enough to have on its own for dinner, but not so over-the-top that you can’t also enjoy it alongside a heftier main, like pizza or pasta.
Author: Chris Morocco

Title: Spicy Peel-and-Eat Skillet Shrimp with Garlic
Ingredients:
1. 1 pound unpeeled raw jumbo shrimp
2. 1/4 cup grapeseed oil, divided
3. 1 teaspoon fine sea salt, divided
4. 8 medium garlic cloves, sliced
5. 1 tablespoon crushed red pepper
6. 1 small lemon
7. 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro leaves and stems
Instructions:
1. Using small scissors, cut lengthwise down the back of each shrimp shell; remove and discard vein. (Do not remove shells.) Rinse shrimp with water, and pat dry. Stir together shrimp, 2 tablespoons oil, and 3/4 teaspoon salt in a bowl.
2. Preheat grill to medium-high (400°F to 450°F). Place a large cast-iron skillet on grill grates. Cover grill; let skillet preheat 5 minutes. Uncover grill; add remaining 2 tablespoons oil to skillet. Add shrimp; cook, uncovered, turning occasionally, until shrimp begin to turn pink, 2 to 3 minutes. Add garlic and crushed red pepper; cook, stirring occasionally, until garlic is golden, shrimp are cooked through, and shrimp shells are beginning to crisp, 3 to 4 minutes.
3. Remove skillet from grill; immediately transfer shrimp to a serving platter. Cut lemon in half; squeeze 1 lemon half over shrimp (discarding seeds). Sprinkle shrimp with cilantro and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt. Cut remaining lemon half into wedges, and serve with shrimp.
Time: Total: 35 mins
Notes: Suggested Pairing: Bright, coastal California Chardonnay.
Author: Aria Alpert Adjani

Title: Tomato-and-Pepper-Vodka Punch
Ingredients:
1. 4 celery stalks with the leaves
2. 5 pounds ripe tomatoes, cored and roughly chopped
3. 1/4 teaspoon minced garlic
4. 1 teaspoon salt or to taste
5. 4 tablespoons sugar
6. 2 tablespoons white-wine Worcestershire sauce
7. 1 750-ml. bottle pepper vodka
8. Garnish: 1 pint tiny cherry tomatoes (if tomatoes are large, cut them in half)
Instructions:
1. Remove the leaves from the celery. Chop the leaves coarsely, setting them aside, and cut the stalks into 1-inch pieces.
